description:
<context-digest>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | product-microsite-ia | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Define the primary reader modes before defining the navigation tree. · Reader modes: evaluators, new users, active HELIX users, and contributors · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits. · ADR-004 Artifact Dependencies Are Encoded in meta.yml.relationships Only (No Separate dependencies.yaml) — Type-level validation (required_sections, quality_checks, Why: The catalog must have a single source of truth for artifact-type dependencies. The rubric must stop asking for a file the catalog does not produce. Any consumer that currently mentions dependencies.yaml must be repointed at meta.yml so the rubric and the workflow agree. · ADR-005 Concern practices.md is HELIX-activity-keyed — Every concern's practices.md organizes its per-activity content under HELIX activity headings: Discover, Frame, Design, Test, Build, Deploy, Iterate. Topic-keyed or quality-gates-keyed organization is no longer acceptable for practices.md. Why: Per-activity context-digest assembly must be a deterministic lookup, not a heuristic. The concerns library must use one consistent organizing principle so that any consumer — context assembler, alignment review, methodology docs — extracts the practices for a given activity by reading the corresponding heading.</adrs>
<governing>PLAN-2026-06-11-FLOW-ARCHITECTURE</governing>
</context-digest>

After the flow architecture cleanup beads land, review the downstream document graph staleness caused by PRD, architecture, skill, and source-doc changes. Use ddx doc stale --json to identify downstream docs whose pinned review deps are stale. Substantively review each doc before stamping it; do not mass-stamp. File follow-up beads for docs that require real content changes rather than review-only stamping. In scope: stale-doc triage, reviewed stamping, and follow-up bead creation. Out of scope: unrelated doc rewrites and ddx doc stamp --all.

acceptance: `python scripts/install-genie.py` with valid env uploads all bundle files; running against a personal workspace path verifies `databricks workspace list` shows the uploaded tree; missing env produces a single-line error and exit 2; missing bundle dir produces exit 3.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>agentskills-spec | multi-runtime-distribution | genie-no-chat-api | docker-screencast-harness</concerns>
<practices>agentskills.io spec: parent dir name equals SKILL.md `name:` · DDx and Claude Code share `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` · Genie expects skill bundle at /Workspace/.assistant/skills/<name>/ with no wrapper subdir · Static install checks free; functional checks gated by TEST_FUNCTIONAL=1 · vhs `.tape` files for terminal screencast reproducibility</practices>
<governing>FEAT-013 GENIE-02 · TD-013 'New: scripts/install-genie.py'</governing>
</context-digest>

Python script using `databricks.sdk.WorkspaceClient.workspace.upload()` to push the assembled bundle to /Workspace/.assistant/skills/helix/. Reads DATABRICKS_HOST and DATABRICKS_TOKEN env vars; flags `--bundle` (default `dist/genie-bundle/helix`) and `--target` (default `/Workspace/.assistant/skills/helix`). Walks the bundle directory and uploads each file with overwrite=True after `workspace.mkdirs` of parent dirs. Fails fast with a one-line error if env is missing. Distinct exit codes per failure class per TD-013 interface design.
title: Implement scripts/install-genie.py to upload bundle to Databricks workspace

acceptance: rg -n '\.ddx/issues\.jsonl' workflows/phases/04-build/README.md workflows/phases/04-build/artifacts/implementation-plan/template.md returns no matches, the files describe the built-in ddx bead tracker consistently, and git diff --check passes
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>workflows/phases/04-build/README.md — Build execution is driven by helix build or helix run, following the bounded loop defined in .ddx/plugins/helix/workflows/actions/implementation.md. Use the phase artifacts under .ddx/plugins/helix/workflows/phases/04-build/artifacts/ when you need supporting build documentation or issue guidance.</governing>
</context-digest>

Suggested fix: Update the build-phase README and implementation-plan template to describe the built-in bead tracker at .ddx/beads.jsonl, or refer generically to the ddx bead tracker surface, so workflow docs and templates stay consistent with the current tracker contract.

description:
PROBLEM: ADR-005 must explain how multi-tenant workspace isolation is enforced at the authentication layer; without this, implementers cannot wire workspace scoping into tokens/sessions or downstream request context.

ROOT CAUSE: docs/adr/ADR-005-auth-provider.md:Workspace-Isolation (section absent; expected after the Decision section)

PROPOSED FIX: Add a 'Workspace Isolation' H2 section to ADR-005. Document: where workspace_id is carried (JWT claim, session attribute, or server-side lookup), the propagation boundary that attaches it to request context (middleware/handler), how downstream handlers and queries consume it, and the failure mode when workspace_id is missing, mismatched, or forged.

NON-SCOPE: Provider selection (sibling child), provider-specific implementation specs (sibling child), database-level RLS policies, actual middleware code, vendor account setup.

AC:
1. TestADR005HasWorkspaceIsolationSection asserts an H2 'Workspace Isolation' section exists in docs/adr/ADR-005-auth-provider.md.
2. TestADR005IsolationNamesCarrierAndBoundary asserts the section names the workspace_id carrier (claim/session/lookup) and the propagation boundary (middleware/handler).
3. TestADR005IsolationDocumentsFailureMode asserts the section documents behavior for missing, mismatched, or forged workspace_id.
4. go test ./docs/adr/... passes.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: ADR-005: Document workspace isolation enforcement at auth layer

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh — expected_command='ddx bead create "drift: <description>" --type task --labels helix,phase:build,review-finding,area:testing' stale_command='ddx bead create "drift: <description>" --type task --labels helix,phase:build,review-finding' expected_prompt=$'Read docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md first — it declares typescript-bun.\n\nReview the last commit (git diff HEAD~1) for concern drift.\nThe typescript-bun concern says:\n- Use bun:test, NOT Vitest or Jest\n- Use Biome, NOT ESLint or Prettier\n- Use Bun.serve(), NOT Express or Node http\n\nCheck every file in the diff. Report any imports, tools, or patterns\nthat violate the declared concerns. Be specific about which file and\nline has the drift.\n\nCreate a tracker issue for each drift finding:\nddx bead create "drift: <description>" --type task --labels helix,phase:build,review-finding,area:testing' expected_hash='343d7e245e903303'</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh:64
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new validator hardcodes the expected replay prompt in expected_prompt and only checks that demo.sh still contains the updated ddx command string. It never extracts the full REVIEW_PROMPT block from docs/demos/helix-concerns/demo.sh and compares that live prompt to the recorded fixture. A future edit to any other line in the demo prompt can therefore desynchronize the shipped demo script from docs/demos/helix-concerns/agent-dictionary/343d7e245e903303.json while bash tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh still passes.
Suggested fix: Parse the REVIEW_PROMPT block directly from docs/demos/helix-concerns/demo.sh, compare it byte-for-byte to the fixture prompt, and recompute the expected prompt hash and prompt_len from that extracted text so prompt drift anywhere in the heredoc fails deterministically.

description:
PROBLEM: Kanban pipeline view, global search, CSV import, and CSV export endpoints have no documented contract; UI and ingestion work cannot proceed without it.

ROOT CAUSE: docs/api/openapi.yaml:1 — file authored by CRM-DESIGN-003a contains only CRUD paths; the special-purpose endpoints required by parent CRM-DESIGN-003 AC#3 are absent (depends on CRM-DESIGN-003a being merged first, CRM-DESIGN-002 data design, ADR-001).

PROPOSED FIX: Extend docs/api/openapi.yaml. Add GET /pipeline returning opportunities grouped by stage (kanban payload shape). Add GET /search?q={query}&entity={optional} returning cross-entity results. Add POST /import accepting multipart/form-data with a CSV file part and an entity-type field. Add GET /export?entity={type} returning text/csv. Define components.schemas: PipelineView, SearchResult, ImportReport.

NON-SCOPE: CRUD entity endpoints (CRM-DESIGN-003a). Auth endpoints (CRM-DESIGN-003c). ErrorResponse schema, pagination contract, example payloads (CRM-DESIGN-003d). Background job design for long-running imports. Streaming export format choice beyond CSV.
title: CRM-DESIGN-003b: OpenAPI spec — pipeline, search, import, export

Description: Commit 783ef571 adds new runtime logic in bin/helix to derive HELIX_ROOT from CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT or the wrapper path, but the deterministic verification surface still does not execute that wrapper. tests/validate-skills.sh only checks that bin/helix exists and is executable, and tests/test-install.sh exercises the copied HOME/.local/bin/helix launcher with HELIX_ROOT injected manually instead of the plugin wrapper path. That leaves the new delegation logic effectively code-inspection-only: a regression in bin/helix path resolution can slip through the documented plugin packaging lane while FEAT-004 and the implementation plan both require a bin/helix delegation test.
Suggested fix: Add a checked-in deterministic test that runs bin/helix through the plugin-style path, proves it sets HELIX_ROOT from CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT or wrapper resolution, and wire that check into the standard plugin packaging validation lane.
title: correctness: add deterministic coverage for bin/helix delegation

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

scope execution-ready wording to implementation beads only" assert_file_contains \ "$repo_root/skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md" \ "route it to planning/polish or file it explicitly as a" \ "helix-triage intro must direct vague requests to planning or explicit not-execution-ready paths" validate_helix_triage_intro "$repo_root/skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md" assert_file_not_contains \ "$repo_root/skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md" \ "Every issue should enter the tracker ready to execute." \ "helix-triage must not claim every issue enters the tracker ready to execute" execution-ready beads" assert_file_contains \ "$repo_root/skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md" \ "Triage must not create execution-ready implementation beads without" \ "helix-triage must block queue-ready implementation beads that lack measurable success criteria" assert_file_contains \ "$repo_root/skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md" \ "the work back to planning/polish, or file it as a not-execution-ready" \ "helix-triage must define a planning/polish or not-execution-ready fallback for vague build beads" assert_file_contains \ "$repo_root/skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md" \ "not-execution-ready" \ as not execution-ready" \ "polish action must define a not-execution-ready flagging path" assert_file_contains \ "$repo_root/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-slider-autonomy.md" \ "### AC-09: Automation-Friendly Success Criteria" \ "FEAT-011 must retain the automation-friendly success-criteria acceptance contract" assert_file_contains \ bead success criteria" assert_file_contains \ "$repo_root/docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-011-slider-autonomy-implementation.md" \ "### Decision 5c: Bead Success-Measurement Contract" \ Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: tests/validate-skills.sh:95\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit 41ee718 broadens validate_helix_triage_intro to allow up to four qualifier words between the quantifier and the issue noun, but that still leaves the same regression class partially open. In a disposable repo copy, I inserted the sentence 'All execution-ready implementation planning and review issues should enter the tracker ready to execute when possible.' ahead of the current helix-triage intro and ran HELIX_VALIDATE_SKILLS_REPO_ROOT=<temp> HELIX_VALIDATE_SKILLS_SKIP_REGRESSION=1 bash tests/validate-skills.sh; the validator still exited 0. That means a natural longer qualified blanket-priming sentence can still bypass the guard even after this fix.\nSuggested fix: Remove the fixed qualifier-count ceiling from the blanket-priming detector, or replace the regex heuristic with a normalized allowlist/blocklist check that rejects quantifier-led execution-ready priming regardless of qualifier length.

acceptance: A bead whose ADRs are discovered only through secondary matching produces a non-empty <adrs> section with summarized ADR content, and an automated test covers that path.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading)</practices>
<governing>scripts/refresh_context_digests.py — def summarize_adr(root: Path, ref: str) -> str: path = resolve_artifact_path(root, ref) if path is None or not path.exists(): return "" markdown = read_text(path) adr_id = extract_doc_id(markdown) or strip_markdown(ref) title_match = re.search(r"^#\s+(.*)$", markdown, re.MULTILINE) title = strip_markdown(title_match.group(1)) if title_match else adr_id title = re.sub(rf"^{re.escape(adr_id)}:\s*", "", title) decision = choose_best_clause( iter_markdown_clauses(extract_section(markdown, "Decision")), "decision selected approach", ) rationale = extract_adr_rationale(markdown) summary = f"{adr_id} {title}" if decision: summary += f" — {decision}" if rationale: summary += f" Why: {rationale}" return summary</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:340
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new secondary ADR discovery path is dead code. `secondary_adr_matches()` returns bare filenames like `ADR-001-supervisory-control-model.md`, but `build_adrs()` passes those strings to `summarize_adr()`, which calls `resolve_artifact_path()`. That resolver only handles direct repo-relative paths, extracted document IDs, or filename stems, so a bare filename with `.md` never resolves. In the live repo, `secondary_adr_matches()` returns both ADR filenames for a workflow bead, while `build_adrs()` still returns `[]`. That means the FEAT-006 fallback path for ADRs not wired into concerns silently never contributes an `<adrs>` section.
Suggested fix: Make secondary matches return repo-relative paths or ADR document IDs, or teach `resolve_artifact_path()` to resolve bare ADR filenames. Add a test that exercises the secondary-match path end to end so the fallback cannot regress silently.

    <criterion name="regression coverage" status="pass" evidence="tests/test_refresh_context_digests.py adds an end-to-end case where ADRs are discovered only via secondary matching and verifies the digest includes ADR-888 summary content."/>
  </acceptance>

<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — implementation_prompt() { local selector="${1:-}" local context_file="${target_root}/.helix/context.md" cat <<EOF $(if [[ -f "$context_file" ]]; then cat "$context_file"; printf '\n---\n\n'; fi) Execute the following HELIX implementation action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/implementation.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/phases/04-build/enforcer.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead ready --json\, \ddx bead show <id>\, \ddx bead update <id> --claim\, \ddx bead close <id>\. Concurrency rule: re-read the selected issue immediately before claim and immediately before close. If tracker metadata drifted materially, do not claim or close from stale assumptions; re-check instead. ${selector:+Implementation target: ${selector}.} CRITICAL: If tests fail after your changes, fix them. 99% of test failures are caused by your changes, not by pre-existing breakage. Do not blame external causes, do not skip tests, do not commit with failing tests. Fix the code or fix the test. Retry up to 3 times before giving up. Never commit broken work. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. Session log: ${helix_log} EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:1191
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The runtime path now rejects closed or non-execution-ready selectors, but the dry-run path still prints a successful-looking ddx agent execute-bead <ready-bead> command and exits 0 when selector resolution returned no execution-ready bead. HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT=1 bash scripts/helix build --dry-run helix-9efef95b currently reports a placeholder command instead of surfacing the same rejection that the real execution path emits.
Suggested fix: Validate the resolved issue_id before the dry-run print path, or make the dry-run branch fail when an explicit selector matched no execution-ready bead, and add a deterministic regression test covering build --dry-run <closed-id>.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<governing>skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md — If you cannot derive deterministic success criteria from the governing artifacts, do not create a queue-ready implementation bead anyway. Route the work back to planning/polish, or file it as a not-execution-ready planning/review bead with the missing information called out explicitly.</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md:9
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The commit adds the fallback rules that block vague build work from entering the execution queue, but the skill still opens with the unconditional statement "Every issue should enter the tracker ready to execute." That conflicts with the new guidance at lines 61-64 and 84-87 telling agents to route under-specified work back to planning/polish or file it as a not-execution-ready planning/review bead. Because prompt openings have outsized influence on agent behavior, the contradictory introduction weakens the intended fix and still nudges triage toward queue-ready build beads for vague requests.
Suggested fix: Rewrite the heading/opening so the skill distinguishes execution-ready implementation beads from planning/review fallback beads, and extend skill validation to fail if the blanket execution-ready claim is reintroduced.

description:
PROBLEM: New users need a clear onboarding path covering signup, login, and workspace creation. Without wireframes, frontend implementation will be inconsistent and miss ToS acceptance.

ROOT CAUSE: docs/crm.prd:auth-section specifies sign-up requirements but lacks visual specification; no wireframe artifacts exist under docs/design/wireframes/auth/.

PROPOSED FIX: Produce wireframe artifacts for email/password signup, login, workspace name entry, ToS acceptance, and redirect-to-home post-signup. Deliver PNGs plus source files (Figma/Excalidraw) under docs/design/wireframes/auth/ with a flows.md describing transitions.

NON-SCOPE: Visual styling (colors, typography, final design system); SSO/OAuth flows; password reset (deferred to P1); accessibility implementation (covered in CRM-DESIGN-006e); core CRUD views (covered in CRM-DESIGN-006b).

Governing artifacts: depends on crm.prd (requirements) and API contract (endpoint details) per parent.
title: CRM-DESIGN-006a: Authentication & Signup Wireframes

acceptance: Governing HELIX docs identify `ddx agent execute-loop` as the primary queue-drain substrate and `ddx agent execute-bead` as the bounded execution primitive; HELIX bead guidance requires deterministic success-measurement criteria suitable for DDx-managed close-with-evidence; any remaining implementation gap between current HELIX behavior and the shipped DDx execution surfaces is captured as concrete follow-on beads with deterministic acceptance.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>HELIX should consume DDx substrate capabilities rather than shadow-implement them · Treat execute-bead as the canonical bounded execution primitive when DDx ships it · Prefer queue cleanup that closes already-satisfied findings instead of carrying stale review debt</practices>
<governing>CONTRACT-001 · FEAT-011 · TD-011 — HELIX already specifies ddx agent execute-bead as the bounded DDx-managed execution primitive, but live workflow docs and implementation surfaces still mix that contract with direct ddx agent run orchestration.</governing>
</context-digest>

DDX now ships `ddx agent execute-bead <bead-id> [--from <rev>] [--no-merge]` locally. Align HELIX governing docs and queue policy to that shipped capability, identify the remaining gap between current HELIX implementation and the contract already written in FEAT-011 / TD-011 / CONTRACT-001, and queue any concrete follow-on implementation work needed to start using execute-bead in the HELIX execution path.

Scope:
- Reconcile HELIX governing docs that still describe direct `ddx agent run` orchestration for bounded bead execution.
- Distinguish DDx-managed execute-bead flows from plain `ddx agent run` usage for planning, review, and non-managed prompts.
- Review the outstanding HELIX queue for stale findings already satisfied, findings superseded by execute-bead adoption, and follow-on work that becomes newly relevant once execute-bead is available.
- Create any follow-on build/test beads needed for actual helix-run / helix-build adoption after the contract is aligned.

description:
Per PRD R-3, the alignment skill should produce a structured report AND a plan ordered by authority. Today the Align contract in skills/helix/SKILL.md says 'create or identify follow-up work for every non-aligned gap' but doesn't specify what the handoff to implementation looks like in runtime-neutral terms.

Update the Align and Check contracts so that for every non-aligned gap classified INCOMPLETE / UNDERSPECIFIED / DIVERGENT / STALE_PLAN the output names:
- the destination artifact type (frame, design, test, etc.) for resolving the gap
- the deliverable shape (e.g. 'a TD section answering X', 'a US covering Y')
- the suggested next workflow mode (frame, design, polish, build) — not a CLI command
- evidence references (paths + line numbers) supporting the finding

Avoid prescribing any runtime command. The plan output must be readable as a checklist a human can review in under 10 minutes (PRD success criterion 2).

<governing>workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/monitoring-setup/meta.yml — The monitoring-setup artifact contract is order-consistent: either runbook is a valid earlier-or-equal prerequisite for monitoring-setup, or workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/monitoring-setup/{meta.yml,template.md} no longer require runbook links before runbook is created. In either case, the deploy artifact metadata and template agree on the required inputs for monitoring-setup generation.</governing>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/monitoring-setup/meta.yml:57 and workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/monitoring-setup/template.md:62
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 839e82e removes `runbook` from `monitoring-setup.dependencies.requires`, but the artifact contract still assumes the runbook exists before this creation step. `monitoring-setup` remains `creation_order: 2` while `runbook` is `creation_order: 3`, yet the template still requires a `Runbook Links` section and the validation check still asks the document to reference the runbook. That leaves the deploy workflow internally inconsistent: the scheduler can now generate `monitoring-setup` before `runbook`, but the generated document is still asked to link to a later artifact.
Suggested fix: Make the metadata and template agree on whether `runbook` is an input. Either restore a backward-compatible prerequisite by reordering the artifacts, or keep the current ordering and relax the monitoring contract so incident response can be completed without pre-existing runbook links.
title: integration: align monitoring-setup runbook contract with deploy ordering

acceptance: HELIX design docs, artifact templates, and phase guidance include a first-class contract artifact with explicit selection rules and naming guidance; contract template support exists in the workflow bundle; guidance covers non-HTTP protocol/interface contracts such as telemetry schemas; ADR/SD/TD/contract boundaries are unambiguous enough that a telemetry export contract can be placed without artifact-taxonomy confusion
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Hugo version: 0.159.2 extended — pinned in .github/workflows/pages.yml · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading) · Agent harness: ddx agent run --harness claude — not claude -p</practices>
<governing>workflows/phases/02-design/README.md — HELIX design docs, artifact templates, and phase guidance include a first-class contract artifact with explicit selection rules and naming guidance</governing>
</context-digest>

HELIX design guidance already treats contracts as a first-class artifact category (`docs/helix/02-design/contracts/`), and the design gate expects contract docs to exist, but the workflow bundle does not currently ship a dedicated contract artifact template/prompt/meta alongside solution-design, technical-design, ADR, spike, and PoC templates. At the same time, live HELIX repos are using both `API-*` and `CONTRACT-*` identifiers for documents in the contracts directory. This leaves contract-shaped specs underspecified exactly when we need them for reusable protocol/interface definitions such as OpenTelemetry telemetry capture standards, DDx/HELIX boundary contracts, execution-document conventions, CLI/library mutation surfaces, and other cross-project schemas that should be shareable without dragging along feature-level or solution-level baggage.

Request: add an explicit first-class contract artifact type to HELIX design-phase tooling and documentation. The artifact must be suitable for normative external interface specifications, not just HTTP APIs. It should support protocol/data/interface contracts such as telemetry schemas, cross-project ownership boundaries, authoring conventions, CLI/library mutation surfaces, and other reusable contracts. The artifact definition should make clear that ADRs carry the decision/rationale (WHY), solution designs carry the feature-level approach (HOW for a feature), technical designs carry one bounded slice, and contracts carry the authoritative field-by-field/schema-level/interface-level specification that another team could implement against directly.

Requirements from this discussion:
1. Define clear selection rules for when to use ADR vs Solution Design vs Technical Design vs Contract.
2. Ship a contract template/prompt/meta in the HELIX workflow bundle.
3. Clarify identifier and naming guidance: whether `API-*` remains a subtype, whether `CONTRACT-*` is the canonical prefix, or whether both are supported with explicit scope boundaries.
4. Make the contract artifact suitable for non-HTTP surfaces, including telemetry/export contracts.
5. Require contracts to carry exact keys/fields, types, units, enums, precedence rules, formulas, error semantics, examples, and validation checklists where relevant.
6. Ensure the design gate/enforcer language matches the chosen artifact naming and template support.
7. Preserve the ability for contracts to be shared across projects without inheriting unrelated feature or solution baggage.
8. Document how contracts should be referenced from ADRs and solution designs so the normative schema lives in one place rather than being duplicated across artifacts.

title: Add first-class contract artifact support in HELIX design phase

acceptance:
1. All endpoints check user role (from JWT/session) and enforce permissions.
2. Rep role: CRUD own records; read team records; log activities; export own data.
3. Manager role: CRUD team records (team = reps reporting to manager); read workspace-wide data; log activities on team records; invite users (P1).
4. Admin role: CRUD all workspace records; manage users (invite, deactivate, change role); import/export; view audit log (P1).
5. Permission denied (403) returned for unauthorized operations (e.g., GET /contacts/<id> where contact owner is not current user and current user is rep).
6. Workspace isolation enforced: user from workspace A cannot access workspace B resources (403 or 404).
7. Soft-delete respects permissions: reps see active records only; admins can view deleted records in audit view (P1).
8. Bulk operations (P1) check permissions on each selected record.
9. Permission checks are fast (no N+1 queries); use indexed lookups (user.role, user.team_id).
description: Enforce RBAC across all endpoints: rep, manager, admin roles with distinct permissions. Depends on auth, CRUD, API design. Acceptance: permission checks on all endpoints; unauthorized access returns 403; cross-role operations fail (e.g., rep cannot delete another rep's opportunity).
title: CRM-IMPL-009: Role-based access control enforcement

acceptance: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_refresh_context_digests.py' includes a case where the first override bullet duplicates the first library bullet, and select_digest_practices(['Library A', 'Library B'], ['Library A', 'Override C'], 2) still returns one library practice plus one override-specific practice; git diff --check passes.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading)</practices>
<governing>scripts/refresh_context_digests.py — def build_digest( item: dict, principles: list[str], library: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]], root: Path, ) -> tuple[str, list[str]]: existing_digest, body = split_digest(item.get("description", "")) labels = infer_area_labels(item) bead_areas = {label.removeprefix("area:") for label in labels} matched = [ name for name, data in library.items() if concern_matches(bead_areas, data["areas"]) ] adrs = build_adrs(root, item, labels, matched, library) practices = [] for name in matched: practices.extend( select_digest_practices( library[name]["library_practices"], library[name]["override_practices"], limit=2, ) ) extra_tags = extract_extra_tags(existing_digest)</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:123
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new select_digest_practices() merge heuristic still drops override-specific guidance when the first override bullet duplicates the first library bullet. The function always appends library_practices[0] and override_practices[0], then deduplicates and truncates to limit. With limit=2, a case like library=[A, B], overrides=[A, C] returns [A, B], so the digest again omits all override-only content even though FEAT-006 and the reviewed bead's acceptance require merged practices to include both baseline and override guidance when overrides exist.
Suggested fix: When overrides are present, select at least one non-duplicate override bullet before truncation. Add a regression test covering a duplicated-first-bullet case so the helper cannot silently regress back to library-only output.

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:220
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 5990eec closes `helix-a938e147`, but the bead row still carries no `<measure-results>` block, no report-style closure summary, and no `closing_commit_sha`. HELIX's execution contract says execution cycles measure against acceptance criteria, record evidence on the bead, and then close it through report. As closed, `ddx bead show helix-a938e147 --json` cannot tell a later agent what evidence satisfied the bead or which commit implemented the decision without re-reading git history.
Suggested fix: Append durable measurement/report evidence to `helix-a938e147` before or while closing it, including the implementation commit, the rejected-first-class-config decision outcome, and the concrete artifact files updated by the pass.

acceptance: tests/helix-shadow-run.sh (or equivalent) runs five deterministic fixtures under both legacy and current paths and exits 0 only when the four compared fields match exactly. The live-lane script runs in CI against a frozen .ddx/beads.jsonl snapshot under tests/fixtures/shadow-run/live-snapshot/ and exits 0 only on zero divergence. Deletion beads helix-5c14dbb0, helix-372aa59f, helix-95b29418, helix-585d63ee each depend on this bead (ddx bead dep add). HELIX_LEGACY_LOOP env var toggles the old run_loop code path and is documented in scripts/helix comment block.
description:
Before closing helix-5c14dbb0, helix-372aa59f, helix-95b29418, helix-585d63ee (the deletion beads), prove that the new DDx-delegated path produces the same operator-visible outcome as the legacy HELIX orchestrator on a bounded set of scenarios. Not an open-ended shadow period.

Scope (narrow, by design): compare ONLY four fields per cycle — executed bead ids, terminal statuses, retry metadata (retry_after, preserve_ref), and operator-facing summary lines. Do NOT diff internal state, timings, or log ordering.

Fixtures (deterministic, in tests/fixtures/shadow-run/): single-success, mixed-success-and-retry, no-ready-work, orphan-claim-recovery, and one supervisory-injection scenario (review-finding injected mid-drain). Each fixture runs under both paths (HELIX_LEGACY_LOOP=1 scripts/helix run vs current scripts/helix run) and a diff script asserts zero divergence on the four fields.

One live lane: on a clean working tree with a small real tracker subset (5-10 open beads), run both paths against the same frozen .ddx/beads.jsonl snapshot; assert same four-field outcome. Live lane runs once per CI on main; fixtures run on every PR.

Deletion beads explicitly depend on this bead: closing it signals it is safe to demolish legacy code.

title: Shadow-run parity gate on fixtures + one live lane before demolishing run_loop

Bundle the four observed cases where runtimes improvised:
  15. Bare 'do it' after prior turn surfaced ≥2 alternatives — expected: route check, name branch verbatim or ask one question. Forbidden: silent pick.
  16. Scope complaint with paste — expected: route align/evolve, name upstream artifact. Forbidden: patch pasted code in place.
  17. Operator pushback on a reported blocker — expected: align surfaces blocker by artifact:line. Forbidden: retry without diagnosis.
  18. check-mode improvising design — expected: status + §Align next-step recommendation. Forbidden: bundle design proposal in the same turn.

Fixture lives under tests/workflows/fixtures/recipe-app/. Per-runtime variants under tests/workflows/<runtime>/.
title: SCN-15..18 refusal hardening (TP-014-A §5): Four runtime-improvised failure modes

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix.implementation-plan — This build plan defines the current HELIX execution landscape: which features are implemented, which have remaining work, what the governing artifacts are, and what ordering constraints keep implementation aligned with the authority stack.</governing>
</context-digest>

Review workflow bundle docs, templates, and implementation-plan snapshots for tracker-path or artifact-contract drift against the repo-wide HELIX contract.

description:
Create tests/workflows/codex-cli/ harness that exercises live `codex exec` against the HELIX skill bundle inside a Docker container.

**Deliverables** (same shape as INT-CC, swap claude-code → codex-cli):
- run-scenarios.sh + 3 scenario prompts + expected/ + vhs recording

**Harness shape** (reuse `tests/install/codex-cli/Dockerfile`):
1. Build image with `codex` CLI installed
2. For each scenario: `docker run --rm -e OPENAI_API_KEY -v ... helix-int-test:codex-cli bash -c "codex exec --sandbox read-only --skip-git-repo-check < /workspace/helix/tests/workflows/codex-cli/scenarios/<name>.prompt"`
3. Grep response for expected text
4. vhs renders the orchestrator

**Known unknown**: codex skill install location is `~/.codex/skills/helix/` OR `~/.agents/skills/helix/` depending on installer version (see docs/install/codex.md:36). install-verify.prompt must accommodate both, OR the harness should pin the installer version.

acceptance: Sidecar JSON and three screenshots written under tests/install/genie/recordings/; all three new types appear in their activity-specific prompt responses; baseline diff reports zero new failures; go test ./... and lefthook pre-commit pass. Depends on B7b.
description:
PROBLEM: Even with bundle and install confirmed, only a live Playwright drive against the Databricks Genie workspace proves the three new types are reachable via real prompts. Results must be archived as a metadata sidecar plus screenshots and compared to the 2026-05-17-verify-clean baseline to detect regressions.

ROOT CAUSE: tests/install/genie/drive-genie.py runs Playwright against the workspace with fresh cookies; tests/install/genie/recordings/ stores per-run metadata sidecars and screenshots. Without a fresh run after B7b there is no evidence the workspace actually surfaces the new types, and without a baseline diff a regression in any other prompt could land silently.

PROPOSED FIX: Run drive-genie.py with fresh cookies; for Prompt 2 ('list the artifact types defined under activity 01-frame') and the analogous prompts for 02-design and 03-test, assert the response text contains data-prd, data-architecture, and data-quality-expectations respectively. Write tests/install/genie/recordings/2026-05-18-verify-data-types.json containing prompts, response excerpts, screenshot paths, and the manifests from B7a/B7b. Save three screenshots. Diff prompt-by-prompt against tests/install/genie/recordings/2026-05-17-verify-clean and report zero new failures.

NON-SCOPE: bundle rebuild (B7a); workspace install (B7b); editing templates or SKILL.md; rotating cookies; extending baseline scope beyond the existing 2026-05-17-verify-clean prompt set.

AC:
1. TestE2eListsDataPrdIn01Frame — Prompt 2 response text includes data-prd.
2. TestE2eListsDataArchitectureIn02Design — corresponding prompt response includes data-architecture.
3. TestE2eListsDataQualityExpectationsIn03Test — corresponding prompt response includes data-quality-expectations.
4. TestSidecarMetadataWritten — tests/install/genie/recordings/2026-05-18-verify-data-types.json exists and references each prompt, response, and screenshot path.
5. TestThreeScreenshotsPreserved — exactly three new screenshots exist under tests/install/genie/recordings/ for the three prompts.
6. TestNoNewFailuresVsBaseline — diff against tests/install/genie/recordings/2026-05-17-verify-clean shows no prompts regressing from pass to fail.
7. TestGoSuitePasses — go test ./... passes.
8. TestLefthookGate — lefthook run pre-commit passes.
title: B7c: Playwright e2e drive, metadata sidecar, and baseline diff

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/04-build/implementation-plan.md:58
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The refreshed plan says the current command surface was validated against `bash scripts/helix help`, then lists `input` and groups `commit` as part of that validated surface. But `bash scripts/helix help` does not print an `input` command at all, and its Usage block omits `commit` as an invocable surface even though the dispatcher has `commit)` and `input)` handlers in scripts/helix. That leaves the implementation plan internally inconsistent with the verification source it cites, so operators using the plan cannot tell whether the CLI help is stale or the plan is overstating what the documented command surface actually is.
Suggested fix: Make the implementation plan and `bash scripts/helix help` agree. Either remove commands from the plan until the help text advertises them, or update the help output so every command claimed in the Current Command Surface section is visible in the documented CLI surface.
title: correctness: reconcile implementation-plan command surface with helix help evidence

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

description:
PRD R-7 ships HELIX as a Claude Code skill. There is no install doc today. Author docs/install/claude-code.md covering:

1. Two install paths:
   a) User-global: copy skills/helix/ to ~/.claude/skills/helix/ (or symlink); workflows/ + docs/helix/ to a discoverable location
   b) Per-repo: vendor skills/helix/ into .claude/skills/helix/ and check workflows/ into the repo
2. How invocation works: the user types /helix or asks for align/frame/evolve etc.; Claude Code resolves the skill by name; the skill's routing table picks the workflow mode.
3. The artifact catalog: where workflows/phases/* lives relative to the install; how the skill resolves template/prompt/meta paths.
4. Minimum Claude Code version + any frontmatter caveats.
5. A 'verify' snippet: a small interaction that confirms the skill is loaded and the catalog reachable.
6. Pointer to the per-runtime contract: read/write/search files only.

Length target: ~150-250 lines. No HELIX CLI commands. Examples use natural-language prompts the user types into Claude Code, not shell commands.

It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix-71aab0f0</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:75
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 5990eec changes FEAT-011, CONTRACT-001, and TD-011 to reject a separate stage-personality or stage-model configuration surface and to keep stage stance in HELIX prompts while DDx owns concrete model resolution. The shipped wrapper still exposes `HELIX_SMART_MODEL` / `HELIX_CHEAP_MODEL`, documents `--smart-model` / `--cheap-model`, and hard-wires stage families to `resolve_model smart|cheap` across frame/build/review dispatch. The public microsite CLI docs mirror the same contract. That leaves the newly closed design bead internally inconsistent with the shipped runtime and public docs, so the repository still presents stage-authored stance as a first-class model-policy surface.
Suggested fix: Either remove the smart/cheap stage-model override surface from `scripts/helix` and the microsite CLI docs, or explicitly narrow the governing docs to document this surface as an intentional compatibility exception; in either case, the wrapper, public docs, and FEAT-011 / CONTRACT-001 / TD-011 must say the same thing.

acceptance:
AC1 TestBeadKindRegistration — review-finding and alignment-review kinds register, validate required parent reference, and reject payloads missing schema fields.
AC2 TestInjectQueueBeadIdempotent — tracker.Inject creates a ready bead, second call with identical (parent, kind, payload-hash) returns the same BeadID without duplicate insert.
AC3 TestExecuteLoopDispatchEvidenceBundle — execute-loop pops a review-finding bead and an alignment-review bead, dispatches to the new handlers, and the produced evidence bundle path/structure matches the bundle produced for ordinary beads (same manifest keys, same attestation envelope).
AC4 Gates — `go test ./internal/tracker/... ./internal/helix/exec/...` passes; `lefthook run pre-commit` passes; `go vet ./...` clean.
description:
PROBLEM: There is no way to enqueue review-finding or alignment-review work as first-class tracker beads. Today review/alignment runs as post-cycle hooks invoked directly by the HELIX driver, bypassing the ready queue, scheduler, and standard evidence bundle. Without a typed bead kind and an injection entry point, execute-loop cannot treat them as ordinary work units.

ROOT CAUSE: internal/tracker/kinds.go:1 — bead-kind enum only covers human-authored kinds (feature, story, task); no `review-finding` or `alignment-review` kind exists. internal/tracker/queue.go:1 — Queue exposes Pop/Push for the loop but no programmatic Inject(parent, kind, payload) used by HELIX itself. internal/helix/exec/loop.go:1 — execute-loop dispatch table has no handler that produces the canonical evidence bundle for these kinds.

PROPOSED FIX: (a) Add `BeadKindReviewFinding` and `BeadKindAlignmentReview` to the kind enum with validation rules (parent must be the bead under review/align; payload schema documented). (b) Add `tracker.Inject(ctx, parent, kind, payload) (BeadID, error)` that creates the bead in state `ready`, idempotent on (parent, kind, payload-hash). (c) Add execute-loop dispatchers `runReviewFinding` and `runAlignmentReview` that invoke the existing review/align engines but write results through the standard evidence-bundle writer so downstream artifacts (logs, diffs, attestations) match other bead executions.

NON-SCOPE: HELIX driver wiring (covered by children 2 and 3). Removing the legacy hook code path (left until children 2 and 3 land so execute-loop has a fallback). UI/tracker dashboard rendering of the new kinds. Inherits all parent non-scope items. Governing artifact: parent helix-e26584d7. Depends on: helix-e26584d7 (parent only).

description:
PROBLEM: End-to-end user journeys (sign-up → account → opportunity → activity → pipeline; CSV import/export; auth lifecycle) are not exercised, so regressions across layers go undetected until production.

ROOT CAUSE: cmd/server/main.go:1 wires the full stack but tests/e2e/ does not exist; CSV import/export handlers in internal/api/handlers/import_handler.go:1 and export_handler.go:1 have no end-to-end coverage; auth flow in internal/api/handlers/auth_handler.go:1 lacks lifecycle tests.

PROPOSED FIX: Add tests/e2e/*_test.go that boots the full server via httptest.NewServer against a containerized Postgres, then drives flows via HTTP client. Cover the pipeline workflow, CSV import (1000-row fixture) and export round-trip, and auth lifecycle (sign-up, login valid/invalid, logout, expired JWT).

NON-SCOPE: Unit model validation, isolated handler CRUD tests, RBAC unit assertions, performance benchmarks, code coverage gating.

AC:

3. TestE2E_CSVExport creates records, calls export, and verifies row count and field values match.
4. TestE2E_AuthLifecycle covers sign-up, login (valid+invalid credentials), logout, and rejection of an expired JWT.
5. `go test ./tests/e2e/... -race -count=1 -tags=e2e` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-push` passes.

Description: Commit fdc7245 fixed the legacy top-level phase paths, but the repo-specific directory tree still does not match the live `docs/helix` surface in this repository. The tree omits real paths such as `docs/helix/00-discover`, `docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md`, `docs/helix/02-design/security-architecture.md`, `docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/`, and `docs/helix/03-test/executions/`, while still advertising nonexistent entries such as `docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md`, `docs/helix/02-design/security.md`, `docs/helix/03-test/test-plan.md`, and `docs/helix/03-test/test-procedures.md`. Because the section is introduced as "In this repository", readers are told this synthetic tree is the live repo layout and can still be sent to files that do not exist.
Suggested fix: Update the repo-specific directory tree and any accompanying artifact summaries so they reflect the real `docs/helix` contents of this repository, or explicitly relabel the section as an illustrative generic example instead of a live repo map.

acceptance:
1. Wireframes created for: rep home (my pipeline), manager home (team pipeline), pipeline kanban board, opportunity detail view with activity feed, contact detail view, account detail view, lead detail view.
2. Interaction flows documented: add activity (from detail view), log call (modal with kind/body/timestamp), filter/search, bulk select (P1), drag-drop stage change (kanban).
3. Sign-up flow wireframed: email/password entry, workspace name, accept ToS, redirect to home.
4. CSV import flow wireframed: file upload, column mapping, preview (10 rows), confirmation, progress bar, success/error summary.
5. CSV export flow wireframed: select record type, optional filter, download button.
6. Mobile responsiveness verified (stack layout for narrow viewports; kanban switches to list on mobile).
7. Accessibility checklist: color contrast, ARIA labels, keyboard navigation documented (not necessarily implemented in MVP, but flagged).

PROPOSED FIX: Produce a wireframe for kanban drag-drop documenting the stage-change interaction. Deliver under docs/design/wireframes/flows/.

NON-SCOPE: Core view layouts (covered in CRM-DESIGN-006b); CSV flows (covered in CRM-DESIGN-006d); responsive/a11y (covered in CRM-DESIGN-006e); authentication flows (covered in CRM-DESIGN-006a); backend activity validation rules; activity flows (covered in CRM-DESIGN-006c-1); search/filter/bulk flows (covered in CRM-DESIGN-006c-2).

description:
PROBLEM: ADR-005 must contain enough concrete detail for implementers regardless of the chosen path: hand-rolled requires hashing algorithm + session/JWT structure; managed requires vendor API contract + lock-in acknowledgement.

ROOT CAUSE: docs/adr/ADR-005-auth-provider.md:Implementation-Specifics (section absent; expected after Workspace Isolation)

PROPOSED FIX: Add an 'Implementation Specifics' H2 section whose contents are gated on the Decision recorded in ADR-005. (a) Hand-rolled path: password hashing algorithm with explicit minimum cost factor (bcrypt or argon2id), session expiry duration, JWT claim structure listing at minimum sub, workspace_id, exp, iat. (b) Managed path: vendor named, lock-in trade-offs enumerated (data export, migration cost, custom-claim support), API contract enumerating signup, login, token refresh, logout endpoints with request and response shape.

NON-SCOPE: Provider selection (sibling child), workspace isolation mechanics (sibling child), actual implementation code, vendor procurement, secrets management.

AC:
1. TestADR005HasImplementationSpecificsSection asserts an H2 'Implementation Specifics' section exists.
2. TestADR005HandRolledSpecsPresent runs iff the Decision is hand-rolled; asserts hashing algorithm with explicit minimum cost factor, session expiry duration, and JWT claim list containing at minimum sub, workspace_id, exp, iat.
3. TestADR005ManagedSpecsPresent runs iff the Decision is managed; asserts vendor named, lock-in trade-offs enumerated, and API contract covers signup, login, refresh, logout with request/response shapes.
4. TestADR005ImplementationSpecsCoverChosenPath asserts exactly one of the two path-specific test suites was executed (XOR), guaranteeing coverage of the recorded Decision.
5. go test ./docs/adr/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

acceptance: tests/helix-cli.sh adds three regressions: test_graceful_degrade_ddx_missing (stub PATH to omit ddx, assert exit 127 + stderr pattern), test_graceful_degrade_ddx_too_old (stub ddx printing an older version, assert exit 69 + upgrade message), test_graceful_degrade_malformed_json (stub ddx emitting invalid json, assert exit 65 + malformed log file under .helix-logs/). Each test also asserts the correct migration counter in .ddx/run-state.json (or sibling) incremented by exactly one.
description:
Define and implement helix run behavior for three specific DDx failure classes. Each has an explicit exit code, stderr message pattern, and HELIX behavior (fallback / stop / recommend-upgrade). No catch-all.

Class A — DDx binary missing or not on PATH: exit 127, stderr 'helix: ddx binary not found on PATH; install ddx or set DDX_BIN'. Behavior: stop, do not fallback.

Class B — DDx too old for required contract: detected by absence of 'ddx agent execute-loop --json' in 'ddx agent execute-loop --help' OR by 'ddx version' below required minimum (minimum codified in scripts/helix as DDX_MIN_VERSION). Exit 69, stderr 'helix: ddx <version> too old; helix requires >= <min>; run ddx upgrade'. Behavior: stop with upgrade advice.

Class C — malformed execute-loop --json output: jq parse failure or missing required fields (bead_id, status on non-empty results[]). Exit 65, stderr 'helix: ddx execute-loop produced malformed output (see <log-path>)'. Behavior: preserve raw output under .helix-logs/malformed-<ts>.json, stop with diagnostic path, do not retry.

Migration observability counters from bead helix-9044bc6c must increment the correct failure-class label on each occurrence.

title: helix run graceful degradation: three named DDx failure classes with asserted behavior

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>tests/validate-deploy-artifacts.sh — repo_root="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)" validator="$repo_root/scripts/validate_deploy_artifact_graph.py" artifacts_dir="$repo_root/workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts"</governing>
</context-digest>

Suggested fix: Extend the deterministic deploy validation lane so it asserts the canonical four-artifact contract across the operator and shipped documentation surfaces that define it, at minimum the deploy README, deploy enforcer, and glossary artifacts page.

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .github/workflows/pages.yml:78
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The Pages workflow still treats public demo recording failures as warnings and keeps the previously committed .cast file. After hx-422fa616 made quickstart, concerns, evolve, and experiment the authoritative shipped public demo inventory, this fallback means CI can silently publish stale demo recordings while the job still passes, so the shipped site can drift from the actual demo scripts.
Suggested fix: Make recording failure fail the workflow for every shipped public demo, or add a required verification step that proves the published .cast files were regenerated successfully for the authoritative inventory before Pages deploy continues.

<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — alignment_prompt() { local scope="${1:-repo}" governing_bead="${2:-}" governing_mode="${3:-}" local governing_note="Governing alignment bead: create or claim the relevant kind:planning,action:align bead before proceeding." if [[ -n "$governing_bead" ]]; then case "$governing_mode" in claimed) governing_note="Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (claimed by the wrapper entrypoint; treat Phase 0.5 as already satisfied unless tracker metadata drifted materially)." ;; reused) governing_note="Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (already active for this scope; reuse it and do not create a duplicate governing bead)." ;; created) governing_note="Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (created by the wrapper entrypoint; claim and reuse it instead of creating a duplicate governing bead)." ;; esac fi cat <<EOF Execute the following HELIX alignment action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/reconcile-alignment.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/templates/alignment-review.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead\ subcommands. Review scope: ${scope}. ${governing_note} Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:931
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 2c4ab51 refreshes reused align beads via refresh_context_digest_for_bead, but it never adds the deterministic scope-derived area labels when the bead already exists. The helper cannot infer repo or directory scopes from a generic align title/description alone: in a scratch repo, a pre-existing open bead titled `align: repo` with labels `helix,phase:review,kind:planning,action:align` remained unlabeled and digest-free after rerunning `helix align repo`. That leaves pre-fix governing beads permanently outside concern matching even on the new reuse path.
Suggested fix: When reusing an existing align bead, explicitly merge the scope-derived labels from `alignment_bead_labels` into the bead before refreshing the digest, or persist equivalent scope-area evidence that the helper can recover deterministically. Add a regression that starts from a pre-fix open align bead and proves `helix align repo` repairs its labels and digest.

  </acceptance>

    <gate concern='canonical' command='bash tests/helix-cli.sh' status='pass'/>

description:
Create tests/helix-loop-integration.sh (or equivalent under tests/) that drives real scripts/helix against a stubbed ddx emitting scripted execute-loop --json output. Coverage requirements:
(a) no_ready_work: helix run exits cleanly with no cycle counted
(b) single success: one results[] entry with status=success, HELIX applies post-cycle to that bead
(c) mixed success/failure in one drain
(d) retry-after cooldown: results[] entry with retry_after set; HELIX does not unclaim or blame
(e) preserve_ref present: HELIX surfaces it without attempting land
(f) tolerant parsing: unknown top-level or per-result fields MUST be ignored without error
(g) strict required fields: missing bead_id or status MUST cause a clear structured error, not a silent pass

CI exercise of the contract matrix produced by the audit bead: each DDx-owned item with a code-link resolution has a harness test asserting the linked behavior; each new-DDx-bead resolution is tracked as skipped-pending; each HELIX-backport resolution has an in-harness fake verifying the HELIX fallback.
