description:
<context-digest>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | product-microsite-ia | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Define the primary reader modes before defining the navigation tree. · Reader modes: evaluators, new users, active HELIX users, and contributors · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits. · ADR-004 Artifact Dependencies Are Encoded in meta.yml.relationships Only (No Separate dependencies.yaml) — Type-level validation (required_sections, quality_checks, Why: The catalog must have a single source of truth for artifact-type dependencies. The rubric must stop asking for a file the catalog does not produce. Any consumer that currently mentions dependencies.yaml must be repointed at meta.yml so the rubric and the workflow agree. · ADR-005 Concern practices.md is HELIX-activity-keyed — Every concern's practices.md organizes its per-activity content under HELIX activity headings: Discover, Frame, Design, Test, Build, Deploy, Iterate. Topic-keyed or quality-gates-keyed organization is no longer acceptable for practices.md. Why: Per-activity context-digest assembly must be a deterministic lookup, not a heuristic. The concerns library must use one consistent organizing principle so that any consumer — context assembler, alignment review, methodology docs — extracts the practices for a given activity by reading the corresponding heading.</adrs>
<governing>PLAN-2026-06-11-FLOW-ARCHITECTURE</governing>
</context-digest>

Break down and execute the approved HELIX flow architecture documentation cleanup. HELIX is document/catalog/graph metadata plus one public helix routing skill. The cleanup aligns PRD, architecture, skill, source docs, website mirrors, fixtures, and validation ratchets around flow/scope-instance/domain-lane/workflow-mode terminology. Execution must preserve the product boundary: HELIX has no user-facing CLI, env-var routing, queue, worker, git/CI/deploy, or runtime dispatch surface. Specs capture intent/contracts, not implementation status. Use ddx doc stamp only on changed and reviewed governed docs; never mass-stamp with ddx doc stamp --all.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — check_prompt() { local scope="${1:-repo}" cat <<EOF Execute the following HELIX check action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/check.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead ready --json\, \ddx bead list --json\, \ddx bead blocked --json\. Review scope: ${scope}. CRITICAL: Your output MUST contain the line "NEXT_ACTION: CODE" where CODE is one of BUILD, DESIGN, POLISH, ALIGN, BACKFILL, WAIT, GUIDANCE, or STOP. This line must appear on its own, not inside a code block or backticks. Without this line the automation cannot continue. Output this line FIRST, before any other analysis. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:1087
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: run_loop still computes a specific selected_issue and then applies queue-drift checks, epic-focus bookkeeping, acceptance-failure attribution, and post-cycle review to that bead, but run_execute_loop_once delegates with ddx agent execute-loop --once and does not pass any supported selector. The DDx contract only says execute-loop claims the next ready bead, so HELIX can no longer assume DDx executed the same bead that local scheduling selected. If DDx drains a different ready item, HELIX will mark the wrong bead as failed, blocked, reviewed, or promoted. The test shim in tests/helix-cli.sh currently hides this mismatch by reading the private HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE environment variable, which is not part of the documented DDx surface.
Suggested fix: preserve the HELIX-selected bead across the managed path, or consume the actual bead ID/result returned by DDx before any post-cycle bookkeeping. Add a deterministic harness case where DDx drains a different ready bead unless HELIX uses a supported selector/result surface.

title: integration: run loop delegates execute-loop without preserving HELIX-selected issue

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-006 — Each concern must declare its category, area scope, components,</governing>
</context-digest>

Review tracker evidence, close-path durability, and validator behavior for repo scope. Focus on closing_commit_sha propagation, omission authorization, validator robustness, and whether tracker evidence remains trustworthy for later review/report passes.

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:817
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new align guard rejects every matching `status: in_progress` governing bead without checking whether the claim is stale. In a harness repro that seeds an `align: repo` bead claimed by dead PID `99999`, `helix align repo` now exits with `alignment already active for repo under owner helix (hx-align-stale); refusing duplicate dispatch` instead of reclaiming the stale governing bead and continuing. That leaves the reviewed issue's intended reclaim behavior unresolved for standalone `helix align` runs and any path that reaches `run_alignment()` without the run-loop orphan-recovery prepass.
Suggested fix: Before failing duplicate dispatch, detect whether the in-progress align bead is orphaned (dead or absent claiming PID / beyond the reclaim threshold) and reclaim it through the existing tracker recovery path; keep the hard failure only for live active owners. Add a deterministic regression that covers stale-bead reclaim in addition to the live-owner rejection path.
title: integration: stale in-progress align bead blocks reclaim

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/deployment-checklist/meta.yml:19 and workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/monitoring-setup/meta.yml:23
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 82cf344 removes the runbook's backward outputs, but the 05-deploy graph is still not order-consistent. `deployment-checklist` declares `creation_order: 1` while requiring `monitoring-setup` and `runbook`, and `monitoring-setup` declares `creation_order: 2` while requiring `runbook`. Automation that treats `dependencies.requires` as upstream prerequisites still sees forward edges to later artifacts, so the deploy graph remains cyclic or unschedulable despite the closed bead claiming consistency.
Suggested fix: Reconcile `creation_order` and `dependencies.requires` across deployment-checklist, monitoring-setup, and runbook so every deploy-phase prerequisite points only to an earlier-or-equal artifact, or move those references into non-prerequisite relationship fields if they are informational only.

acceptance: docs/install/README.md exists. It states the minimal runtime contract, indexes the three per-runtime docs, points at workflows/ and skills/helix/SKILL.md as source of truth, and names the no-fork policy. Either it is linked from the website homepage or a follow-up bead exists for that homepage link.
description:
Create docs/install/README.md as the entry point for adopters. It must:

1. State HELIX's minimal runtime contract: a compliant runtime is one that can (a) read markdown files, (b) write markdown files, (c) search files by path/pattern, (d) optionally execute shell commands. No more is assumed. Link to PRD R-4 + 'Constraints' for the formal statement.
2. Index the three runtime install docs: Claude Code, Codex (CLI + extension), Databricks Code Genie. One-paragraph teaser each, with a 'best fit for…' line.
3. Link to workflows/ for the artifact catalog and skills/helix/SKILL.md for the routing skill — explicitly note these are the source of truth; per-runtime docs only describe discovery + invocation.
4. State the install-time fork policy: per-runtime docs are not allowed to fork content. If a runtime requires content adaptation, that adaptation lives in the per-runtime doc as a clearly-marked shim, not in skills/ or workflows/.
5. Link from the homepage. (Coordinate with whoever maintains website/content; if a homepage link cannot be added in this bead, raise a follow-up bead.)
title: Author docs/install/README.md index + minimal runtime contract

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:205
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The refreshed workflow docs now tell triage, evolve, polish, and context-digest assembly to use the repo helper when it exists, but the helper does not implement the required ADR and governing-spec phases of the FEAT-006 assembly algorithm. `build_digest()` never resolves ADR references at all, and `build_governing()` reduces governing context to `spec-id` plus the first acceptance clause instead of loading the referenced artifact and extracting the governing requirement. The live digest on helix-7b3c6980 shows the result: no <adrs> block and only `FEAT-006 — Open beads in scope...` as governing context. That leaves future beads with materially less authority than the workflow contract promises while the docs advertise the helper as the canonical deterministic path.
Suggested fix: Either extend the helper to resolve ADR references and governing clauses from the referenced spec files, or narrow the workflow docs so the helper is explicitly limited to label refresh/basic digest normalization until it implements the full FEAT-006 assembly contract.
title: drift: make digest helper honor ADR and governing-spec assembly requirements

Create the Docker test scenario for DDx. Layout:
- `tests/install/ddx/Dockerfile` — minimal base image with the runtime CLI installed.
- `tests/install/ddx/install.sh` — runs: `ddx install helix --local /workspace/helix --force`
- `tests/install/ddx/verify.sh` — runs `tests/install/shared/verify-skill-layout.sh` against the installed location, plus a functional check gated by `TEST_FUNCTIONAL=1` that invokes the runtime non-interactively and asserts the response names modes from `expected-modes.txt`.
- `tests/install/ddx/record.tape` — vhs script that records the install + verify sequence to `tests/install/ddx/recording.gif`.

Notes: ddx CLI installed in base image; `ddx install helix --local --force` against a mounted source; verify-skill-layout.sh against `~/.ddx/plugins/helix/skills/helix`.

<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

description:
PROBLEM: Rep home, manager home, and entity detail views (contact, account, opportunity, lead) have no wireframes, blocking frontend implementation of the primary navigation surfaces.
ROOT CAUSE: docs/design/wireframes/views/ is empty; crm.prd §personas distinguishes rep vs manager home but layouts are unspecified (docs/helix/01-frame/crm.prd.md:personas-section).
PROPOSED FIX: Author docs/design/wireframes/views/{rep-home,manager-home,contact-detail,account-detail,opportunity-detail,lead-detail}.md with ASCII layouts, region annotations, and data-source references to crm.api-contract endpoints. Opportunity detail must include an embedded activity feed region.
NON-SCOPE: Auth flows, kanban board, interaction modals, CSV import/export, mobile responsiveness, accessibility checklist.
GOVERNING ARTIFACTS: docs/helix/01-frame/crm.prd.md, docs/helix/02-design/crm.api-contract.md.
DEPENDENCIES: crm.prd, crm.api-contract.
notes: Cancelled 2026-05-19: CRM-DESIGN-006 (UI/UX wireframes) decomposition hit 5 iterations producing no standalone wireframe artifacts under docs/examples/crm/, only inline mentions in prd.md and solution-design.md. Wireframes are not a HELIX artifact type (HELIX defines product-vision, PRD, ADRs, technical-design, etc.; wireframes are a design technique downstream of HELIX, not part of it). The worked example is complete with the HELIX-defined artifact set already in docs/examples/crm/. No further wireframe beads should be filed under this epic; if wireframe-style content is wanted for the worked example, add it as a single static doc page authored by hand, not a HELIX-governed artifact.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>15f6198adfd4ed22649dbd9401e001f93d092d8d</governing>
<evidence>- HEAD commit a381afe93c8f6377ca6ce2f2560f28947df75b05 is tracker-only closure for helix-7d158bdf
- Automated review scope requested: last-commit in helix run loop
- Reviewed implementation bead: helix-7d158bdf
- Reviewed implementation commit resolved from tracker-only close commit: 15f6198adfd4ed22649dbd9401e001f93d092d8d</evidence>
</context-digest>

description:

No further wireframe beads should be filed under this epic; if wireframe-style content is wanted for the worked example, add it as a single static doc page authored by hand, not a HELIX-governed artifact.

NON-SCOPE: Do not change Hugo/Hextra theme behavior, website publishing configuration, demo asciinema recordings, agent harness invocation, OpenTelemetry runtime implementation, DDx/HELIX execution engine behavior, or enforcement behavior outside the design-phase contract recognition/update surface.

description:

Scope per demo: helix-align, helix-brief, helix-adopt, helix-plan, helix-evolve, helix-concerns, helix-review, helix-execute.

For each demo:
1. Re-capture with capture_session.py using the fixture inputs already in docs/demos/<demo>/.
2. Verify the captured transcript against the existing assertions.yml; update assertions.yml when the modernized contract intentionally changes expected slices, with a one-line rationale per change in a CHANGELOG entry or PR body.
3. Confirm the demo renders correctly on the demos page (website/content/use or docs/demos integration — whichever the current pipeline uses).
4. Spot-check that the new transcripts demonstrate the new capabilities (validate mode in align demo; graph-threading in evolve demo) where natural.

Dependencies: must wait on helix-2b235e7e Track B child beads (skill-capability completion) and on helix-d8f307d4 (workflows split).

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/phases/05-deploy/enforcer.md:224
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 5b99811120e4be20852613452de2e19f55063ebd updates workflows/phases/05-deploy/README.md to treat `deployment-checklist`, `monitoring-setup`, `runbook`, and `release-notes` as the live deploy artifact set, but workflows/phases/05-deploy/enforcer.md still tells operators to create only checklists, runbooks, and monitoring configurations as needed. The deploy phase guidance is therefore still internally inconsistent after the README fix: the gate and artifact metadata require four first-class outputs under docs/helix/05-deploy/, while the enforcer continues to advertise the older three-artifact model.
Suggested fix: Update the Deploy Artifacts section in workflows/phases/05-deploy/enforcer.md so it names the current four-artifact contract and points operators at the canonical docs/helix/05-deploy/ outputs.

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/state-rules.yml:46
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 27c7051 makes ITERATE require all matching deploy beads to be complete and no open deploy bead to remain, but the surrounding story-state contract still treats active deploy work as `open` only. HELIX claims execution beads by setting `status: in_progress` (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-002-helix-cli.md:173-179, workflows/EXECUTION.md:457, tests/helix-cli.sh:2364). A story with a claimed deploy bead therefore no longer satisfies the updated ITERATE condition, yet the written DEPLOY examples and query hints still talk only about open deploy issues. That leaves the contract without a deterministic phase for stories whose deploy work is actively in progress.
Suggested fix: Update the deploy/iterate state-detection contract so any non-closed matching `phase:deploy` bead, including `in_progress`, keeps the story in DEPLOY. Align workflows/state-rules.yml, workflows/state-machine.yaml, workflows/artifact-hierarchy.md, and tests/validate-state-rules.sh around that tracker-status rule.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>1c509db1f91b2869edf63c3afc820715869e67ec</governing>
</context-digest>

acceptance: The HELIX iterate contract defines a deterministic way to prove that story `US-{id}` reached ITERATE. Either canonical iterate output templates include an explicit story-reference field with documented query rules, or the story state machine stops requiring shared iterate docs to reference `US-{id}` and instead uses deploy/tracker evidence that already exists.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/state-machine.yaml:224
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new contract says a story reaches ITERATE when shared iterate outputs or linked follow-on work reference `US-{id}`, but the canonical iterate artifacts do not define any required story identifier or story-reference field. `metrics-dashboard`, `security-metrics`, and `improvement-backlog` are iteration-wide documents, and their templates currently expose review windows, metrics, recommendations, and tracker refs without any story-scoped key. As written, the new state machine requires story-linked iterate evidence that the canonical iterate outputs are not required to carry, so story-level iterate detection becomes non-deterministic whenever a deploy completes without separate follow-on issues.
Suggested fix: Either add an explicit story-reference contract to the iterate outputs/templates and document how it is queried, or keep story-level iterate detection anchored in deploy/tracker evidence until a deterministic story-keyed iterate artifact exists.

acceptance: ddx bead show helix-8093c918 reports closing_commit_sha=d242771dbfcbb8baf7585f6c49f049b5aad30f13, and automated last-commit review resolution for that bead targets the queue-snapshot implementation commit rather than the unrelated parent tracker-close commit.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:99
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit d242771 closes bead helix-8093c918 in .ddx/beads.jsonl, but the row records closing_commit_sha=5122af50a8015de1f340f84a5965f79f5ba8cf31, which is the unrelated parent commit that closed helix-420f4f76. That leaves the tracker pointing at the wrong implementation diff for helix-8093c918. In the automated review loop, fresh-eyes review prefers a bead's closing_commit_sha when resolving the implementation commit behind tracker-only follow-up commits, so this bad SHA can send later review or archaeology to an unrelated change and break durable evidence for what actually fixed the queue snapshot.
Suggested fix: Update helix-8093c918 so closing_commit_sha records the actual implementation commit d242771dbfcbb8baf7585f6c49f049b5aad30f13, and tighten the closure bookkeeping path so a bead closed in the current commit cannot inherit an unrelated parent SHA.

description:
PROBLEM: Data models (Contact, Account, Lead, Opportunity, Activity, User) lack unit test coverage for validation rules and audit field behavior, risking silent data corruption.

ROOT CAUSE: internal/models/*.go (e.g., internal/models/contact.go:1, internal/models/account.go:1, internal/models/lead.go:1, internal/models/opportunity.go:1, internal/models/activity.go:1, internal/models/user.go:1) define struct types and validation hooks but have no corresponding *_test.go files.

PROPOSED FIX: Add table-driven unit tests in internal/models/*_test.go covering required-field validation, format validation (email, phone), enum constraints, and audit-field (CreatedAt/UpdatedAt/CreatedBy/UpdatedBy) population on Create/Update hooks. Use testify/assert and an in-memory SQLite (gorm.io/driver/sqlite with :memory:) for hook tests.

NON-SCOPE: API handlers, RBAC, cross-workspace isolation, performance benchmarks, CSV import/export, E2E flows.

AC:
1. TestContactValidation, TestAccountValidation, TestLeadValidation, TestOpportunityValidation, TestActivityValidation, TestUserValidation cover required fields, format rules, and enum constraints with table-driven cases.

3. `go test ./internal/models/... -race -count=1` passes.

<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix-30814e44</governing>
</context-digest>

description:
Create tests/workflows/claude-code/ harness that exercises live `claude -p` against the HELIX skill bundle inside a Docker container.

**Deliverables**:
- `tests/workflows/claude-code/run-scenarios.sh` — orchestrator that builds image, runs each scenario, captures output, exits nonzero on any failure
- `tests/workflows/claude-code/scenarios/install-verify.prompt` — "Confirm HELIX is installed: list the routing skill name and the artifact catalog activities"
- `tests/workflows/claude-code/scenarios/skill-list.prompt` — "List the HELIX routing modes you can route to. Be concise."
- `tests/workflows/claude-code/scenarios/bootstrap.prompt` — TP-014 SCN-01 prompt: "I want to build a TODO list app. Use HELIX to capture the framing artifacts."
- `tests/workflows/claude-code/expected/` — assertion-text snippets each scenario response must contain (e.g., "input", "frame", "align" mode names; vision/PRD file artifacts; etc.)
- `tests/workflows/claude-code/recordings/INT-CC.tape` — vhs script
- `tests/workflows/claude-code/recordings/INT-CC.gif` — generated recording (committed)

**Harness shape** (reuse `tests/install/claude-code/Dockerfile` as the base image):
1. `docker build -t helix-int-test:claude-code -f tests/install/claude-code/Dockerfile .`
2. For each scenario file: `docker run --rm -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY -v $PWD:/workspace/helix:ro helix-int-test:claude-code bash -c "claude -p < /workspace/helix/tests/workflows/claude-code/scenarios/<name>.prompt"`
3. Grep response for expected text from `expected/<name>.expect`
4. vhs renders the orchestrator session

acceptance: A deterministic repo test fails on a checked-in vague execution-ready acceptance fixture, passes when the fixture is flagged or rejected as not execution-ready, and runs from the standard validation lane together with bash tests/validate-skills.sh
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tests/validate-skills.sh:207
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The implementation acceptance requires deterministic validation of at least one rejection or flagging path for non-measurable acceptance text, but the new validator only checks for literal substrings in docs and skills. `bash tests/validate-skills.sh` passes as long as the wording remains present, even if `helix-triage` or `helix-polish` never actually reject or reroute a vague execution-ready bead. A regression in the real queue-readiness behavior would therefore ship with a green validation lane because the test never exercises any negative case.
Suggested fix: Add a deterministic fixture-based validation that feeds vague acceptance text such as `works correctly` through the canonical triage/polish validation surface, or a dedicated helper used by those surfaces, and assert that the bead is flagged not execution-ready or rejected.

1. Unit tests for data models: Contact, Account, Lead, Opportunity, Activity, User; test validation, audit field updates.

3. Integration tests for cross-workspace isolation: same endpoint called with different workspace_id returns different results or 403.

5. Integration tests for soft-delete: deleted records not returned in list/get; pagination adjusted for deleted count.
6. E2E tests for major workflows: sign-up → create account → create opportunity → log activity → view pipeline; import CSV → verify records; export CSV → verify contents.
7. E2E tests for auth: sign-up, login (valid/invalid), logout, session/JWT expiry.
8. Performance tests: search response <1s for 100k records; pipeline view <500ms for 25 reps; import 1000 records <5s.
9. Test database: use in-memory SQLite (for CI) or containerized PostgreSQL (for integration).
10. Test coverage: aim for ≥80% code coverage on API endpoints; 100% on critical paths (auth, RBAC, cross-workspace checks).

acceptance: Tag push triggers install-test-full alongside release-genie-bundle. All four terminal scenarios pass with functional checks. Four .gif recordings attached to the release. A test tag that breaks the marketplace flow halts the release before publishing assets.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>claude-code-install | manifest-consistency | ci-coverage | marketplace-flow</concerns>
<practices>Canonical repo: DocumentDrivenDX/helix · Marketplace flow tested on PR (--plugin-dir HEAD smoke) AND release tag (real flow) · Functional checks in CI gated to release tags with GH secrets · Screencasts auto-captured by vhs and attached to release · No silent fallbacks: failed install must fail the test, not WARN-and-continue</practices>
<governing>plan §Phase E (A5 release cadence) · .github/workflows/release-genie-bundle.yml</governing>
</context-digest>

Extend `.github/workflows/release-genie-bundle.yml` (or create a new `install-test-release.yml` triggered on tag push) with an `install-test-full` job that:
  - Builds the Docker images under tests/install/{ddx,claude-code,codex-cli,copilot-cli}/
  - Runs install + verify per runtime with TEST_FUNCTIONAL=1
  - Pulls auth from GitHub Actions secrets: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GH_TOKEN (with Copilot license)
  - Genie scenario is manual-only; skip in CI with explicit log (`genie:browser-required` tag) — Databricks creds not in CI secrets
  - Captures vhs screencasts for each runtime; attaches as release artifacts
  - Fails the release if any runtime scenario fails (no silent degradation)
  - Cost cap: fail if total token spend exceeds $10 per run (estimated $2-5 typical)
Functional checks remain release-only — never on PR.
title: C8: add install-test-full job for release-tag runs (A5 full Docker matrix)

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>workflow-coverage | shared-fixture | runtime-parity | refusal-hardening</concerns>
<practices>tests/workflows/fixtures/recipe-app/ is the single shared fixture across all 5 runtimes · Pass criteria are runtime-neutral; per-runtime variants vary by invocation/observability only · Static checks free, functional checks gated by TEST_FUNCTIONAL=1 · Genie scenarios may emit `genie:catalog-unreachable` skip until helix-96f7dd34 resolves · Symlink/inherit from tests/install/<runtime>/install.sh where reusable</practices>
<governing>FEAT-014 FIX-01 · TP-014 §4 fixture spec</governing>
</context-digest>

Create the five seed prompt files all scenarios consume:
- `seed/intent.txt` — ~30 words. Sparse user intent for scenario 1 (bootstrap). Example: 'Build a recipe-sharing app for amateur cooks. Users browse, save, and post recipes; basic comments and ratings; mobile-first.'
- `seed/change-request.txt` — ~40 words. SQLite → Postgres for scenario 3 (evolve).
- `seed/contradiction.txt` — ~50 words. Feature request that contradicts ADR-001 (e.g. MongoDB as primary store), for scenario 7b.
- `seed/ambiguous.txt` — ~15 words. Genuinely ambiguous intent for scenario 7c (e.g. 'Help me with that thing we discussed.').
- `seed/mechanical-prompts.txt` — list of one-line mechanical/non-HELIX prompts for scenario 7d non-match (e.g. 'pull and install', 'Use gpt-5.4-mini', 'Stop the worker.').
title: FIX-01: author tests/workflows/fixtures/recipe-app/seed/ (5 seed prompt files)

acceptance:
1. Each workspace has unique email address (e.g., workspace-<uuid>@crm.example.com).
2. Email forwarding creates activity (kind=email) on matching contact (matched by email sender).
3. Matching algorithm: exact email match on contact.email; if no match, prompt user to select contact (or skip).
4. Activity body includes email subject + first 200 chars of body.
5. Email-thread deduplication: same email thread (Re: same subject) creates single activity (optional, P2).
6. Attachments: not stored in CRM (could integrate with cloud storage in P2).
7. Requires email service (SendGrid, AWS SES, others) and webhook handling (cloud provider receives email, triggers webhook).
description: Forward email to workspace address; auto-logs activity on matching contact. Req R-10: email forwarded to crm-<workspace-id>@example.com creates activity. Status: P1. Depends on CRUD, activity, auth, cloud email service. Open decision: email matching algorithm and cloud file storage.

description:

ROOT CAUSE: docs/crm.prd:activities-section and list-view sections describe filter/search and bulk behaviors behaviorally but lack interaction wireframes; no flow diagrams in docs/design/wireframes/flows/ for discovery/selection paths.

PROPOSED FIX: Produce wireframes for filter/search interaction and bulk select (annotated as P1). Deliver under docs/design/wireframes/flows/.

NON-SCOPE: Core view layouts (CRM-DESIGN-006b); CSV flows (CRM-DESIGN-006d); responsive/a11y (CRM-DESIGN-006e); authentication flows (CRM-DESIGN-006a); backend activity validation rules; activity logging flows (sibling child); kanban drag-drop (sibling child).

Governing artifacts: depends on CRM-DESIGN-006b (list views referenced by interactions); crm.prd and API contract per parent.
title: CRM-DESIGN-006c-2: Discovery & Selection Interaction Flows

<practices>Execution and review cycles must leave durable tracker evidence on the bead itself · Closed beads should record measurement results, closure context, and implementation commit references so later agents can verify outcomes without git archaeology · User-facing docs fixes and tracker state should stay aligned in the same pass</practices>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:133
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit f697786ca433dbfaaefd4b14a26500dd3a8a7d94 closes review-finding bead helix-66c50c8e, but the bead row still has no <measure-results> block, no report-style closure summary, and no closing_commit_sha. HELIX's execution contract requires implementation cycles to record measurement evidence on the governing bead and then close it through report, so ddx bead show helix-66c50c8e cannot tell a later agent what passed or which commit implemented the fix without re-reading git history.
Suggested fix: Append durable measure/report evidence to helix-66c50c8e before or while closing it, including the implementation commit, acceptance evidence for docs/README.md, and a short closure summary.

acceptance:
AC1 TestReviewRunnerInjectsBead — after a cycle completes, Runner.Run calls tracker.Inject with kind=review-finding, correct parent and payload fields, and does not invoke the legacy hook writer.
AC2 TestNoAutoReviewFlagSuppressesInjection — invoking `helix review --no-auto-review` (and setting helix.review.auto_inject=false) causes Runner.Run to skip injection and return nil; tracker has no new review-finding bead afterward.
AC3 TestExecuteLoopConsumesReviewFinding — end-to-end: a parent bead completes, review-finding bead is injected, execute-loop pops it on the next tick, runs the review handler, and writes the evidence bundle to the canonical path.
AC4 TestDeprecatedHookKeyWarns — setting only the legacy hooks.auto_review key still works but emits a deprecation warning naming the new key.
AC5 Gates — `go test ./internal/helix/review/... ./cmd/helix/...` passes; `lefthook run pre-commit` passes; `go vet ./...` clean.
description:
PROBLEM: HELIX `review` currently runs the review engine inline as a post-cycle hook on the parent bead and writes findings directly; this bypasses the ready queue, prevents scheduling/retry, and produces hook-shaped output rather than a standard evidence bundle. Operators cannot opt out except by disabling the entire hook system.

ROOT CAUSE: internal/helix/review/runner.go:1 — Runner.Run invokes engine.Execute synchronously after cycle completion and writes to .helix/reviews/ rather than calling tracker.Inject. cmd/helix/review.go:1 — CLI has no `--no-auto-review` flag; auto-injection toggle is buried in config under hooks.auto_review which is a runtime hook switch, not an injection switch.

PROPOSED FIX: (a) Replace the post-cycle hook invocation with `tracker.Inject(ctx, parent, BeadKindReviewFinding, payload)` using the shared API from child 1; payload carries parent bead id, base_rev, result_rev, and review profile id. (b) Add `--no-auto-review` flag to `cmd/helix/review.go` and equivalent `helix.review.auto_inject` config key (default true); when false, Runner.Run returns without injecting. (c) Delete the old hook-output writer call path once injection is wired; keep config key `hooks.auto_review` only as a deprecated alias that maps to the new key with a one-cycle warning.

NON-SCOPE: Alignment path (covered by child 3). Tracker bead-kind plumbing (covered by child 1). Reformatting the review-finding payload schema beyond the fields named above. Inherits all parent non-scope items. Governing artifact: parent helix-e26584d7. Depends on: child 1 (bead kind + Inject API + execute-loop dispatcher).

description:
PROBLEM: Refresh has no canonical drifted-artifact inputs to exercise against; tests cannot demonstrate behavior across activities or classifications.

ROOT CAUSE: tests/refresh/fixture/ does not exist in the repository (filesystem absence). Per plan v4 Test fixture section, one drifted artifact per activity 00-discover..06-iterate is required plus an explicit DIVERGENT fixture under 06-iterate/.

PROPOSED FIX: Create tests/refresh/fixture/00-discover/, 01-frame/, 02-design/, 03-test/, 04-build/, 05-deploy/, 06-iterate/ each containing one drifted artifact (markdown with frontmatter). Add a second artifact under 06-iterate/ explicitly marked classification: DIVERGENT. Frontmatter on every fixture sets `classification:` to one of INCOMPLETE | DIVERGENT | STALE_PLAN so downstream assertions are deterministic. At least one STALE_PLAN fixture must appear somewhere in 00-..06-.

NON-SCOPE: Refresh logic implementation; expected/ tree (separate child); run.sh harness (separate child); fixtures outside activities 00-..06-.

AC:
1. TestFixtureActivityCoverage: each of tests/refresh/fixture/{00-discover,01-frame,02-design,03-test,04-build,05-deploy,06-iterate}/ contains >=1 markdown artifact.
2. TestFixtureDivergentUnderIterate: tests/refresh/fixture/06-iterate/ contains exactly one artifact with `classification: DIVERGENT` and at least one other artifact.
3. TestFixtureStalePlanPresent: at least one fixture in the tree has `classification: STALE_PLAN`.
4. TestFixtureClassificationsValid: every fixture file parses and its `classification` is in {INCOMPLETE, DIVERGENT, STALE_PLAN}.
5. TestFixtureNoExtraneousActivities: no directories exist under tests/refresh/fixture/ outside the 00-..06- set.

Gates: `go test ./tests/refresh/...` (fixture-layout tests) and `lefthook run pre-commit` both pass.
title: C1: Refresh fixture tree (drifted artifacts per activity)

acceptance: scripts/demos/check_assertions.py parses list items with colon-containing values correctly. description: scripts/demos/check_assertions.py's parse_assertions function has an indent-precedence bug: a list item like '  - "bun:test"' (indent 6, value contains a colon) is parsed as a dict entry instead of a list element because the indent-6 dict branch wins over the list-item branch when ':' appears in the value. Worked around in helix-concerns demo by using 'bun test' (space-separated). Real fix: the parser should recognize the leading '- ' as a list item before considering the colon as a dict separator. Surfaced by helix-4d57d085 / helix-concerns subagent.
title: Fix check_assertions.py parser quirk: colon in list-item values

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — review_prompt() { local scope="${1:-last-commit}" local in_loop="${2:-0}" cat <<EOF Execute the following HELIX fresh-eyes review action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/fresh-eyes-review.md") Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead\ subcommands. Review scope: ${scope}. ${in_loop:+This review is running inside the automated helix run loop after an implementation cycle.} IMPORTANT: File each actionable finding (critical, high, medium severity) as a tracker issue with label \review-finding\ so findings are durable and queryable via \ddx bead list --label review-finding\. See the "Filing Findings as Tracker Issues" section in the action for the exact command pattern. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:246
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The reviewed commit makes `helix input` the preferred first-class intake surface in FEAT-002, FEAT-011, workflows/EXECUTION.md, and workflows/REFERENCE.md, but the shipped `helix help` output still omits `helix input` entirely and presents `helix run` as the leading surface. Operators following the live CLI help cannot discover the new contract, so the docs-to-CLI boundary remains inconsistent after commit 466924b.
Suggested fix: Add `helix input` to the usage/command help text with its autonomy flag shape, and update the help wording so `helix run` / `helix build` are clearly compatibility wrappers relative to the DDx-managed path. Add deterministic help-output coverage to prevent regression.

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts:1
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The FEAT-007 verification lane is green, but the Playwright suite still does not exercise the user-facing search workflow at all. A text search over website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts shows page-load, navigation, and screenshot coverage only; there are no search input interactions or assertions on result routing. That violates the active e2e-playwright concern practice that every user-facing workflow have at least one end-to-end test, and it is why the broken /glossary search route in the rendered index was able to ship undetected in commit e61a4f6.
Suggested fix: Add a Playwright scenario that opens site search, queries at least glossary and CLI content, asserts the result URLs are valid, and verifies navigation succeeds when a result is selected.

description:
PROBLEM: Refresh outputs cannot be diff-asserted without a golden oracle that distinguishes auto-fixable from handoff-only classifications.

ROOT CAUSE: tests/refresh/expected/ does not exist. Per plan v4, INCOMPLETE fixtures get a post-refresh shape; DIVERGENT and STALE_PLAN fixtures must remain byte-identical to their fixture/ counterparts (Refresh must not mutate them).

PROPOSED FIX: Mirror the directory layout of tests/refresh/fixture/ under tests/refresh/expected/. For each INCOMPLETE fixture, author the post-refresh shape (filled-in fields, normalized frontmatter, body completions). For each DIVERGENT and STALE_PLAN fixture, copy the fixture file verbatim into expected/ at the same relative path.

NON-SCOPE: Refresh contract behavior; fixture authoring (C1); harness script (C3); semantics of "normalized frontmatter" beyond what plan v4 specifies for INCOMPLETE outputs.

AC:
1. TestExpectedMirrorsFixture: for every file F at tests/refresh/fixture/<rel>, a file exists at tests/refresh/expected/<rel>.
2. TestExpectedNoExtraFiles: tests/refresh/expected/ contains no files lacking a counterpart in tests/refresh/fixture/.
3. TestIncompleteExpectedDiffers: every fixture with `classification: INCOMPLETE` differs from its expected counterpart in at least one frontmatter or body field.
4. TestDivergentExpectedIdentical: every fixture with `classification: DIVERGENT` is byte-identical to its expected counterpart.
5. TestStalePlanExpectedIdentical: every fixture with `classification: STALE_PLAN` is byte-identical to its expected counterpart.

Gates: `go test ./tests/refresh/...` (oracle-shape tests) and `lefthook run pre-commit` both pass. Depends on C1 fixture tree being in place.
title: C2: Refresh expected/ oracle tree

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<governing>FEAT-005 — alignment_prompt() { local scope="${1:-repo}" governing_bead="${2:-}" governing_mode="${3:-}" local governing_note="Governing alignment bead: create or claim the relevant kind:planning,action:align bead before proceeding." if [[ -n "$governing_bead" ]]; then case "$governing_mode" in claimed) governing_note="Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (claimed by the wrapper entrypoint; treat Phase 0.5 as already satisfied unless tracker metadata drifted materially)." ;; reused) governing_note="Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (already active for this scope; reuse it and do not create a duplicate governing bead)." ;; created) governing_note="Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (created by the wrapper entrypoint; claim and reuse it instead of creating a duplicate governing bead)." ;; esac fi cat <<EOF Execute the following HELIX alignment action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/reconcile-alignment.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/templates/alignment-review.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead\ subcommands. Review scope: ${scope}. ${governing_note} Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:819
Category: drift

acceptance: 1) helix stuck --json emits the full schema above; jq -e '.[] | select(.bead_id == null or .reason == null or .suggested_action == null)' returns no rows on all fixture scenarios. 2) Integration fixture retires an orphaned-claim blocker created in a prior session (fixture setup demonstrates claimed_pid != executor's $$); assertion fails if the stuck bead's claim was made by the test runner. 3) Parity checklist: for each of dep-blocked, retry-parked, orphaned-claim, epic-blocked-via-child, and queue-drift, there is a fixture and assertion that helix stuck surfaces it with the correct reason and suggested_action. 4) tests/helix-cli.sh test test_helix_stuck_retires_foreign_session_blocker passes.
description:
Implement 'helix stuck' as a HELIX-side subcommand (scripts/helix dispatch + case) composing existing DDx primitives: ddx bead list --status in_progress --json (filter by claim age and pid liveness), ddx bead blocked --json (dep-blocked), plus retry-after filter over ddx bead list --json (retry-parked). No new ddx subcommand.

Output schema (helix stuck --json): array of {bead_id, claim_age_seconds|null, claimed_pid_alive<bool|null>, blocking_deps: [ids], reason: enum(dep-blocked|retry-parked|orphaned-claim), suggested_action: enum(unclaim|defer|escalate), next_eligible_at|null}.

Retires one real blocker report end-to-end: fixture seeds a tracker where a bead has been claimed by a pid that does NOT belong to the executor (claimed_pid != $$), orphan-threshold passed; helix stuck identifies it, operator applies suggested_action unclaim via ddx bead update --unclaim, bead becomes execution-ready. The stuck bead must NOT have been created or claimed by the executor of this bead — greppable via fixture setup showing claimed_pid distinct from the agent's process id.

Absorbs the intent of .helix-logs/blockers-*.md: once helix stuck covers the five classes currently reported there (intractable after retries, dep-chain, orphaned claim, epic-blocked-via-child, queue-drift), the blocker markdown writer can be demolished by a later bead.

acceptance: The demo re-recording tool (script under scripts/ or tests/) creates relative symlinks. A pre-commit/CI lint fails if any symlink under docs/demos/**/fixture/.claude/skills/ has an absolute target. After the next demo refresh, `find docs/demos -type l -xtype l` returns empty on Linux.
description:
## Summary

Today (2026-05-18) the 8 demo-fixture symlinks under docs/demos/<demo>/fixture/.claude/skills/helix had been re-broken with absolute /Users/erik/Projects/helix/skills/helix targets, blocking `ddx install --force --local` on every non-macOS system. Fixed in commit 0351e2e by replacing with ../../../../../../skills/helix.

BUT: git log shows this is a RECURRING fix:

- 0351e2e 2026-05-18 fix: relativise 8 demo-fixture symlinks (this fix)
- 2d301f7              chore: restore skill symlinks to relative canonical form
- 0a58a78              fix: migrate all workflow paths to repo-relative .ddx/plugins/helix/
- 746ee4e 2026-05-14 demos: re-record 8 session demos against modernized HELIX skill

Whatever process re-records the session demos (some scripts/demo-* tool, an asciinema fixture step, or a Hugo build hook) creates the .claude/skills/helix symlinks using absolute /Users/erik/Projects/helix/... paths every time it runs. This is a regression source that will recur on the next demo refresh.

## Fix

Find the demo-recording script(s) under `scripts/` or `tests/` and patch the symlink-creation step to use relative targets (e.g. ln -s ../../../../../../skills/helix). Add a pre-commit lint that fails if any `docs/demos/**/fixture/.claude/skills/helix` symlink target contains an absolute path.
title: demo re-recording process re-introduces /Users/erik-prefixed absolute symlinks; fix the generator

acceptance:
1. TestSignupEndpointShape — POST /auth/signup declared with LoginRequest-style body plus workspace name, 201 response references User and Workspace schemas, security: [].
2. TestLoginEndpointShape — POST /auth/login declared, requestBody references LoginRequest, 200 references LoginResponse, security: [].
3. TestLogoutEndpointShape — POST /auth/logout declared, 204 response, requires bearerAuth.
4. TestSecuritySchemeDefined — components.securitySchemes.bearerAuth present (type: http, scheme: bearer); document-level security applies bearerAuth.
5. TestAuthSchemasDefined — components.schemas includes User, Workspace, LoginRequest, LoginResponse.
6. Gate: `go test ./api/spec/...` passes.
7. Gate: `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM: Workspace signup, login, and logout endpoints have no documented contract; no security scheme is declared, so other endpoints cannot reference auth requirements.

ROOT CAUSE: docs/api/openapi.yaml:1 — no /auth/* paths and no components.securitySchemes entry exist; parent CRM-DESIGN-003 AC#4 is unmet (depends on CRM-DESIGN-003a, CRM-DESIGN-002 user/workspace model, ADR-001).

PROPOSED FIX: Extend docs/api/openapi.yaml. Add POST /auth/signup (creates workspace + first user, returns User + Workspace), POST /auth/login (returns auth token or sets session cookie), POST /auth/logout (invalidates session). Declare components.securitySchemes.bearerAuth (per ADR-001 framework convention) and apply it at the document level with auth endpoints overriding to security: []. Add User, Workspace, LoginRequest, LoginResponse schemas.

NON-SCOPE: CRUD entity endpoints (CRM-DESIGN-003a). Special endpoints (CRM-DESIGN-003b). ErrorResponse body, pagination, examples (CRM-DESIGN-003d). Password policy or hashing algorithm choice. Multi-factor auth. Password reset flow (handled in separate bead).
title: CRM-DESIGN-003c: OpenAPI spec — auth endpoints and security scheme

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<governing>scripts/refresh_context_digests.py — def main() -> int: parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Refresh HELIX bead context digests and area labels.") parser.add_argument("--apply", action="store_true", help="Write updates back to the tracker.") parser.add_argument("--bead", action="append", default=[], help="Restrict updates to specific bead IDs.") parser.add_argument("--root", help="Project root containing the target tracker and docs.") parser.add_argument( "--library-root", help="HELIX library root containing workflows/ and scripts/ resources.", ) parser.add_argument("--tracker", help="Explicit tracker path. Defaults to <root>/.ddx/beads.jsonl.") parser.add_argument("--status", default="open", help="Tracker status to target (default: open).") args = parser.parse_args()</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:170
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: The helper now resolves concern metadata and ADR refs only from <library_root>/workflows/concerns (scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:170,381). FEAT-006 still says projects can add custom concerns, but in plugin mode the project root and HELIX library root differ. Reproducing with --root <project> --library-root <plugin> and a concern defined only in <project>/workflows/concerns/custom-cli causes the helper to crash with FileNotFoundError on <library_root>/workflows/concerns/custom-cli/practices.md before any digest can be written. That turns supported project-local concerns into a hard failure for refresh_context_digests.py and any HELIX creation path that shells through it.
Suggested fix: Make concern resolution check project-local workflows/concerns/<name>/ first and fall back to the HELIX library copy, or otherwise merge both sources intentionally, then add a regression test that exercises distinct project-root and library-root paths with a custom concern.
title: integration: support project-local custom concerns during digest refresh

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:110
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new digest helper discards library practices whenever a concern has project overrides. `build_concern_library()` uses `overrides.get(name) or parse_practice_bullets(...)`, so every matched concern in this repo contributes only override bullets to the rebuilt digest. FEAT-006 and the context-digest reference require project overrides to be applied on top of library practices, not to replace them entirely. The resulting live digests omit base concern guidance such as library testing and artifact-sync practices, which weakens the self-contained execution context the helper was introduced to restore.
Suggested fix: Merge library practices with project overrides deterministically instead of choosing one or the other. Preserve the core area-matched library practices, then layer repo-specific override bullets on top when assembling the compact digest.

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/runbook/meta.yml:11
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The implementation bead for commit 82cf344 was measured with one-file `yq` checks against `runbook/meta.yml`, and the repo has no deterministic validation that checks cross-artifact creation-order consistency for the 05-deploy graph. That gap let the fix pass even though `deployment-checklist` and `monitoring-setup` still require later deploy artifacts. Without a shared validator, future graph edits can again report local success while leaving the canonical deploy contract contradictory.
Suggested fix: Add a deterministic repo validation that loads the 05-deploy artifact metadata together and fails when any `dependencies.requires` edge points to a later artifact or when the deploy artifact graph contains a cycle. Run it in the normal workflow-contract validation lane.

4. TestPaginationParametersDefined — components.parameters.Limit and components.parameters.Offset exist with the documented defaults and bounds.
5. TestListEndpointsUsePagination — GET /contacts, /accounts, /leads, /opportunities, /activities, /search each reference Limit and Offset parameters.

description:
PROBLEM: Endpoints authored in sibling beads have no shared error envelope, no pagination contract on list operations, and no example payloads; clients cannot generate fixtures or handle failures consistently.

ROOT CAUSE: docs/api/openapi.yaml:1 — components.schemas has no ErrorResponse type; list operations from CRM-DESIGN-003a/b declare no pagination parameters; no operation declares `examples` blocks. Parent CRM-DESIGN-003 AC#5, AC#6, AC#7 are unmet (depends on CRM-DESIGN-003a, CRM-DESIGN-003b, CRM-DESIGN-003c).

PROPOSED FIX: Extend docs/api/openapi.yaml. Define components.schemas.ErrorResponse (fields: code string, message string, details object optional). Add components.responses entries for BadRequest (400), Unauthorized (401), Forbidden (403), NotFound (404), Conflict (409), UnprocessableEntity (422), InternalError (500), each referencing ErrorResponse. Reference these responses from every operation. Define components.parameters.Limit (int, default 50, max 200) and components.parameters.Offset (int, default 0); reference both from every list endpoint (/contacts, /accounts, /leads, /opportunities, /activities, /search). Add at least one `examples` block on every operation's request (where applicable) and 2xx response.

NON-SCOPE: Adding new endpoint paths (owned by siblings 003a/b/c). Cursor-based pagination (limit/offset chosen for this iteration; revisit in evolve). Internationalization of error messages.
title: CRM-DESIGN-003d: API cross-cutting contracts — errors, pagination, examples

description:

Without a dedicated template/prompt/meta entry, authors have to force reusable interface specifications into the wrong artifact type. Governing artifact reference: workflows/phases/02-design/README.md. Dependency ID: helix-13cfe23f.
ROOT CAUSE: workflows/phases/02-design/README.md:1 expects design artifacts to be produced by the workflow bundle, but the bundle lacks a dedicated Contract template, prompt, and metadata entry aligned with docs/helix/02-design/contracts/ usage.
PROPOSED FIX: Add the Contract artifact template, prompt, and metadata files in the existing design workflow bundle structure using local naming conventions. The template must include sections for contract identity, scope, consumers/producers, normative fields, data types, units, enums, precedence rules, formulas, error semantics, compatibility/versioning, examples, and validation checklist.
NON-SCOPE: Do not change Hugo/Hextra theme behavior, website publishing configuration, demo asciinema recordings, agent harness invocation, OpenTelemetry runtime implementation, DDx/HELIX execution engine behavior, or unrelated artifact templates except for link/index updates required to expose Contract.
title: Add contract template, prompt, and metadata to the design workflow bundle

acceptance: A checked-in deterministic repo test reproduces the Pages Record demos flow with a failing wrapped command and proves no cast is copied; the same test proves a passing wrapped command still copies a cast; .github/workflows/test.yml runs that test in the standard validation lane.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>.github/workflows/test.yml — run: bash tests/validate-deploy-artifacts.sh</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .github/workflows/test.yml:25
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 15f6198 fixes the Pages recording loop in .github/workflows/pages.yml, and local repro confirms the new status-file check blocks copying failed demo casts. But the repository's standard validation lane still never exercises that failure path: .github/workflows/test.yml only runs bash tests/helix-cli.sh, bash tests/validate-deploy-artifacts.sh, bash tests/validate-state-rules.sh, bash tests/validate-skills.sh, and bash tests/test-install.sh, and rg confirms none of those suites reference pages.yml, Record demos, or the new status-file contract. That leaves the fix protected only by one-off local repro notes in the commit message, which violates the active demo-asciinema and e2e-playwright expectation that user-facing demo/site workflows have deterministic regression checks rather than manual-only verification.
Suggested fix: Add a checked-in deterministic test that simulates the Pages Record demos shell flow for both failing and passing wrapped demo commands, asserts the failing case exits before cast copy, wire that test into the normal repo validation lane, and keep the acceptance aligned with .github/workflows/pages.yml.

acceptance: `workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/runbook/meta.yml` no longer declares `deployment-checklist` or `monitoring-setup` as enabled outputs, and its relationship metadata is consistent with `creation_order: 3` and the listed `requires` dependencies.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/runbook/meta.yml:33
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The restored runbook metadata says the artifact requires `deployment-checklist` and `monitoring-setup`, but it also declares those same peer artifacts as outputs in `dependencies.enables` and `relationships.informs` while `creation_order` is 3. That makes the artifact graph internally contradictory: automation reading `enables` can treat earlier deploy artifacts as newly unlocked after runbook creation even though the runbook itself depends on them.
Suggested fix: Remove backward `enables` and downstream relationship claims for earlier deploy artifacts, or otherwise rewrite the runbook/checklist/monitoring metadata so the dependency graph and creation order are acyclic and consistent.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — Options: --agent Agent harness to use: codex, claude, ddx-agent, embedded, or any harness from \ddx agent list\ (default: ${agent}) --claude Shorthand for --agent claude --codex Shorthand for --agent codex --ddx-agent Shorthand for --agent ddx-agent --embedded Shorthand for --agent embedded --forge Deprecated alias for the embedded DDx agent harness --model Explicit model override to pass through to DDx / the harness --effort Reasoning effort: low, medium, high, max (defaults vary by action) --review-every Run alignment every N completed implementation cycles --max-cycles Stop after N completed implementation cycles --no-auto-align Do not auto-run alignment when check returns ALIGN --no-auto-review Do not run fresh-eyes review after each implementation --review-threshold N Skip review for changes below N lines (default: 100) --review-agent Use a different agent for reviews (default: adversarial alternate) --dry-run Print the agent command instead of executing it --quiet, -q Suppress progress output (command echo, prompt sizes) --summary, -s Concise output with log-file line pointers (implies --quiet)</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:1155
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: The new generic `HELIX_MODEL` / `--model` surface is forwarded unconditionally to DDx-managed execution in `run_execute_bead` and `run_execute_loop_once` after `managed_build_agent` may switch the harness to the auto-selected alternate agent. In the default codex-primary / claude-alternate setup, `env HELIX_AGENT=codex HELIX_ALT_AGENT=claude HELIX_MODEL=gpt-5.4 bash scripts/helix build --dry-run ...` now emits `--harness claude --model gpt-5.4`, and `helix run --dry-run` emits the same cross-harness pair. That recreates exactly the concrete-model-policy coupling FEAT-011 / CONTRACT-001 / TD-011 were trying to remove and can make the new public override surface fail as soon as adversarial rotation is active.
Suggested fix: Only pass an explicit model override to DDx-managed execution when the execution harness matches the requested primary harness, or otherwise disable/override adversarial rotation when `--model` is supplied. Add deterministic CLI coverage so dry-run output never combines a codex-specific model override with `--harness claude` (or the inverse).
