acceptance:
1. BenchmarkSearch_100k seeds 100k records and fails if median latency ≥1s.
2. BenchmarkPipelineView_25Reps fails if response time ≥500ms.
3. BenchmarkCSVImport_1000Rows fails if import takes ≥5s.
4. TestCoverageGate (or scripts/check-coverage.sh invoked from lefthook) fails the build when overall handler coverage <80% or when auth/RBAC/cross-workspace packages are <100%.
5. `go test ./... -bench=. -benchtime=1x -run=^$` passes locally.
6. `lefthook run pre-push` enforces the coverage gate and passes.
description:
PROBLEM: Performance SLOs (search <1s @ 100k records, pipeline <500ms for 25 reps, import 1000 rows <5s) and coverage targets (≥80% handlers, 100% on auth/RBAC/isolation critical paths) are unenforced, so regressions ship silently.

ROOT CAUSE: No Go benchmark functions exist for search, pipeline, or import paths in internal/api/handlers/; lefthook.yml has no coverage threshold gate; CI does not parse `go test -coverprofile` output against critical-path thresholds.

PROPOSED FIX: Add Go benchmarks (BenchmarkSearch_100k, BenchmarkPipelineView_25Reps, BenchmarkCSVImport_1000Rows) that seed fixtures and assert wall-clock budgets via b.Elapsed(). Add scripts/check-coverage.sh that parses `go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...` and fails if overall handler coverage <80% or if auth/RBAC/isolation packages <100%. Wire the script into lefthook.yml pre-push.

NON-SCOPE: Adding new unit/integration/E2E tests (covered by 010a-010d); changing handler implementations; tuning queries to meet SLOs (separate optimization work if benchmarks fail).

AC:
1. BenchmarkSearch_100k seeds 100k records and fails if median latency ≥1s.
2. BenchmarkPipelineView_25Reps fails if response time ≥500ms.
3. BenchmarkCSVImport_1000Rows fails if import takes ≥5s.
4. TestCoverageGate (or scripts/check-coverage.sh invoked from lefthook) fails the build when overall handler coverage <80% or when auth/RBAC/cross-workspace packages are <100%.
5. `go test ./... -bench=. -benchtime=1x -run=^$` passes locally.
6. `lefthook run pre-push` enforces the coverage gate and passes.

title: CRM-IMPL-010e: Performance benchmarks and coverage gating

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/validate_context_digests.py:24
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new validator accepts any open HELIX bead whose description starts with `Explicit omission rationale:`, even though the FEAT-006 contract only permits that form when a governing workflow explicitly allows digest omission. A temp tracker containing a generic open build bead with `description="Explicit omission rationale: skipped for convenience"` now exits 0 under `python3 scripts/validate_context_digests.py --tracker <tempfile>`, and a repo-wide search finds no workflow/action that currently grants that exception. This turns the validator into a bypass: a bead can silently dodge the digest requirement by writing the magic prefix, so missing-digest regressions can pass the gate instead of being surfaced.
Suggested fix: Make omission-rationale acceptance conditional on explicit workflow authorization that the validator can verify deterministically (for example a dedicated label/field set by the allowed creation paths), and add a negative fixture proving a generic HELIX bead with the prefix still fails validation.

The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

acceptance: Every skills/helix-*/SKILL.md has a methodology body that does not reference 'ddx', 'bead', '.helix/', or 'spec-id' in normative prose. Substrate-specific invocations live below a clearly-marked 'Running with DDx' (or equivalent) section. A new reader can understand what each skill does without knowing what DDx is. Existing DDx-based workflows still operate — the substrate appendices preserve current invocations.
description:
HELIX skills today are heavily DDx-flavored. Almost every skill in skills/helix-*/SKILL.md invokes substrate-specific tooling: helix-input wraps 'ddx bead create'; helix-triage builds 'ddx bead create' flag invocations; helix-run, helix-build, helix-check wrap 'ddx agent run' and 'ddx agent execute-loop'; most reference the .helix/ tracker workspace, phase:build labels, and spec-id custom fields. The methodology (what to do, why, how to decide) is intertwined with the queue-control surface (how to invoke a specific tracker).

This blocks the portability vision: HELIX is meant to work as a methodology + artifact templates outside the DDx tracker (e.g., a Databricks installation, manual operation, alternative substrates). Skills should describe intent and methodology; substrate invocations belong in adapters or appendices.

Scope: per skill in skills/helix-*/SKILL.md:
1. Rewrite the main body to describe what the skill *does* in methodology terms — turn sparse intent into structured work items with these labels; identify the affected governed artifact; execute one bounded slice; etc. Use substrate-neutral vocabulary: 'work item' rather than 'bead', 'tracker' rather than 'DDx tracker'.
2. Move every substrate-specific command line into a 'Running with DDx' appendix at the bottom of the skill, preserving today's exact invocations so existing operator habits keep working.
3. Where a skill currently exists primarily to wrap a DDx invocation (helix-triage in particular), make the methodology core thin but real — the skill should still teach the right thinking even if the operator runs it without DDx.

This bead depends on helix-ada6e67e (α) and helix-f6332f6c (β) so the artifact schema and template shapes are stable before skills get reworked.
notes: Promoted: portable skill language is core product work, not packaging polish.
title: Refactor HELIX skills to substrate-neutral language; substrate-specific invocations in appendices

<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-007 — HELIX needs a public-facing documentation site and scripted demo reels that show users what HELIX does, how it works, and how to get started. The microsite is the primary onboarding surface — it must take a new user from "what is this?" to running helix run on their own project.</governing>
</context-digest>

<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<governing>pages — for demo in quickstart concerns evolve experiment; do echo "::group::Recording $demo" dir=$(mktemp -d) cast="website/static/demos/helix-${demo}.cast" recorded_cast="${dir}/helix-${demo}.cast" (cd "$dir" && asciinema rec \ --cols 100 --rows 30 \ --command "HELIX_DEMO_RECORDING=1 bash ${GITHUB_WORKSPACE}/docs/demos/helix-${demo}/demo.sh" \ "$recorded_cast" 2>&1) test -s "$recorded_cast" cp -f "$recorded_cast" "$cast" echo "✓ $demo recorded ($(wc -c < "$cast") bytes)" echo "::endgroup::" done</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .github/workflows/pages.yml:79
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 5b284c8 removed the warning fallback, but the new implementation still does not fail the Pages workflow when a shipped demo command exits non-zero. Local reproduction shows 'asciinema rec --command "bash -lc '\''exit 17'\''"' returns exit code 0 and writes a non-empty cast, so the current step's '(...)', 'test -s', and 'cp -f' sequence still succeeds and copies a cast from a failed demo run.
Suggested fix: Capture the wrapped demo command's exit status explicitly and assert it before accepting the cast, or add a verification mechanism that proves each shipped demo completed successfully before the Pages job continues.

Create the Docker test scenario for GitHub Copilot CLI. Layout:
- `tests/install/copilot-cli/Dockerfile` — minimal base image with the runtime CLI installed.
- `tests/install/copilot-cli/install.sh` — runs: `(copy `.github/copilot-instructions.md` into the working dir of a clone)`
- `tests/install/copilot-cli/verify.sh` — runs `tests/install/shared/verify-skill-layout.sh` against the installed location, plus a functional check gated by `TEST_FUNCTIONAL=1` that invokes the runtime non-interactively and asserts the response names modes from `expected-modes.txt`.
- `tests/install/copilot-cli/record.tape` — vhs script that records the install + verify sequence to `tests/install/copilot-cli/recording.gif`.

Notes: gh CLI with Copilot extension installed; GH_TOKEN with Copilot license; functional check via `gh copilot suggest` gated by TEST_FUNCTIONAL=1.

acceptance:
1. CRUD endpoints implemented for Contact: POST /contacts (create), GET /contacts (list), GET /contacts/:id (read), PUT /contacts/:id (update), DELETE /contacts/:id (soft-delete).
2. CRUD endpoints for Account, Lead, Opportunity following same pattern.
3. List endpoints return paginated results (default 20, max 100 per page); accept limit + offset.
4. Create endpoints validate required fields (name, email for contacts; name for accounts, etc.) and return 422 on validation error.
5. Update endpoints handle partial updates (PATCH or selective PUT); return updated entity.
6. Delete endpoints soft-delete (set is_deleted = true); queries (list, get) filter WHERE is_deleted = false by default.
7. Create/update set audit fields: created_at/created_by on create; updated_at/updated_by on any change.
8. All endpoints scoped to workspace_id (via middleware); cross-workspace queries fail.
9. Permission checks enforced: rep can CRUD own records + read manager/team records (not modify); manager can CRUD team records; admin can CRUD all.
10. Response includes created_at, updated_at, created_by, updated_by (for audit trail visibility).
11. Duplicate email/account name in same workspace returns 409 Conflict.
description: Implement all CRUD endpoints for contacts, accounts, leads, opportunities. Requires: database schema, auth, API contract. Acceptance: all PRD R-2 requirements met; CRUD operations respect role-based permissions; soft-delete respected in queries.
title: CRM-IMPL-003: Core CRUD operations (contacts, accounts, leads, opportunities)

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/04-build/implementation-plan.md:82
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The Live Queue Snapshot section says the snapshot was captured after claiming `helix-62559453` and that there was `1` in-progress open bead (`helix-62559453`). But the backlog tables immediately below enumerate 22 issue IDs that exactly match the current post-close open queue and do not include `helix-62559453` at all. The section therefore mixes two different tracker states: a snapshot that still counts the refresh bead as open, and a backlog listing that already excludes it. Because this document is meant to steer implementation ordering from live tracker state, that mismatch can send later runs down the wrong queue or make the counts impossible to audit against `ddx bead status` / `ddx bead list --status open --json`.
Suggested fix: Rebuild the Live Queue Snapshot and backlog tables from one tracker moment and make them agree. If `helix-62559453` is part of the counted open queue, include it or reduce the backlog table count accordingly; otherwise rewrite the prose/counts to the post-close queue state and remove the in-progress claim.

acceptance: tests/helix-cli.sh test test_supervisory_injection_stamps_provenance asserts that when HELIX injects a review-finding bead, the resulting bead has all four provenance fields (labels + three custom fields) AND the fields survive across a helix stop / helix start cycle. Second test test_helix_status_surfaces_injection_causality asserts helix status --json injected_supervisory[0] contains {id, type, triggering_cycle, triggering_bead, trigger_source, remediation} with none null. Without these provenance fields, HELIX may not close the bead.
description:
When HELIX injects a review-finding or alignment-review bead into the queue (the supervisory-bead model per CONTRACT-001 'Queue-Injected Supervisory Beads'), stamp structured provenance on the injected bead itself — not just in a log line. Provenance must support operator causality reconstruction without reading freeform logs.

Provenance fields (stamped via ddx bead create --labels and --set at injection time):
- labels include 'helix-injected:review-finding' or 'helix-injected:alignment-review'
- --set helix-trigger-source=<enum: post-cycle-review | periodic-alignment | post-epic-close | acceptance-failure>
- --set helix-trigger-cycle=<int>
- --set helix-trigger-bead=<bead-id of the execution bead whose close triggered injection, or 'queue-empty' for drain-triggered>
- --set helix-remediation=<short string: e.g. 'verify-governance-diff' | 're-run-with-explicit-scope'>

Round-trip proof: given any bead with a 'helix-injected:*' label, an operator can run 'ddx bead show <id> --json' and extract all four provenance fields; and 'helix status --json' (bead helix-9044bc6c's surface) shows these injected beads grouped by trigger-source in the injected_supervisory array, with enough detail to answer 'why did this bead appear?'

Provenance must survive a helix stop / helix start cycle: test asserts fields persist in the beads.jsonl after the process that created them has exited.

title: Supervisory-bead injection with causality stamped on bead metadata and round-trip proof

acceptance: HELIX design/docs define an integration contract where HELIX selects stage tier and harness constraints while DDx resolves the concrete model via ddx-agent catalog policy; compatibility for existing HELIX smart/cheap overrides is specified; scripts/helix and tests/helix-cli.sh implementation steps are identified without leaving hardcoded model-version policy in HELIX.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Hugo version: 0.159.2 extended — pinned in .github/workflows/pages.yml · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading) · Agent harness: ddx agent run --harness claude — not claude -p</practices>
<governing>workflows/EXECUTION.md — HELIX design/docs define an integration contract where HELIX selects stage tier and harness constraints while DDx resolves the concrete model via ddx-agent catalog policy</governing>
<evidence>
- scripts/helix currently hardcodes concrete smart/cheap model names in resolve_model(): claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-6, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini.
- HELIX docs and help text expose --smart-model / --cheap-model and describe concrete defaults in ways that duplicate DDx policy.
- The desired behavior is: HELIX decides whether a stage needs smart/fast/cheap and which harness family to use; DDx resolves the concrete current model through ddx-agent catalog policy under user constraints.
- This removes split-brain model policy and avoids HELIX drifting behind current recommended models when ddx-agent catalog data changes.
</evidence>
</context-digest>

Design how HELIX should consume DDx tier/model policy backed by ddx-agent's shared model catalog instead of hardcoding concrete model names.

Scope:
- Define the integration contract between HELIX and DDx for tier-based model selection.
- Decide whether HELIX should pass tier names, family aliases, harness constraints, or another structured request to DDx.
- Identify compatibility behavior for existing HELIX_SMART_MODEL / HELIX_CHEAP_MODEL overrides and existing CLI flags.
- Update HELIX execution contract/docs so supervisory stage selection remains in HELIX while concrete model policy is delegated downward through DDx to ddx-agent catalog data.
- Outline the implementation/test plan for scripts/helix and tests/helix-cli.sh.

Notes:
- Keep the responsibility split crisp: HELIX owns stage policy (review/design/align vs build/check/polish), DDx owns concrete harness/model resolution, ddx-agent owns reusable model catalog data.
- The resulting design should avoid reintroducing hardcoded codex/claude model version strings in HELIX.

description:
PROBLEM: The rebuilt bundle from B7a must be uploaded to the Databricks Genie workspace so the live e2e in B7c can observe the three new artifact types through the real catalog.

ROOT CAUSE: scripts/install-genie.py:1 uploads bundle artifacts to the workspace Genie space using current cookies/credentials; without this step the workspace continues to serve the pre-B6 catalog and any Playwright drive would falsely report the new types as absent.

PROPOSED FIX: After B7a succeeds, run python scripts/install-genie.py against the workspace; assert exit 0; capture the upload manifest (file list with paths under 01-frame, 02-design, 03-test) for inclusion in the B7c sidecar.

NON-SCOPE: rebuilding the bundle (B7a); driving Playwright prompts (B7c); rotating workspace credentials; substituting non-Databricks platforms (UC, SDP EXPECT, DBU stay Databricks-specific per §Role Boundary); modifying SKILL.md.

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/validate_execution_ready_beads.py:59
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new validator's execution-ready predicate does not match the canonical queue surface. On the live tracker, running python3 scripts/validate_execution_ready_beads.py .ddx/beads.jsonl inspects 193 beads, including 169 closed beads, while ddx bead ready --execution --json reports 28 execution-ready beads. Because the helper ignores status and queue-readiness semantics, its default path produces noisy failures against historical tracker state instead of validating the same bead set HELIX would actually execute.
Suggested fix: Derive the candidate bead set from ddx bead ready --execution --json or replicate its readiness filters exactly before applying acceptance validation, and add a deterministic fixture that proves closed, deferred, and otherwise non-ready beads are excluded.

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix-bec526ad</governing>
</context-digest>

acceptance:
(Supersedes the "grep response" step in the description.)
1. `bash tests/workflows/copilot-cli/run-scenarios.sh` exits 0 with GITHUB_TOKEN set.
2. Activation assertion: the harness asserts on the strongest structured signal `gh copilot` exposes (exit code, structured output, or a documented invocation marker). gh copilot is shell-command-oriented and may expose no skill-invocation event; if so, the harness README explicitly documents this limitation.
3. Negative control (required even where the signal is weak): the same scenario with HELIX content/skill absent exits NONZERO, so the test is not a vacuous prose grep.
4. tests/workflows/copilot-cli/recordings/INT-CP.gif generated via vhs and committed. docs/install/copilot.md links it.
5. Copilot response-shape difference and any activation-signal limitation documented in the harness README. (No routing.jsonl required: gh copilot is not a chat-router runtime.)
description:
Create tests/workflows/copilot-cli/ harness that exercises `gh copilot suggest` against operator prompts that should engage the HELIX skill.

**Caveat**: GitHub Copilot CLI's `gh copilot suggest` is shell-command-oriented, not free-form chat. The "skill activation" assertion is different from chat runtimes — we test that Copilot, given a HELIX-aware prompt, recognizes and names HELIX modes in its response, rather than generating bespoke shell.

**Deliverables**:
- run-scenarios.sh + 3 scenario prompts (install-verify, skill-list, bootstrap-style "explain how HELIX would frame X")
- expected/ assertion text
- vhs recording → INT-CP.gif

**Harness shape** (reuse `tests/install/copilot-cli/Dockerfile`):
1. Build image with `gh` + Copilot extension
2. For each scenario: `gh copilot suggest -t shell "<prompt>"` (or `-t gh` depending on which `-t` flag gives free-form output in the current Copilot version)
3. Grep response

**Risk**: docs/install/copilot.md:121-127 says `gh copilot` GA was January 2026. If the current Copilot version's surface has changed since then, the harness will need adaptation.

<context-digest>
<principles>Validate Your Work · Runtime Neutrality · Design for Simplicity</principles>
<concerns>installer-coverage | screencast-evidence | runtime-parity</concerns>
<practices>vhs records the Docker run · gh copilot has a different response shape than chat-based runtimes; assertion is "names HELIX modes when asked" rather than "produces artifacts" · GITHUB_TOKEN required</practices>
<governing>docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-014-helix-workflow-coverage.md SCN-01 (bootstrap) + SCN-06 (review) · docs/install/copilot-cli.md · existing tests/install/copilot-cli/ scaffolding</governing>
</context-digest>
title: INT-CP: GitHub Copilot CLI integration test — install + skill activation + bootstrap scenario

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>41ee718be305dc96c2a9d938ae6d317c0ce3adc5</governing>
</context-digest>

ROOT CAUSE: docs/helix/03-design/wireframes/csv/ directory is absent; CSV import and export are required by docs/helix/02-frame/prd/crm.prd.md:1 (CSV section) but never produced.

PROPOSED FIX: Author wireframes under docs/helix/03-design/wireframes/csv/ for import.md (file upload, column mapping, 10-row preview, confirmation, progress bar, success/error summary) and export.md (record type select, optional filter, download button). description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:39 and .ddx/beads.jsonl:60
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Implementation commit 80acf3d95d0818ad367d5bf4df112f812cfb9bb2 writes new <measure-results> blocks on helix-4f9883af and helix-7171e7a1 with timestamps 2026-04-10T12:40:00Z and 2026-04-10T12:42:00Z, but the commit itself is dated 2026-04-10T04:33:55-04:00 (08:33:55Z) and the affected rows have updated_at values 08:31:42Z and 08:35:02Z. The new notes therefore claim UTC times that are about four hours in the future, which misorders tracker evidence and breaks the measure.md contract that <timestamp> records the actual measurement time in UTC.
Suggested fix: Generate <measure-results><timestamp> values in real UTC (or omit the Z suffix when writing local time), then correct the two affected bead notes so their timestamps are chronologically consistent with the commit and updated_at metadata.

acceptance: skills/helix/SKILL.md contains a 'validate' route in the Routing Rules table and a Validate section under Workflow Contracts. The contract names: artifact-type resolution; template+prompt+meta.yml load; structural and prompt-section conformance checks; finding classification; produce updates. Skill body remains runtime-neutral (no ddx/helix CLI commands in normative body). A grep for 'validate' in skills/helix/SKILL.md finds the new section.
description:
skills/helix/SKILL.md currently has align (drift/gaps), evolve (thread changes), and check (next-action). It lacks an explicit mode for validating an artifact instance against its own template's prompt and structural shape — the dogfood loop described in PRD R-3 (alignment skill output) and R-6 (self-application).

Add a validate route to the routing table and a corresponding contract. Contract shape (mirroring the existing route contracts):

1. Load the artifact instance and resolve its artifact type (via ddx: frontmatter when present, else by path/filename pattern).
2. Load the template.md, prompt.md, and meta.yml for that artifact type from workflows/phases/*/artifacts/<type>/.
3. Compare instance to template structurally (required section headings present, required frontmatter fields populated) and compare instance content to prompt.md guidance (each prompt section answered or explicitly marked N/A).
4. Emit findings keyed to template/prompt sections, classified as ALIGNED, INCOMPLETE, UNDERSPECIFIED, or STALE per existing align taxonomy.
5. Produce updates (apply directly when the user invoked validate to fix, or surface a plan when they invoked it to audit).

Add 'validate' to the routing table with intent 'Check an artifact instance against its template and prompt; improve in place'. Keep the contract body under ~25 lines, matching the density of existing routes.

title: Add template-conformance validate mode to HELIX routing skill

description:
Author docs/install/codex.md covering two distinct Codex surfaces and naming the difference clearly:

A) OpenAI Codex CLI (the newer agent CLI): the skill body and workflows catalog drop into the repo as referenced markdown; an AGENTS.md entry points Codex at the skill; invocation is natural language inside a Codex session.

B) GitHub Copilot Workspace / Codex extension: a more chat-style prompt-loading model; document how to load skills/helix/SKILL.md into the workspace prompt context or via Copilot's instruction-file mechanism. Likely lighter-touch than the CLI install.

Both surfaces share the same source content (skills/helix/ + workflows/). The doc must make clear that HELIX is not Codex-specific; the differences are purely in how each surface discovers and invokes a skill.

Sections:
1. Which Codex am I using? (decision matrix: CLI vs extension)
2. Install A — Codex CLI: file layout, AGENTS.md entry, verify snippet.
3. Install B — Codex extension / Copilot: prompt loading, repo-instruction file location, verify snippet.
4. Shared content reference: workflows/ catalog discovery rules.
5. Caveats: any feature parity gaps between the two surfaces.

acceptance:
1. With a malformed tracker, HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT=1 DDX_BEAD_DIR=<temp> bash scripts/helix build --dry-run <selector> exits non-zero and surfaces the tracker/resolution failure instead of only printing no execution-ready bead matched the build selector.
2. The same malformed-tracker scenario for HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT=1 DDX_BEAD_DIR=<temp> bash scripts/helix build <selector> exits non-zero and surfaces the tracker/resolution failure instead of only printing no execution-ready bead matched the build selector.
3. tests/helix-cli.sh adds deterministic regression coverage that distinguishes resolver failure from a genuine selector miss.
4. git diff --check passes.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — implementation_prompt() { local selector="${1:-}" local context_file="${target_root}/.helix/context.md" cat <<EOF $(if [[ -f "$context_file" ]]; then cat "$context_file"; printf '\n---\n\n'; fi) Execute the following HELIX implementation action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/implementation.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/phases/04-build/enforcer.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead ready --json\, \ddx bead show <id>\, \ddx bead update <id> --claim\, \ddx bead close <id>\. Concurrency rule: re-read the selected issue immediately before claim and immediately before close. If tracker metadata drifted materially, do not claim or close from stale assumptions; re-check instead. ${selector:+Implementation target: ${selector}.} CRITICAL: If tests fail after your changes, fix them. 99% of test failures are caused by your changes, not by pre-existing breakage. Do not blame external causes, do not skip tests, do not commit with failing tests. Fix the code or fix the test. Retry up to 3 times before giving up. Never commit broken work. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. Session log: ${helix_log} EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:2320
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 2d2715d changes the explicit-selector managed-execution path to run `resolve_execution_target "$selector" 2>/dev/null || true` before both dry-run and real execution. That swallows resolver failures from `tracker_ready --json --execution` or `ddx jq` and then misreports the outcome as `helix: no execution-ready bead matched the build selector`. Reproduction from this review: create a temp `.ddx/beads.jsonl` containing malformed JSON, then run `HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT=1 DDX_BEAD_DIR=<temp> bash scripts/helix build --dry-run hx-bad` or `... build hx-bad`; both exit 1 with the selector-miss message instead of surfacing the tracker parse failure.
Suggested fix: Preserve real resolver failures for explicit selectors by distinguishing "no match" from "resolution failed". Only emit the selector-miss summary when resolution succeeded and returned no issue, and add deterministic coverage for malformed-tracker or resolver-failure cases on both dry-run and real execution paths.
title: integration: preserve tracker errors when explicit build selector resolution fails

acceptance: No normative file references .helix/issues.jsonl or beads.jsonl. All tracker path references say .ddx/beads.jsonl. Test fixtures write to .ddx/beads.jsonl. bash tests/helix-cli.sh passes. grep confirms zero stale path refs.
title: Align all .helix/issues.jsonl references to .ddx/beads.jsonl in normative docs and tests

acceptance: 1) helix status --json emits a single object with required fields: current_cycle (int), running_bead (object or null), parked_beads (array with {id, reason, next_eligible, age_seconds}), injected_supervisory (array with {id, type, triggering_cycle, triggering_bead}), last_stop_reason (string); jq -e verifies none are missing. 2) helix status completes in under 200ms on a tracker with 100 open beads (measured via time; test fixture seeds the tracker). 3) tests/helix-cli.sh adds a regression where launching a second 'helix run' while the first holds the lock exits 2 with the lock-holder message within 1 second, and does not mutate the tracker. 4) .ddx/run-state.json (or documented sibling) contains the four migration counters and increments them across a scripted run.
description:
Consolidate helix status into a single surface answering: (a) current cycle number, (b) currently-executing bead id and claim age, (c) parked beads classified by reason (dep-blocked, retry-parked with next-eligible-time, orphaned-claim), (d) recently-injected supervisory beads (review-finding, alignment-review) with triggering cycle and triggering bead, (e) stop reason of the last run. Implementation is HELIX-side: scripts/helix composes existing DDx JSON primitives (ddx bead list --json, ddx bead blocked --json, ddx agent workers --json, execute-loop --json event log tail). No new ddx subcommand.

Parallel-run mutex: second 'helix run' on the same project root must detect an active run (via DDX_PROJECT_ROOT-scoped advisory lock or existing .helix-run.pid) and return exit code 2 with stderr 'helix run: already running (pid=<N>, started=<ts>)' BEFORE claiming any bead.

Migration observability counters (written to .ddx/run-state.json or a sibling file on each helix run): ddx_delegation_success_count, ddx_delegation_failure_count with reason classes (missing_binary, too_old, malformed_json, timeout), parallel_run_denied_count, supervisory_injection_count by type.

<context-digest>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>PLAN-2026-06-11-FLOW-ARCHITECTURE</governing>
</context-digest>

Treat T23 as retiring env override, T08 as historical/deferred helix-web, T20/T21 as data-skill workaround/malformed to review under one public helix skill, T27 as legacy cross-methodology/sibling-flow unless rewritten with a real current cross-scope mechanism, and T29 as the external-edge required=false invariant. CI-* bench rows are strict-link/scope-instance examples, not canonical user marker examples. RC-01 is legacy methodologies compatibility; RC-02 is flows rename compatibility, not helix_version:2. C007/C009/C010/C021/C022/C014 and routing-evals, routing-evals-smoke, routing-evals-failures rows with sibling/env semantics must be marked legacy/expected-failing or migrated with schema/golden changes. - `verify-sections.sh <artifact> <expected-anchors>` — assert every `^## ` heading in expected-anchors is present in artifact.
- `verify-frontmatter.sh <artifact> <expected-fields>` — YAML frontmatter parses; every field in expected-fields is present with documented value/shape.

- `verify-no-orphan-files.sh <root> <allowed-paths>` — no files outside the allowed-paths glob set.

Each exits 0 on pass, 1 on fail with single-line diagnostic. Reuses the python yaml + regex pattern from tests/install/shared/verify-skill-layout.sh.

<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix.workflow — HELIX enforces Test-Driven Development (TDD) through a structured phase approach where tests are written BEFORE implementation. This ensures quality is built-in from the start and specifications are executable. Human creativity and AI capabilities collaborate throughout, with tests serving as the contract between design and implementation.</governing>
</context-digest>

acceptance:
AC1 TestAlignRunnerInjectsBead — after a cycle completes, Runner.Run calls tracker.Inject with kind=alignment-review, correct parent and payload fields, and does not invoke the legacy hook writer.
AC2 TestNoAutoAlignFlagSuppressesInjection — invoking `helix align --no-auto-align` (and setting helix.align.auto_inject=false) causes Runner.Run to skip injection and return nil; tracker has no new alignment-review bead afterward.
AC3 TestExecuteLoopConsumesAlignmentReview — end-to-end: parent bead completes, alignment-review bead is injected, execute-loop pops it on the next tick, runs the alignment handler, and writes the evidence bundle to the canonical path.
AC4 TestDeprecatedHookKeyWarns — setting only legacy hooks.auto_align still works but emits a deprecation warning naming the new key.
AC5 Gates — `go test ./internal/helix/align/... ./cmd/helix/...` passes; `lefthook run pre-commit` passes; `go vet ./...` clean.
description:
PROBLEM: HELIX `align` currently runs the alignment engine inline as a post-cycle hook; like review, this bypasses the ready queue and produces non-standard output. Operators have no first-class flag to disable the auto-injection independently of the hook system.

ROOT CAUSE: internal/helix/align/runner.go:1 — Runner.Run invokes the alignment engine synchronously after cycle completion and writes reconciliation artifacts directly rather than calling tracker.Inject. cmd/helix/align.go:1 — CLI has no `--no-auto-align` flag; the only switch is hooks.auto_align under the hook subsystem.

PROPOSED FIX: (a) Replace the post-cycle hook invocation with `tracker.Inject(ctx, parent, BeadKindAlignmentReview, payload)` using the shared API from child 1; payload carries parent bead id, upstream artifact references (PRD/spec ids), and divergence summary fingerprint. (b) Add `--no-auto-align` flag to `cmd/helix/align.go` and equivalent `helix.align.auto_inject` config key (default true); when false, Runner.Run returns without injecting. (c) Delete the old hook-output writer path once injection is wired; keep `hooks.auto_align` only as a deprecated alias.

NON-SCOPE: Review path (covered by child 2). Tracker bead-kind plumbing (covered by child 1). Changes to the divergence-detection algorithm itself. Inherits all parent non-scope items. Governing artifact: parent helix-e26584d7. Depends on: child 1 (bead kind + Inject API + execute-loop dispatcher).

acceptance: In an automated run-loop cycle that creates a tracker-closure sync commit after implementation, the post-implementation review threshold and review scope still inspect the implementation diff rather than the closure-sync diff; deterministic tests cover the commit-selection behavior and pass.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:2936
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: In the automated run loop, post-implementation review decides whether to run and what to review from `git diff HEAD~1`, then invokes `run_review last-commit`. In this repository the most recent commit after the implementation cycle is `91860b8` (`helix-c0d6bdf3 sync tracker closure`), which changes only `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. The actual implementation diff is one commit earlier at `d6da2d5`. This means the auto-review threshold can undercount the work as a tiny tracker-only change and skip review, or review only bookkeeping instead of the implementation that just ran. The action contract at `workflows/actions/fresh-eyes-review.md:39` and the operator docs at `workflows/EXECUTION.md:604` reinforce the same incorrect `last-commit` assumption for this in-loop context.
Suggested fix: Thread the executed issue ID or implementation commit SHA through the run loop and make auto-review inspect that implementation diff instead of raw HEAD~1 when a tracker close sync commit exists. Update the fresh-eyes review action and execution docs to describe the in-loop behavior explicitly, and add deterministic coverage showing review still targets the implementation commit after a tracker-closure sync commit.
title: integration: review last-commit should target implementation diff, not tracker closure sync

acceptance: An updated HELIX skill bundle (or SKILL.md body, or build script) is in place such that re-running the Playwright e2e against a fresh Genie Code install causes prompts 2 and 3 to pass per the criteria in tests/install/genie/test-procedure.md; new recording captured and stored alongside the 2026-05-16 attempt in tests/install/genie/recordings/. If the remedy requires upstream Databricks changes, the bead documents the workaround HELIX users should apply until then.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>genie-no-chat-api | agentskills-spec | multi-runtime-distribution</concerns>
<practices>Genie Code skills are auto-discovered at /Workspace/.assistant/skills/<name>/ · agentskills.io spec recommends references/ for heavy content (progressive disclosure) · Skill body must direct the runtime to catalog paths it can actually read · Captured evidence: tests/install/genie/recordings/2026-05-16-verify.webm + screenshots</practices>
<governing>FEAT-013 GENIE-01..03 · TD-013 · agentskills-spec.md · 2026-05-16 e2e recording</governing>
</context-digest>

## Observation

During the FEAT-013 Genie e2e verification (2026-05-16), prompt 1 (list modes) passed cleanly — Genie loaded the skill at `/Workspace/.assistant/skills/helix/SKILL.md`, returned the routing table with all expected modes, and cited `§Frame`, `§Align`, `§Validate`, `§Evolve`, `§Design`, `§Backfill`. The skill is loaded.

Prompt 2 (catalog reachable: 'list artifact types defined under activity 01-frame, show the path of each artifact-type directory') failed. Genie attempted to read folders named `datahelix`, `.helix`, `templates` in the user's workspace filesystem — NOT in the skill bundle at `/Workspace/.assistant/skills/helix/references/activities/01-frame/`. The bundle was confirmed present and intact (scripts/verify-genie.py all 14 checks pass).

## Implication

Genie Code does not appear to treat SKILL.md's parent directory as an automatically-mounted content root for the skill. The agentskills.io progressive-disclosure pattern (heavy content under `references/`) requires the runtime to resolve relative paths from the skill body to bundle-local files; Genie either lacks this behavior or requires the skill body to use a specific tool-call syntax to fetch bundle resources.

## Evidence

- Recording: `tests/install/genie/recordings/2026-05-16-verify.webm`
- Screenshots: `tests/install/genie/recordings/screenshots/`
- Metadata: `tests/install/genie/recordings/2026-05-16-verify.json`
- Successful skill load proven by prompt-1 response
- Catalog presence proven by `python scripts/verify-genie.py` (14/14 pass)

## Possible remedies (investigate)

1. Update `skills/helix/SKILL.md` body to explicitly state the catalog lives at `references/activities/` relative to SKILL.md and to call out the agentskills.io progressive-disclosure model. Cost: small SKILL.md edit; risk: may not be sufficient if Genie doesn't auto-resolve bundle-relative paths.
2. Configure the bundle to materialize the catalog at a workspace path Genie naturally inspects (e.g. mirror to a user-visible Repos-backed dir). Cost: build-script complexity; risk: couples HELIX to Databricks Repos conventions.
3. Use Genie's `@<filename>` mention syntax to explicitly provide catalog files as context per prompt. Cost: pollutes every prompt; doesn't scale.
4. Inline a minimal artifact-type index into SKILL.md so common queries (list artifact types under activity X) succeed without filesystem traversal. Heavier templates would still require a tool call. Cost: bloats SKILL.md.
5. File on Databricks: request that Genie Code mount the skill directory as a readable root when invoking the skill. Out of HELIX's control but lowest long-term cost.

## Out of scope

Does not affect Claude Code, Codex CLI, Copilot, or DDx — those runtimes either share the source filesystem with the user (filesystem-based discovery) or load the skill from a plugin path they can read sibling directories of. The bug is specific to Genie's skill-bundle isolation model.
notes:
RESOLVED 2026-05-17. Remedy #4 (inline §Catalog Resolution table)
unblocks Genie when measured against a clean account.

Clean-instruction e2e run captured at
tests/install/genie/recordings/2026-05-17-verify-clean.{webm,json}:
  prompt 1 (list-modes)        idle  18.1s  5461 chars  PASS
  prompt 2 (catalog-reachable) idle  19.0s  7494 chars  PASS
  prompt 3 (smoke-routing)     idle 157.2s 14581 chars  PASS

vs confounded v041 run on the same SKILL.md:
  prompt 1: 600s timeout, 3200 chars (worked but slow)
  prompt 2: 600s timeout, 3336 chars (filesystem loop)
  prompt 3: 600s timeout, 3502 chars (queued, never processed)

Difference attributable entirely to operator clearing the user-level
Genie Code custom instruction that pointed at /Users/.../datahelix/
and biased path-style lookups.

Prompt 2's response literally quotes 'Per §Catalog Resolution in the
HELIX SKILL.md, activity 01-frame owns 16 artifact types' and lists
all 16 with references/activities/<...> paths — proving the inline
table reaches Genie and answers the query without filesystem
traversal.

Closing this bead.
title: Genie Code can't reach bundled catalog: path-resolution behavior treats user workspace as content root, not the SKILL.md parent

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

description:
Create tests/workflows/genie/ harness that drives the Databricks Genie Code chat surface via Playwright, exercising the HELIX skill in the Workspace.

**Key difference from other runtimes**: Genie has no chat API and no CLI. Integration test is a Playwright script that:
1. Launches headless Chromium
2. Loads workspace URL with DBAUTH cookie auth
3. Opens Genie Code chat panel
4. Types each scenario prompt + waits for response completion (Stop button heuristic — see prior 2026-05-17 work in tests/install/genie/recordings/)
5. Captures full webm + per-prompt event log

**Deliverables**:
- `tests/workflows/genie/run-scenarios.sh` — Playwright driver (probably calling node + a `playwright.spec.ts` or `genie-driver.py`)
- `tests/workflows/genie/scenarios/install-verify.prompt`, `skill-list.prompt`, `bootstrap.prompt`
- `tests/workflows/genie/expected/<name>.expect`
- `tests/workflows/genie/recordings/INT-GN.webm` + `INT-GN-events.json` (recording IS the screencast)
- Operator-supplied `DATABRICKS_HOST` + `DATABRICKS_WORKSPACE_URL` env + DBAUTH cookie file at a documented path

**Reuse**: tests/install/genie/recordings/ already has 3 Playwright-driven recordings from 2026-05-16/17. Use the same driver pattern.

<context-digest>
<principles>Validate Your Work · Runtime Neutrality · Design for Simplicity</principles>

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix-d2146c63</governing>
</context-digest>

A11y checklist enumerates color contrast, ARIA labels, keyboard navigation; flag MVP-implementation vs spec-only items.
NON-SCOPE: Auth, home/detail views, kanban interactions, individual entity detail wireframes.
GOVERNING ARTIFACTS: docs/helix/01-frame/crm.prd.md, docs/helix/02-design/crm.api-contract.md.

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh:98
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The updated validator extracts the helix-concerns REVIEW_PROMPT body and matches the fixture payload against that extracted text, but the semantic checks for the required labeled ddx command still run against the entire demo.sh file via assert_contains/assert_not_contains. If the same command string appears in a comment, another prompt, or unrelated helper text, bash tests/validate-demo-fixtures.sh can still pass even when the REVIEW_PROMPT itself drops the area-labeled command or retains the stale unlabeled command.
Suggested fix: Bind the expected_command and stale_command assertions to the extracted REVIEW_PROMPT text inside assert_fixture_matches_demo_prompt (or return the extracted prompt and assert against that value) so the validator enforces the reviewed prompt semantics rather than any unrelated occurrence elsewhere in demo.sh.

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: website/content/docs/glossary/artifacts.md:251
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: Commit b148a319 makes `release-notes` a required deploy exit artifact and therefore reinforces the live four-artifact deploy contract (`deployment-checklist`, `monitoring-setup`, `runbook`, `release-notes`). The microsite artifact glossary still says deploy artifacts are created only "as needed" and lists examples rather than the current first-class contract, and it never documents `monitoring-setup` as its own artifact entry at all. Under the active `hugo-hextra` concern, governing artifact changes that are surfaced on the microsite must update the corresponding microsite pages in the same pass; this page still advertises the pre-restoration deploy model.
Suggested fix: Update the Deploy section of `website/content/docs/glossary/artifacts.md` so it describes the current deploy artifact contract explicitly and add a dedicated `monitoring-setup` entry alongside `deployment-checklist`, `runbook`, and `release-notes`.

PROPOSED FIX: Produce wireframes for CSV import (file upload, column mapping, 10-row preview, confirmation, progress bar, success/error summary) and CSV export (record type select, optional filter, download). Deliver under docs/design/wireframes/csv/.

NON-SCOPE: Core view layouts (covered in CRM-DESIGN-006b); generic interaction flows (covered in CRM-DESIGN-006c); responsive/a11y (covered in CRM-DESIGN-006e); authentication views (covered in CRM-DESIGN-006a); backend import worker design and error-handling contract.

Governing artifacts: depends on crm.prd (requirements) and API contract (endpoint details) per parent.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>workflows/actions/reconcile-alignment.md — Your goal is to re-align the implementation with the authoritative planning stack, identify explicit divergence, determine whether additional execution work remains for the reviewed scope, and produce deterministic next steps using the built-in tracker (ddx bead).</governing>
</context-digest>

Review the repo-scope execution wrapper and alignment-governance surfaces against the HELIX authority stack. Focus on governing-bead acquisition, stale-claim reclaim, scope-area seeding, dry-run parity, and related deterministic CLI coverage.

title: Alignment review: repo - execution wrapper and align governance

acceptance: helix run --agent ddx-agent dispatches work via ddx agent run --harness agent. Agent list and model selection uses ddx agent list / ddx agent capabilities instead of hard-coded values. Existing codex and claude agents still work, and --forge remains a compatibility alias.
description: The helix CLI hard-codes agent harness names and model lists. It should instead call ddx agent list and ddx agent capabilities to discover available harnesses and models at runtime. Also add ddx-agent as a supported HELIX surface alongside codex and claude, normalizing to the DDx harness name `agent` and dispatching via `ddx agent run --harness agent`. Keep `--forge` only as a deprecated compatibility alias while the rename propagates. This enables local model execution through ddx-agent without preserving the old forge name in HELIX.
title: Support ddx-agent as a HELIX agent and use ddx agent for discovery instead of hard-coding

acceptance: scripts/helix re-reads live bead status immediately before reclaim updates in ensure_alignment_bead and recover_orphaned_issues; a deterministic tests/helix-cli.sh regression proves a bead closed after the initial snapshot is skipped rather than reclaimed or re-opened
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — implementation_prompt() { local selector="${1:-}" local context_file="${target_root}/.helix/context.md" cat <<EOF $(if [[ -f "$context_file" ]]; then cat "$context_file"; printf '\n---\n\n'; fi) Execute the following HELIX implementation action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/implementation.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/phases/04-build/enforcer.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead ready --json\, \ddx bead show <id>\, \ddx bead update <id> --claim\, \ddx bead close <id>\. Concurrency rule: re-read the selected issue immediately before claim and immediately before close. If tracker metadata drifted materially, do not claim or close from stale assumptions; re-check instead. ${selector:+Implementation target: ${selector}.} CRITICAL: If tests fail after your changes, fix them. 99% of test failures are caused by your changes, not by pre-existing breakage. Do not blame external causes, do not skip tests, do not commit with failing tests. Fix the code or fix the test. Retry up to 3 times before giving up. Never commit broken work. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. Session log: ${helix_log} EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:971-975 and scripts/helix:3004-3016
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: The new reclaim helper refactor now decides reclaimability from a single tracker snapshot and then mutates tracker state without re-reading the bead immediately before the update. In ensure_alignment_bead(), a matching align bead can be closed after line 965 but before lines 974-975, and HELIX will still unclaim and re-claim it, reopening a bead that another actor just completed and dispatching duplicate alignment work. In recover_orphaned_issues(), the same stale-snapshot pattern at lines 3006-3016 can clear claim metadata on a bead that was closed after the initial read, erasing closure evidence instead of skipping it. This violates the repo’s concurrency model in ADR-001, which requires helix-run to treat tracker changes as live control input at safe boundaries.
Suggested fix: Re-read the live bead state immediately before every reclaiming tracker mutation, and skip reclaim when the bead is no longer in_progress. Add deterministic CLI coverage that simulates a bead closing after the initial snapshot so align/orphan recovery leave the closed bead untouched and do not dispatch duplicate work.
title: integration: reclaim paths act on stale tracker snapshots

description: Upgrade the existing rust-cargo concern from its current skeletal state to reflect the mature practices in niflheim and axon. Harvest: workspace lint inheritance (pedantic+nursery clippy), deny-level for unwrap/todo/dbg/print_stdout, unsafe_code=deny workspace-wide, cargo-deny (license/advisory/source auditing), cargo-machete (unused deps), thiserror for libs / anyhow for apps, cargo profile tuning (release/dev/profiling), MSRV pinning with rust-toolchain.toml, property-based testing (proptest + loom), scripts/with-pinned-rust.sh pattern, edition 2024. Projects: niflheim (primary source), axon. Push updated concern into both projects' docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md.
title: Concern: rust-cargo upgrade — harvest niflheim + axon practices

description:
PROBLEM: HELIX design guidance needs an unambiguous first-class Contract artifact category so authors can choose ADR vs Solution Design vs Technical Design vs Contract without taxonomy confusion. Contracts must be shareable normative specifications, not feature-level or decision-rationale documents. Governing artifact reference: workflows/phases/02-design/README.md. Dependency ID: helix-13cfe23f.
ROOT CAUSE: workflows/phases/02-design/README.md:1 describes the HELIX design phase, but the current phase guidance does not fully define Contract as the authoritative schema/interface artifact, does not settle CONTRACT-* vs API-* identifier guidance, and does not document how ADRs and solution designs should reference contracts without duplicating normative content.
PROPOSED FIX: Update workflows/phases/02-design/README.md and any adjacent design-phase guidance to add explicit selection rules for ADR, Solution Design, Technical Design, and Contract; define Contract as the field-by-field/schema-level/interface-level source of truth; clarify whether API-* is a subtype or legacy/supported alias under the canonical CONTRACT-* model; and document reference patterns from ADRs and solution designs to contracts.
NON-SCOPE: Do not change Hugo/Hextra theme behavior, website publishing configuration, demo asciinema recordings, agent harness invocation, OpenTelemetry runtime implementation, DDx/HELIX execution engine behavior, or unrelated artifact categories beyond the selection-boundary text needed to position Contract.
title: Define HELIX contract artifact taxonomy and naming rules

<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

<governing>scripts/refresh_context_digests.py — def infer_area_labels(item: dict) -> list[str]: labels = set(label for label in item.get("labels", []) if label.startswith("area:")) title = item.get("title", "").lower() for path in bead_paths(item): normalized = path.lower() if normalized.startswith("website/"): labels.add("area:site") if normalized.startswith("docs/demos/"): labels.add("area:demo") if normalized.startswith("docs/"): labels.add("area:docs") if normalized.startswith("tests/"): labels.add("area:testing") if normalized.startswith("scripts/") or normalized.startswith("bin/"): labels.add("area:cli") if normalized.startswith("workflows/") or normalized.startswith(".ddx/"): labels.add("area:workflow") if "/artifacts/" in normalized or normalized.endswith("/artifacts"): labels.add("area:artifacts") if "playwright coverage" in title or "deterministic coverage" in title or "deterministic verification" in title: labels.add("area:testing") if "helix-cli.sh hang" in title: labels.add("area:testing") if "deleted artifact type" in title or "artifact order consistency" in title: labels.add("area:artifacts") return sorted(labels)</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:433
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The reviewed fix updates `infer_area_labels()` to treat paths ending in `/artifacts` as artifact scope, but `bead_paths()` still never extracts extensionless directory scopes such as `workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts`. In a direct helper repro, an align bead or finding that references only that directory path returns `bead_paths=[]` and `infer_area_labels=[]`, so digest refresh still cannot reconstruct `area:workflow` or `area:artifacts` once those labels are missing. The new CLI test only covers wrapper-created labels and does not exercise helper-only inference, so this regression remains undetected.
Suggested fix: Teach `bead_paths()` to recognize extensionless HELIX artifact-directory paths (or otherwise feed raw `spec-id` or scope directory values into area inference), then add deterministic coverage that fails when `refresh_context_digests.py` cannot recover workflow and artifact labels for a `workflows/.../artifacts` scope.

    <criterion name='deterministic-helper-regression-covered' status='pass' evidence='tests/test_refresh_context_digests.py adds a regression that exercises helper-only inference for workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts.'/>

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>2907b90c89e51c72bc1922eb096221a6800141e7</governing>
</context-digest>
