acceptance: Every workflows/phases/*/artifacts/*/ directory contains meta.yml, template.md, and prompt.md. rg dependencies.yaml workflows/ returns no matches. No existing meta.yml carries a dependencies: block. The DDx artifact-types loader (one consumer) sees every HELIX artifact type; a substrate-neutral walk of the meta.yml inventory enumerates the same set.
description:
HELIX's methodology defines a catalog of artifact types (Vision, PRD, FEAT, US, ADR, SD, TD, TP, etc.). Each artifact type ships type-level metadata (id, prefix, phase, output location and naming, prompt and template pointers, examples, id_format) that lets any substrate (the DDx tracker today, potentially others) register and reason about that artifact type.

Today the metadata is incomplete and inconsistent:
- Five artifact directories under workflows/phases/ have no meta.yml at all (product-vision, business-case, competitive-analysis, opportunity-canvas, implementation-plan) — they are invisible to any registry that walks meta.yml files.
- Fourteen dependencies.yaml files across artifact directories are home-rolled and parallel to meta.yml — pure cruft. The instance graph lives in instance-doc depends_on lists; type-level dependency declarations are not consumed by any substrate today.
- Existing meta.yml files carry a dependencies: block that no consumer reads.

Scope:
1. Create meta.yml for every artifact missing one. Fields: artifact.{id, prefix, phase}, output.{location, naming}, id_format.{prefix, pattern, example}, prompts.{generation, review}, template.file, examples[].
2. Delete all dependencies.yaml files under workflows/phases/.
3. Strip the dependencies: block from every existing meta.yml.

HELIX defines this metadata as an open schema. DDx happens to consume a subset of it (see DDx's cli/internal/artifacttypes/loader.go), and the field set chosen here should be a strict superset of what any single substrate parses — so any compliant consumer works out of the box. No graph data is lost — the instance graph lives in instance-doc depends_on lists.
notes: Folded into runtime-neutral collapse critical path: complete the artifact-type metadata before schema/docs/skill portability work. DDx is one consumer, not the schema authority.
title: Complete HELIX's artifact-type metadata across all artifact directories

<governing>website/assets/json/search-data.json — After building the site in website/, the rendered search index contains /docs/glossary/ but not /glossary, and selecting the glossary search result navigates to an existing page.</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: website/assets/json/search-data.json:35
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new repo-owned search-data override preserves a broken synthetic glossary route. After running the Hugo build in website/, the rendered public/en.search-data.json contains both /docs/glossary/ and a second /glossary record, even though the actual page lives only at /docs/glossary/. Hextra's search client uses those JSON keys directly as result routes, so searching for glossary content can surface a non-existent destination. This is now HELIX-owned behavior because commit e61a4f6 shadows the upstream template into website/assets/json/search-data.json.
Suggested fix: Build the synthetic glossary record from the real glossary page permalink instead of relLangURL "glossary", and add a deterministic assertion that the rendered search index contains no nonexistent glossary route.

acceptance:
1. TestCrossWorkspaceIsolation_<Resource> for each P0 resource verifies that requests with a foreign workspace_id return 403 or 404 and never leak data.
2. TestRBAC_RepCannotDeletePeerRecord verifies a rep gets 403 when deleting another rep's record.
3. TestRBAC_ManagerCRUDTeamRecords verifies a manager can CRUD records owned by their team members.
4. TestRBAC_AdminCRUDAllRecords verifies an admin can CRUD across all records in their workspace.
5. `go test ./internal/api/handlers/... -run 'Isolation|RBAC' -race -count=1 -tags=integration` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-push` passes.
description:
PROBLEM: Multi-tenant isolation and role-based access control are security-critical but lack integration tests, allowing regressions to leak data between workspaces or bypass role checks.

ROOT CAUSE: internal/middleware/workspace.go:1 and internal/middleware/rbac.go:1 enforce tenant scoping and role gating, but no test exercises the negative paths (foreign workspace_id, rep deleting peer record).

PROPOSED FIX: Add internal/api/handlers/isolation_test.go and rbac_test.go that seed two workspaces and three role users (rep, manager, admin), then call each endpoint with mismatched workspace_id and with each role. Assert 403/404 for unauthorized access and 200 for permitted access. Use the same test harness as 010b (testcontainers Postgres or in-memory SQLite fallback).

NON-SCOPE: CRUD schema validation, pagination, soft-delete mechanics, E2E flows, performance benchmarks.

AC:
1. TestCrossWorkspaceIsolation_<Resource> for each P0 resource verifies that requests with a foreign workspace_id return 403 or 404 and never leak data.
2. TestRBAC_RepCannotDeletePeerRecord verifies a rep gets 403 when deleting another rep's record.
3. TestRBAC_ManagerCRUDTeamRecords verifies a manager can CRUD records owned by their team members.
4. TestRBAC_AdminCRUDAllRecords verifies an admin can CRUD across all records in their workspace.
5. `go test ./internal/api/handlers/... -run 'Isolation|RBAC' -race -count=1 -tags=integration` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-push` passes.

<governing>FEAT-005 — check_prompt() { local scope="${1:-repo}" cat <<EOF Execute the following HELIX check action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/check.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead ready --json\, \ddx bead list --json\, \ddx bead blocked --json\. Review scope: ${scope}. CRITICAL: Your output MUST contain the line "NEXT_ACTION: CODE" where CODE is one of BUILD, DESIGN, POLISH, ALIGN, BACKFILL, WAIT, GUIDANCE, or STOP. This line must appear on its own, not inside a code block or backticks. Without this line the automation cannot continue. Output this line FIRST, before any other analysis. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:2518
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `sync_issue_closing_commit_sha()` mutates the tracker with `tracker_update` and then runs `git commit --only -qm` without checking that commit's exit status before printing success. Both current callers invoke the helper as `sync_issue_closing_commit_sha ... || true` (`scripts/helix:2537` and `scripts/helix:3386`), and Bash disables `set -e` inside a function executed in an `||` list. A minimal reproduction, `bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; f(){ false; echo after; }; f || true; echo survived'`, prints `after` and `survived`, proving the helper can continue after an inner failure. If the repo-local tracker sync commit fails (for example because git identity is missing or the commit is otherwise rejected), HELIX can leave an uncommitted `closing_commit_sha` mutation in `.ddx/beads.jsonl` while still proceeding to review or the next loop step as though the sync succeeded.
Suggested fix: Treat the repo-local tracker sync commit as a required step: check the `git commit` exit status explicitly, return non-zero on failure, and either roll back the tracker mutation or stop the review/run path before continuing. Add deterministic regression coverage for a sync-commit failure path so the loop cannot proceed with a dirty tracker worktree.
title: correctness: sync closing_commit_sha must not continue after tracker-sync commit failure

acceptance: All Test* above pass; bash tests/refresh/run.sh and lefthook gates green.
description:
PROBLEM: There is no automated end-to-end check that Refresh produces correct per-classification outcomes against the fixture tree, nor that mutating inputs or removing the DIVERGENT case fails loudly.

ROOT CAUSE: tests/refresh/run.sh does not exist; no script invokes Refresh against tests/refresh/fixture/, diffs against tests/refresh/expected/, or validates report counts and write-scope.

PROPOSED FIX: Implement tests/refresh/run.sh that: (1) runs Refresh against tests/refresh/fixture/ into a temp work tree; (2) diffs every output path against tests/refresh/expected/<rel>; (3) parses the Refresh report and asserts per-classification counts match the count of fixtures of that classification; (4) asserts the DIVERGENT fixture appears in the report with handoff fields populated; (5) asserts no path outside 00-..06- was written; (6) exits non-zero with a diagnostic on any failure. Script must be deterministic and runnable from repo root.

NON-SCOPE: Implementing Refresh contract itself; modifying fixture (C1) or expected (C2) trees; CI wiring beyond lefthook.

AC:
1. TestRunPassesOnGoldenTree: `bash tests/refresh/run.sh` exits 0 against the unmodified fixture+expected trees.
2. TestRunFailsOnMutatedFixture: mutating any single file under tests/refresh/fixture/ causes run.sh to exit non-zero with a diagnostic naming that path.
3. TestRunFailsOnMissingDivergent: removing the DIVERGENT artifact from tests/refresh/fixture/06-iterate/ causes run.sh to exit non-zero with a message that the handoff path was not exercised.
4. TestRunFailsOnStrayWrite: a simulated Refresh write outside 00-..06- causes run.sh to exit non-zero naming the stray path.
5. TestRunChecksPerClassificationCounts: the report's INCOMPLETE/DIVERGENT/STALE_PLAN counts must equal the fixture-derived counts; injecting a mismatched count fails the run.
6. TestRunAssertsDivergentHandoffFields: the DIVERGENT entry in the report has handoff fields present and non-empty; clearing them fails the run.

Gates: `bash tests/refresh/run.sh` and `lefthook run pre-commit` both pass. Depends on C1 and C2, and on the Refresh contract landing.
title: C3: Refresh run.sh harness + classification/report assertions

description:
PROBLEM: REST CRUD endpoints for all P0 resources lack integration coverage, leaving status codes, response schemas, pagination, and soft-delete semantics unverified.

ROOT CAUSE: internal/api/handlers/*.go (contact_handler.go:1, account_handler.go:1, lead_handler.go:1, opportunity_handler.go:1, activity_handler.go:1) register routes via internal/api/router.go but have no httptest-based handler tests.

PROPOSED FIX: Add internal/api/handlers/*_integration_test.go using net/http/httptest and a containerized PostgreSQL via testcontainers-go (fallback: in-memory SQLite when TESTCONTAINERS_DISABLED=1 for CI). Exercise Create/Read/List/Update/Delete for each P0 resource, asserting status codes, JSON schema (via encoding/json round-trip into typed DTOs), pagination cursors, and that soft-deleted rows are excluded from List/Get and counted correctly.

NON-SCOPE: RBAC enforcement, cross-workspace isolation, E2E flows, performance benchmarks, unit model validation.

AC:
1. TestCRUD_Contact, TestCRUD_Account, TestCRUD_Lead, TestCRUD_Opportunity, TestCRUD_Activity each cover Create/Read/List/Update/Delete with status code and response schema assertions.
2. TestListPagination_<Resource> verifies page size, cursor advancement, and total count for each P0 resource.
3. TestSoftDelete_<Resource> verifies deleted rows are excluded from List/Get and that pagination total reflects the exclusion.
4. `go test ./internal/api/handlers/... -race -count=1 -tags=integration` passes.
5. `lefthook run pre-push` passes with the new tests staged.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>253d14f4611502d1986a2bd5699c3329f7c17c19</governing>

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>

<governing>workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/deployment-checklist/meta.yml — A checked-in deterministic test or validator covers the 05-deploy artifact metadata and fails on forward dependencies.requires edges or cycles among deploy artifacts. The test passes for the current deployment-checklist, monitoring-setup, and runbook metadata and is runnable from the repository's standard validation surface.</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts/deployment-checklist/meta.yml:12
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The fix in commit 839e82e was verified only with an ad hoc `python3` structural check recorded in the commit message and bead notes. `rg` across `tests`, `scripts`, and `workflows` found no committed regression test or validator that exercises the 05-deploy artifact `creation_order` and `dependencies.requires` invariants, so this exact forward-edge bug class can regress without any durable automated signal.
Suggested fix: Add a checked-in deterministic test or validator that fails when a 05-deploy artifact requires another deploy artifact with a later `workflow.creation_order` or when the deploy requires graph becomes cyclic, and run it from the normal repo test surface.

Document drag-drop stage change and filter/search interactions on the kanban. Include mobile fallback (kanban -> list) and accessibility annotations on every artifact.

NON-SCOPE: Detail views (covered by CRM-DESIGN-006b), activity logging and bulk select (CRM-DESIGN-006b), sign-up/login (CRM-DESIGN-006c), CSV import/export (CRM-DESIGN-006d). No frontend implementation. No detail-view layouts.

acceptance: workflows/concerns/typescript-bun/concern.md and practices.md reflect real erp+nexiq practices and are specific enough to prevent Node.js drift. Both projects have docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md referencing typescript-bun.
description: Review and strengthen the existing typescript-bun concern by harvesting real practices from erp and nexiq. This is the concern that motivated the whole system — nexiq drifted to Node.js when it should have stayed on Bun. Harvest: erp practices (monorepo with workspaces, biome config, deployment setup, testing framework), nexiq practices (monorepo, vitest, current state to understand the drift). Ensure the concern is strong enough that an agent selecting it cannot accidentally reach for npm/node/yarn. Verify Bun-native API preferences, package manager enforcement, test runner choice. Areas: all. Push into erp and nexiq docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md.
title: Concern: typescript-bun review — harvest erp + nexiq practices

acceptance:
1. TestRunAllShExitsZeroOnAllPass: with all scenarios passing, tests/workflows/run-all.sh exits 0.
2. TestRunAllShExitCountsFailures: with N failing scenarios across runtimes, exit code equals N.
3. TestRunAllShTreatsGenieCatalogUnreachableAsSkip: scenarios emitting `genie:catalog-unreachable` do not increment failure count.
4. TestRunAllShUsesSharedFixtureOnly: harness invokes scenarios against tests/workflows/fixtures/recipe-app/ exclusively (no per-runtime fixture forks).
5. TestRunAllShSkipsVhsWhenAbsent: missing vhs binary does not fail the run; recording step is skipped silently.
GATES: `go test ./tests/workflows/...` passes; `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM: No top-level workflow harness exists to orchestrate per-runtime workflow scenarios against the shared fixture. tests/install/run-all.sh covers install verification but not workflow execution.

ROOT CAUSE: tests/workflows/run-all.sh:1 — missing file. Reference pattern: tests/install/run-all.sh:1 demonstrates per-runtime Docker-image build + scenario iteration + failed-count exit, but is install-scoped and cannot be reused as-is.

PROPOSED FIX: Create tests/workflows/run-all.sh that (a) iterates the five runtimes (claude-code, codex, copilot, cursor, genie); (b) builds or reuses Docker images per runtime, symlinking/inheriting from tests/install/<runtime>/install.sh where reusable; (c) executes scenarios against the single shared fixture tests/workflows/fixtures/recipe-app/; (d) captures vhs recordings when the vhs binary is available, otherwise skips silently; (e) accumulates failed-scenario count across runtimes; (f) exits with that integer count (0 = all passed). Pass criteria stay runtime-neutral; per-runtime variants differ only by invocation/observability. Treat genie scenarios emitting `genie:catalog-unreachable` as skip rather than fail until helix-96f7dd34 resolves.

NON-SCOPE: justfile recipes (ENT-02, ENT-03); resolving helix-96f7dd34; authoring new per-runtime scenarios beyond the orchestrator contract; modifying tests/install/run-all.sh.

GOVERNING: FEAT-014 ENT-01 · tests/install/run-all.sh · tests/workflows/fixtures/recipe-app/
PARENT: helix-88f790e4
title: ENT-01: tests/workflows/run-all.sh harness orchestrator

description:
<context-digest>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | product-microsite-ia | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Define the primary reader modes before defining the navigation tree. · Reader modes: evaluators, new users, active HELIX users, and contributors · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits. · ADR-003 Autonomy is a Three-Position Spectrum, Not a Fixed Level — We adopt a three-position autonomy spectrum — low, medium, high, default medium — that controls checkpoint density only. The level changes how often a workflow pauses for confirmation; it never changes which activities run or whether a hard stop is honored. Why: HELIX must express an autonomy policy that a capable runtime honors, with a fixed vocabulary, a deterministic resolution order, and two invariants: a hard stop no level may pass, and a guarantee that autonomy never collapses the activity loop. The policy must be runtime-neutral — no CLAUDE.md dependency, no runtime config schema. · ADR-004 Artifact Dependencies Are Encoded in meta.yml.relationships Only (No Separate dependencies.yaml) — Type-level validation (required_sections, quality_checks, Why: The catalog must have a single source of truth for artifact-type dependencies. The rubric must stop asking for a file the catalog does not produce. Any consumer that currently mentions dependencies.yaml must be repointed at meta.yml so the rubric and the workflow agree.</adrs>
<governing>PLAN-2026-06-11-FLOW-ARCHITECTURE</governing>
</context-digest>

Update hand-authored website/user docs and regenerate generated mirrors so public documentation teaches the current HELIX surface: documents, markers, artifact templates, graph/catalog concepts, and the single helix routing skill. Remove or mark historical current-looking sibling skills/flows, env-var routing, user-facing scripts/binaries, CLI flags, status-tracking-in-specs language, and stale name: helix-align examples. Exact Codex sweep targets are docs/install/codex.md and docs/website/content/use/codex-recipe.md; expected outcome is no-op unless retired wording is found. In scope: docs/website/content install/use/reference/workflow/ddx-runtime/why/artifacts pages, generated artifact mirrors, and generation commands. Out of scope: changing product source docs except to fix source-before-mirror issues.
title: Align user website docs with the single helix flow model

acceptance: skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md explicitly says triage must not create execution-ready implementation beads without measurable success criteria, defines the fallback route to planning/polish or not-execution-ready status, and bash tests/validate-skills.sh fails if that rule is removed
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md:51
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The revised triage skill now tells agents to write deterministic acceptance and says queue-drained execution beads should prefer exact commands, files, and end-state conditions, but it still does not define the same negative path that polish now has when the governing artifacts do not support a measurable execution contract. workflows/EXECUTION.md:606-608 says beads that cannot be closed from explicit evidence should be refined by helix polish or helix triage before entering the execution queue. As written, helix-triage can still create phase:build beads with vague acceptance text whenever the intake request is underspecified, leaving execution readiness to later human judgment instead of blocking it at creation time.
Suggested fix: Extend skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md so that when deterministic success criteria cannot be derived, triage routes the work back to planning/polish or explicitly marks it not execution-ready instead of creating a queue-ready implementation bead.
title: correctness: triage still lacks a non-execution-ready fallback for vague build beads

acceptance:
1. GET /search?q=<query> endpoint accepts search term; returns paginated results.
2. Search includes: contacts (name, email, phone, title), accounts (name, domain, industry), opportunities (name, account name, owner name), leads (name, email, company).
3. Matching: full-text search or substring match (at minimum, case-insensitive LIKE on name/email/company).
4. Results paginated (default 20 per type); response includes result_type (contact/account/opportunity/lead) + relevant fields.
5. Results respect workspace_id isolation; no cross-workspace leakage.
6. Performance target: p95 < 1s for workspaces with ≤100k records; use database indices or search engine (Elasticsearch if needed for scale).
7. Results ordered by relevance (exact match > substring match > fuzzy match, if implemented).
8. Null results return 200 with empty results array (not 404).
description: Implement global search: GET /search?q=term returns contacts, accounts, opportunities matching name/email/company. Req R-7: sub-second for workspaces ≤100k records. Depends on database, CRUD, auth.
title: CRM-IMPL-006: Search functionality

description:
PROBLEM: Kanban pipeline view and global search endpoints have no documented contract; UI work cannot proceed without it.

ROOT CAUSE: docs/api/openapi.yaml:1 — file authored by CRM-DESIGN-003a contains only CRUD paths; the pipeline and search endpoints required by parent CRM-DESIGN-003 AC#3 are absent (depends on CRM-DESIGN-003a being merged first, CRM-DESIGN-002 data design, ADR-001).

PROPOSED FIX: Extend docs/api/openapi.yaml. Add GET /pipeline returning opportunities grouped by stage (kanban payload shape). Add GET /search?q={query}&entity={optional} returning cross-entity results. Define components.schemas: PipelineView, SearchResult.

NON-SCOPE: CRUD entity endpoints (CRM-DESIGN-003a). Auth endpoints (CRM-DESIGN-003c). ErrorResponse schema, pagination contract, example payloads (CRM-DESIGN-003d). Import/export endpoints (sibling child CRM-DESIGN-003b-2). Background job design. Streaming format choices.
title: CRM-DESIGN-003b-1: OpenAPI spec — pipeline and search endpoints

description:
<context-digest>

Create the Docker test scenario for OpenAI Codex CLI. Layout:
- `tests/install/codex-cli/Dockerfile` — minimal base image with the runtime CLI installed.
- `tests/install/codex-cli/install.sh` — runs: `mkdir -p ~/.codex/skills/helix && cp -r /workspace/helix/skills/helix/* ~/.codex/skills/helix/`
- `tests/install/codex-cli/verify.sh` — runs `tests/install/shared/verify-skill-layout.sh` against the installed location, plus a functional check gated by `TEST_FUNCTIONAL=1` that invokes the runtime non-interactively and asserts the response names modes from `expected-modes.txt`.
- `tests/install/codex-cli/record.tape` — vhs script that records the install + verify sequence to `tests/install/codex-cli/recording.gif`.

Notes: Codex CLI installed; OPENAI_API_KEY from env; functional check via `codex exec --ephemeral` gated by TEST_FUNCTIONAL=1.
title: Author tests/install/codex-cli/ scenario: Dockerfile + install.sh + verify.sh + record.tape

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/refresh_context_digests.py:379
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The updated select_digest_practices() helper only avoids duplicates against the already-prioritized prefix, so it still drops override-specific guidance when an early override duplicates a later library bullet. For example, select_digest_practices(['Library practice A', 'Library practice B'], ['Library practice B', 'Override practice C'], 2) currently returns ['Library practice A', 'Library practice B'] instead of preserving any override-only practice. That still violates the merged-practices contract for context digests when overrides exist.
Suggested fix: Choose the prioritized override from override bullets that are not present anywhere in library_practices before falling back to a duplicate, and add a regression test for the later-library-duplicate case so the digest always retains one override-specific practice when available.

<governing>FEAT-005 — alignment_prompt() { local scope="${1:-repo}" governing_bead="${2:-}" governing_mode="${3:-}" local governing_note="Governing alignment bead: create or claim the relevant kind:planning,action:align bead before proceeding." if [[ -n "$governing_bead" ]]; then case "$governing_mode" in claimed) governing_note="Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (claimed by the wrapper entrypoint; treat Phase 0.5 as already satisfied unless tracker metadata drifted materially)." ;; reused) governing_note="Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (already active for this scope; reuse it and do not create a duplicate governing bead)." ;; created) governing_note="Governing alignment bead: ${governing_bead} (created by the wrapper entrypoint; claim and reuse it instead of creating a duplicate governing bead)." ;; esac fi cat <<EOF Execute the following HELIX alignment action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/reconcile-alignment.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/templates/alignment-review.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead\ subcommands. Review scope: ${scope}. ${governing_note} Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:789
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: find_alignment_bead selects any non-closed align bead, and ensure_alignment_bead treats an existing in-progress bead as reusable without reclaiming it. A second helix align run can therefore proceed under a bead still owned by another actor or left stale after a crash, which violates the action contract to search open beads and claim the governing bead before continuing.
Suggested fix: Restrict lookup to status=open for reusable candidates, or explicitly detect in-progress beads and reclaim or fail with a tracker-visible error instead of silently reusing them.

description:
<context-digest>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | product-microsite-ia | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Define the primary reader modes before defining the navigation tree. · Reader modes: evaluators, new users, active HELIX users, and contributors · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits. · ADR-004 Artifact Dependencies Are Encoded in meta.yml.relationships Only (No Separate dependencies.yaml) — Type-level validation (required_sections, quality_checks, Why: The catalog must have a single source of truth for artifact-type dependencies. The rubric must stop asking for a file the catalog does not produce. Any consumer that currently mentions dependencies.yaml must be repointed at meta.yml so the rubric and the workflow agree. · ADR-005 Concern practices.md is HELIX-activity-keyed — Every concern's practices.md organizes its per-activity content under HELIX activity headings: Discover, Frame, Design, Test, Build, Deploy, Iterate. Topic-keyed or quality-gates-keyed organization is no longer acceptable for practices.md. Why: Per-activity context-digest assembly must be a deterministic lookup, not a heuristic. The concerns library must use one consistent organizing principle so that any consumer — context assembler, alignment review, methodology docs — extracts the practices for a given activity by reading the corresponding heading.</adrs>
<governing>PLAN-2026-06-11-FLOW-ARCHITECTURE</governing>
</context-digest>

Repair docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-plugin-packaging.md and add bounded historical/report/plan dispositions to dated docs that still present retired sibling-flow architecture as current. FEAT-004 has known blind relabel corruption and abandoned 17-skill plus /helix:<x> selector language. Reconstruct intent around one public helix skill and plugin packaging without inventing implementation status. Dated family/library/sibling/result docs under docs/helix/02-design should be marked historical/report/plan when they teach retired sibling architecture as current. In scope: FEAT-004 and targeted dated source docs. Out of scope: website mirrors except if mechanically regenerated by a later bead, SKILL.md, fixtures.
title: Repair FEAT-004 and label dated sibling-flow docs

<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:6
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit cc30a3c9b49e68125d0960369a7f9207002e6068 closes helix-09f1b7ca, but the bead row still has no <measure-results> block, no report-style closure summary, and no closing_commit_sha. HELIX's implementation and report contract requires execution cycles to record measurement evidence on the governing bead and then close it with durable closure context. As closed, ddx bead show helix-09f1b7ca --json cannot tell a later agent what evidence satisfied the issue or which commit implemented the gate change without re-reading git history.
Suggested fix: Append durable measure/report evidence to helix-09f1b7ca before or while closing it, including the implementation commit SHA, acceptance evidence for workflows/phases/06-iterate/GATE.yaml, and a short closure summary.

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>1a8db12e9143960a0db7ccb7fa423afb326ace44</governing>
</context-digest>

<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>scripts/validate_context_digests.py — if missing_context or unauthorized_omission or missing_review_area: for line_number, bead_id, title in missing_context: print( f"{tracker_path}:{line_number}: bead {bead_id} is missing " f"<context-digest> or workflow-authorized explicit omission rationale: {title}", file=sys.stderr, ) for line_number, bead_id, title in unauthorized_omission: print( f"{tracker_path}:{line_number}: bead {bead_id} uses an explicit omission rationale " f"without {AUTHORIZED_OMISSION_LABEL}: {title}", file=sys.stderr, ) for line_number, bead_id, title in missing_review_area: print( f"{tracker_path}:{line_number}: review-finding bead {bead_id} is missing area:* label(s): {title}", file=sys.stderr, ) print( "validated context digests / workflow-authorized omission rationales on 0 bead(s): " f"{len(missing_context)} missing digest-or-rationale entry(ies), " f"{len(unauthorized_omission)} unauthorized omission rationale entry(ies), " f"{len(missing_review_area)} review-finding bead(s) missing area labels", file=sys.stderr, ) return 1</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/validate_context_digests.py:29
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The validator now treats `digest:omission-authorized` as sufficient proof that a bead came from a workflow-authorized omission path, but this commit never introduces any concrete authorized omission path or provenance the validator can verify. A repo-wide search after `cc8c280` finds only the validator, docs, and the new test fixture mentioning the label; there is still no specific workflow that grants omission for a named bead class. In a temp tracker containing only `labels=["helix","phase:build","digest:omission-authorized"]` and `description="Explicit omission rationale: skipped for convenience"`, `python3 scripts/validate_context_digests.py --tracker <tempfile>` exits 0, so any bead can still bypass the digest requirement by self-applying the label.
Suggested fix: Replace the self-attested label check with deterministic authorization the validator can verify from a real, named omission path (for example an allowlisted workflow/type combination or dedicated machine-set field), update the governing workflow docs to define that path explicitly, and add a regression test showing a label-only bead still fails while a genuinely authorized omission path passes.

Scope: repo public tracker contract surfaces
Category: drift

Description: The live wrapper resolves the built-in tracker from .ddx by default (`tracker_dir="${DDX_BEAD_DIR:-${HELIX_TRACKER_DIR:-${target_root}/.ddx}}"`), but the shipped help text and execution guide still say `HELIX_TRACKER_DIR` defaults to `.helix/`. <practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. acceptance:
1. Activity model/table created (kind, body, author_id, occurred_at, record_type, record_id, created_at, created_by).
2. POST /activities endpoint accepts kind (call/email/meeting/note), body, occurred_at, record_type, record_id.
3. Activities are immutable after creation (no PUT/PATCH in MVP; P2 allows soft-delete via archive flag).
4. GET /activities?record_type=contact&record_id=<id> returns activities ordered by occurred_at DESC.
5. Activity author is automatically set to current user (from JWT/session).
6. Reps can log activities on any record (contact, account, opportunity) in their workspace.
7. Activities appear in record detail views (contact/opportunity detail shows activity feed).
8. Occurred_at can be set to past date (rep logs call from yesterday), different from created_at (when logged into system).
9. Permission check: user can only see activities in their workspace; cross-workspace access denied.
description: Implement activity logging: POST /activities (create), GET /activities (query by record), activity feed on detail views. Supports call, email, meeting, note types. Depends on database, auth, CRUD.
title: CRM-IMPL-004: Activity logging

acceptance:
1. Sign-up endpoint (POST /auth/signup) accepts email, password, workspace_name; creates Workspace record and User record with role=admin.
2. Login endpoint (POST /auth/login) accepts email, password; validates credentials; returns JWT (with sub=user_id, workspace_id claim) or sets secure session cookie.
3. Password hashing: bcrypt (cost 12+) or equivalent; passwords never logged or stored in plaintext.
4. Logout endpoint (POST /auth/logout) invalidates session or tokens.
5. All endpoints enforce authorization: middleware injects workspace_id from JWT/session into every query (WHERE workspace_id = ?).
6. Cross-workspace isolation test: rep from workspace A cannot fetch workspace B's contact via GET /contacts/<id> or any query; returns 403 or 404.
7. Role-based access control implemented: rep role, manager role, admin role with correct permissions (documented in CRM-DESIGN-004 security).
8. Password reset flow: email verification token, time-limited reset link (P1 or stretch).
description: Implement email/password auth, sign-up flow, JWT or session-based login, workspace creation, role assignment (rep/manager/admin). Depends on ADR-005 (auth provider), ADR-001 (framework), CRM-DESIGN-003 (API contract). Acceptance: sign-up creates workspace + user; login returns JWT/session; all queries scoped to workspace_id; cross-workspace access returns 404.
title: CRM-IMPL-002: Authentication, sign-up, and workspace isolation

acceptance:
(Activation here is already behavioral, not prose; this tightens it and adds the negative control.)
1. `bash tests/workflows/ddx/run-scenarios.sh` exits 0 (with an API key for whatever DDx routes to): installs HELIX (`ddx install helix --local /workspace/helix --force`), creates a fixture SCN-01 bead, runs `ddx work --once --json`.
2. Activation/behavior assertion (structured — keep): parse result.json for the routing decision (`route <provider>/<model>`) AND assert the execution-evidence dir contains the produced artifact (vision.md or PRD.md). Not a prose grep.
3. Negative control: running the fixture bead with HELIX NOT installed produces no HELIX routing/artifact and exits NONZERO, proving the assertion depends on the skill.
4. tests/workflows/ddx/recordings/INT-DD.gif generated via vhs and committed, showing install + bead-create + work-once + evidence inspection. docs/install/README.md (DDx section) links it.
description:
Create tests/workflows/ddx/ harness that exercises DDx as the reference runtime — install HELIX, file a fixture bead, run one bead-execution, assert the artifacts.

**Deliverables**:
- `tests/workflows/ddx/run-scenarios.sh` — orchestrator
- `tests/workflows/ddx/fixtures/seed-bead.json` — minimal bead spec for SCN-01 bootstrap (sparse intent)
- `tests/workflows/ddx/expected/` — execution evidence assertions
- `tests/workflows/ddx/recordings/INT-DD.tape` + `INT-DD.gif`

**Harness shape** (reuse `tests/install/ddx/Dockerfile`):
1. Build image with `ddx` binary
2. `ddx install helix --local /workspace/helix --force`
3. `ddx bead create <SCN-01-prompt-bead> --type task --priority 1 --description ... --acceptance ...`
4. `ddx work --once --json > result.json`
5. Grep result.json for routing decision (`route claude/<model>`) + execution evidence path
6. Read execution evidence dir; assert vision.md or PRD.md was produced
7. vhs renders the whole orchestrator

**Note**: this also exercises the DDx routing surface that's been flaky in this session (ddx-a827d07f, ddx-65d3ba51). If the routing fails, the test should fail loudly so we see it.

<context-digest>
<principles>Validate Your Work · Runtime Neutrality · Design for Simplicity</principles>
<concerns>installer-coverage | screencast-evidence | runtime-parity</concerns>
<practices>DDx is the reference runtime · test is `ddx install helix --local` then `ddx work --once --json` against fixture beads · assertion: routing decision + execution evidence land · vhs recording of the queue drain</practices>
<governing>docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-014-helix-workflow-coverage.md SCN-01 (bootstrap) + SCN-06 (review) · docs/install/ddx.md · existing tests/install/ddx/ scaffolding</governing>

<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-007 — HELIX needs a public-facing documentation site and scripted demo reels that show users what HELIX does, how it works, and how to get started. The microsite is the primary onboarding surface — it must take a new user from "what is this?" to running helix run on their own project.</governing>
</context-digest>

Review README, microsite pages, and demos for drift against the current HELIX/DDx public execution contract and active site/demo concerns.

description: Add a portable HELIX voice configuration for artifact authoring. HELIX artifacts are durable signal between humans and LLMs, so HELIX should own the canonical artifact voice profile instead of making Sloptimizer the source of truth. Sloptimizer may optionally consume the profile when installed. In-scope files: workflows/voice.yml or equivalent, workflows/artifact-schema.md, workflows/conventions.md, skills/helix/SKILL.md, scripts/tests needed to validate catalog metadata, and artifact meta.yml files under workflows/activities/*/artifacts/. Out-of-scope: rewriting artifact examples into a new prose style, creating separate public helix-* skills, requiring Sloptimizer as an installation dependency, changing website copy beyond aligning the website voice guide to the profile registry.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — review_prompt() { local scope="${1:-last-commit}" local in_loop="${2:-0}" cat <<EOF Execute the following HELIX fresh-eyes review action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/fresh-eyes-review.md") Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead\ subcommands. Review scope: ${scope}. ${in_loop:+This review is running inside the automated helix run loop after an implementation cycle.} IMPORTANT: File each actionable finding (critical, high, medium severity) as a tracker issue with label \review-finding\ plus at least one scope-appropriate \area:*\ label so findings are durable, concern-aware, and queryable via \ddx bead list --label review-finding\. See the "Filing Findings as Tracker Issues" section in the action for the exact command pattern. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:784
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: `alignment_area_labels_from_path()` only emits `area:artifacts` when the scope string contains `/artifacts/`. A directory scope that ends exactly at the artifacts root, such as `workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts`, misses that label entirely. In a scratch run, `helix align workflows/phases/05-deploy/artifacts` created a governing bead with only `area:workflow`, and the stored description rendered `Scope areas: workflow.` That means artifact-directory alignment passes still lose the artifact concern surface this commit was supposed to restore.
Suggested fix: Treat paths whose final segment is `artifacts` as artifact scope in both wrapper-side label seeding and any helper-side inference used for digest refresh, then add deterministic coverage for directory-scoped align targets.

    <criterion name="digest inference matches wrapper scope inference" status="pass" evidence="scripts/refresh_context_digests.py now infers area:artifacts for paths ending exactly with /artifacts, keeping digest refresh aligned with wrapper labels."/>
    <criterion name="deterministic regression coverage exists" status="pass" evidence="tests/helix-cli.sh adds test_align_artifact_directory_scope_preserves_artifact_area_label to fail if the artifact-directory scope drops area:artifacts or omits both scope areas from the governing bead description."/>
  </acceptance>
  <gates>

  <commits>

</measure-results>

description:
PROBLEM: No just recipe gates functional workflow scenarios behind TEST_FUNCTIONAL=1 for release-tag CI, blocking the runtime-parity validation step required by FEAT-014.

ROOT CAUSE: justfile — no `workflow-test-functional` recipe; TEST_FUNCTIONAL env contract not surfaced through the justfile interface.

PROPOSED FIX: Add `workflow-test-functional` recipe to justfile that exports TEST_FUNCTIONAL=1 and invokes tests/workflows/run-all.sh. Document required auth secrets and accepted skip-tags (including `genie:catalog-unreachable` per helix-96f7dd34 carve-out) in a recipe-adjacent comment or referenced doc. Recipe must surface skip-tag emissions so a passing run with documented skips is distinguishable from a clean pass in logs.

NON-SCOPE: Static recipe (ENT-02); harness script implementation (ENT-01); provisioning CI secrets themselves (operator-required); changes to helix-96f7dd34 resolution.

GOVERNING: FEAT-014 ENT-03 · justfile · tests/workflows/run-all.sh
PARENT: helix-88f790e4
DEPENDS-ON: ENT-01 harness contract

The example structure and template commands therefore point to directories that do not exist, so a user following the README will fail to locate the documented paths or run the documented commands.
Suggested fix: Update the project-convention section to describe the supported `docs/helix/` layout and valid `workflows/phases/...` artifact paths or replace the commands with the current supported workflow entrypoints.
title: correctness: replace stale helix-phases and ddx apply paths in docs README

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>.ddx/beads.jsonl — ddx bead show helix-0596f6b0 --json returns status=closed and closing_commit_sha=null after tracker-close commit ce7b4f54aaa7388b548cb32e4b54626325adcb18</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:3
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The automated review correctly resolved `last-commit` to implementation commit `3bc47865145f9256c8297021830133061e754e3f` because `HEAD` is the tracker-only close commit `ce7b4f54aaa7388b548cb32e4b54626325adcb18`, but the live `helix-0596f6b0` tracker row still has `closing_commit_sha: null`. That leaves the closed execution bead without the durable implementation-commit pointer that the close commit message says it persisted, so subsequent review/report passes must recover the true implementation commit from git history instead of the bead itself.
Suggested fix: Update `helix-0596f6b0` in `.ddx/beads.jsonl` so `closing_commit_sha` is set to `3bc47865145f9256c8297021830133061e754e3f` when the bead is closed, keeping the tracker row self-contained for later review and report actions.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading)</practices>
<governing>FEAT-005 — implementation_prompt() { local selector="${1:-}" local context_file="${target_root}/.helix/context.md" cat <<EOF $(if [[ -f "$context_file" ]]; then cat "$context_file"; printf '\n---\n\n'; fi) Execute the following HELIX implementation action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/implementation.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/phases/04-build/enforcer.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead ready --json\, \ddx bead show <id>\, \ddx bead update <id> --claim\, \ddx bead close <id>\. Concurrency rule: re-read the selected issue immediately before claim and immediately before close. If tracker metadata drifted materially, do not claim or close from stale assumptions; re-check instead. ${selector:+Implementation target: ${selector}.} CRITICAL: If tests fail after your changes, fix them. 99% of test failures are caused by your changes, not by pre-existing breakage. Do not blame external causes, do not skip tests, do not commit with failing tests. Fix the code or fix the test. Retry up to 3 times before giving up. Never commit broken work. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. Session log: ${helix_log} EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:2388
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit ae4c544 unconditionally runs `git add "$tracker_file" && git commit ...` after `tracker_close`. That works when the built-in tracker lives under the repo, but HELIX still accepts `DDX_BEAD_DIR` / `HELIX_TRACKER_DIR` overrides. When the tracker directory is external, `git add` fails with `is outside repository` after the implementation commit has already been created. Reproduction: in a temp repo with `DDX_BEAD_DIR` pointing at a sibling directory, `bash scripts/helix commit hx-ext-0` exits 128 after creating the implementation commit, leaves the bead closed only in the external tracker file, and never reaches push/reporting.
Suggested fix: Gate the tracker-sync commit on whether `$tracker_file` is inside `$target_root`. If the tracker is external, still sync `closing_commit_sha` in the tracker, but skip the repo-local tracker commit or use a different persistence path that does not require `git add` on an out-of-repo file. Add a deterministic harness test covering external `DDX_BEAD_DIR` mode.
title: integration: keep helix commit working with external tracker dirs

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/validate_context_digests.py:47
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new validator rejects every open HELIX bead whose description lacks a <context-digest> block, but the updated FEAT-006 contract and workflows/actions/input.md:84-85 explicitly allow an open bead to carry an explicit omission rationale when the contract permits omission. Reproducing against a temp tracker entry whose description is 'Explicit omission rationale: ...' exits 1 and reports the bead as invalid. That means the new validation gate can fail on queue entries that satisfy the documented omission path instead of the missing-digest failure mode it is supposed to catch.
Suggested fix: Teach the validator to recognize the documented explicit-omission-rationale path, define the allowed marker/format in the contract if needed, and add deterministic fixtures that prove rationale-bearing beads pass while truly missing digests still fail.
title: correctness: allow explicit digest-omission rationale in validator

description:
PROBLEM: Contract artifacts must support more than HTTP APIs. HELIX needs reusable normative specifications for telemetry schemas, export protocols, ownership boundaries, authoring conventions, CLI/library mutation surfaces, and other cross-project interfaces. Governing artifact reference: workflows/phases/02-design/README.md. Dependency ID: helix-13cfe23f.
ROOT CAUSE: workflows/phases/02-design/README.md:1 and docs/helix/02-design/contracts/:1 recognize contracts as a design artifact area, but current guidance is underspecified for non-HTTP contracts such as telemetry/export schemas and reusable protocol/interface definitions.
PROPOSED FIX: Extend the Contract docs and template language to explicitly cover non-HTTP surfaces. Include examples or guidance hooks for telemetry/export contracts, data/interface contracts, CLI/library mutation contracts, cross-project ownership boundaries, and authoring conventions. State that HTTP API contracts are one subtype, not the whole Contract category.
NON-SCOPE: Do not change Hugo/Hextra theme behavior, website publishing configuration, demo asciinema recordings, agent harness invocation, OpenTelemetry runtime implementation, DDx/HELIX execution engine behavior, or generate a real project-specific telemetry contract beyond illustrative template guidance.
title: Make contract guidance cover non-HTTP protocol and telemetry interfaces

Description: Commit 1637cb4a719977f5a67280d1acd2056de99e5ccc amends helix-09f1b7ca's <measure-results> block by adding the diff-hygiene acceptance criterion, a matching diff-hygiene gate, and updated report-summary text, but it leaves the existing <timestamp> unchanged. acceptance:
1. A temp tracker containing an open HELIX bead that only self-applies digest:omission-authorized plus digest-omission-path=helix-input:legacy-migration fails python3 scripts/validate_context_digests.py --tracker <tempfile>.
2. The repository defines and documents the additional provenance required for the real helix input legacy-migration omission path.
3. tests/validate-context-digests.sh covers the forged-path negative case and a genuine authorized-path positive case.
4. git diff --check passes.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>scripts/validate_context_digests.py — if missing_context or unauthorized_omission or missing_review_area: for line_number, bead_id, title in missing_context: print( f"{tracker_path}:{line_number}: bead {bead_id} is missing " f"<context-digest> or workflow-authorized explicit omission rationale: {title}", file=sys.stderr, ) for line_number, bead_id, title in unauthorized_omission: print( f"{tracker_path}:{line_number}: bead {bead_id} uses an explicit omission rationale " f"without {AUTHORIZED_OMISSION_LABEL} plus an allowed " f"{AUTHORIZED_OMISSION_PATH_FIELD}: {title}", file=sys.stderr, ) for line_number, bead_id, title in missing_review_area: print( f"{tracker_path}:{line_number}: review-finding bead {bead_id} is missing area:* label(s): {title}", file=sys.stderr, ) print( "validated context digests / workflow-authorized omission rationales on 0 bead(s): " f"{len(missing_context)} missing digest-or-rationale entry(ies), " f"{len(unauthorized_omission)} unauthorized omission rationale entry(ies), " f"{len(missing_review_area)} review-finding bead(s) missing area labels", file=sys.stderr, ) return 1</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/validate_context_digests.py:31
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: has_authorized_omission_rationale() now requires both digest:omission-authorized and digest-omission-path=helix-input:legacy-migration, but it still trusts those fields purely from the bead payload. A temp tracker containing a normal open bead with those two self-applied fields and description="Explicit omission rationale: forged authorized omission path" passes python3 scripts/validate_context_digests.py --tracker <tempfile> with exit code 0, even though the docs reserve that path for the real helix input legacy-migration flow. The validator therefore still cannot distinguish a workflow-produced authorized omission from a forged bead that copied the allowlisted string.
Suggested fix: Tie omission-path authorization to durable provenance the validator can verify from the bead itself or surrounding workflow state, then add a deterministic regression case proving a forged bead with the allowlisted path still fails while a real helix input legacy-migration bead passes.
title: correctness: allowlisted omission path remains self-attested

acceptance: helix build <selector> rejects closed, blocked, or non-execution-ready beads before DDx dispatch, and bash tests/helix-cli.sh includes a regression case for a closed selector that passes.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — implementation_prompt() { local selector="${1:-}" local context_file="${target_root}/.helix/context.md" cat <<EOF $(if [[ -f "$context_file" ]]; then cat "$context_file"; printf '\n---\n\n'; fi) Execute the following HELIX implementation action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/implementation.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/phases/04-build/enforcer.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead ready --json\, \ddx bead show <id>\, \ddx bead update <id> --claim\, \ddx bead close <id>\. Concurrency rule: re-read the selected issue immediately before claim and immediately before close. If tracker metadata drifted materially, do not claim or close from stale assumptions; re-check instead. ${selector:+Implementation target: ${selector}.} CRITICAL: If tests fail after your changes, fix them. 99% of test failures are caused by your changes, not by pre-existing breakage. Do not blame external causes, do not skip tests, do not commit with failing tests. Fix the code or fix the test. Retry up to 3 times before giving up. Never commit broken work. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. Session log: ${helix_log} EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:1031
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: resolve_execution_target returns any bead whose ID exists in the tracker before consulting the execution-ready queue. That means helix build <bead-id> will dispatch DDx managed execution for closed, blocked, or otherwise non-execution-ready beads, even though the compatibility contract says build should resolve a matching ready bead before calling ddx agent execute-bead. In this repository, HELIX_EXEC_CONTEXT=1 bash scripts/helix build helix-9efef95b attempted to execute the already-closed bead helix-9efef95b instead of rejecting it.
Suggested fix: resolve explicit selectors against ddx bead ready --execution output, or re-check status and execution-eligible fields after lookup, and fail before DDx dispatch when the selector is not currently execution-ready.
notes:
<measure-results>
  <timestamp>2026-04-11T09:02:42Z</timestamp>
  <status>PASS</status>
  <acceptance>
    <criterion name='selector resolves only execution-ready beads' status='pass' evidence='Removed the explicit tracker_show fast-path from scripts/helix so resolve_execution_target now resolves selectors exclusively against ddx bead ready --execution output.'/>
    <criterion name='closed selector regression covered' status='pass' evidence='Added test_build_rejects_closed_selector_before_execute_bead to tests/helix-cli.sh and registered it in the deterministic harness.'/>
  </acceptance>
  <gates>
    <gate concern='cli' command='bash tests/helix-cli.sh' status='pass'/>
    <gate concern='repo' command='git diff --check' status='pass'/>
  </gates>
  <ratchets />
</measure-results>
title: correctness: build selector accepts closed or non-ready bead IDs

Category: correctness

Description: The digest assembly algorithm selects ADRs by spec-id/topic heuristics instead of starting from the ADRs explicitly referenced by matched concerns and project overrides. That breaks the documented Spike/POC -> ADR -> Concern -> Context Digest chain and can omit the rationale that FEAT-006 requires to be injected.
Suggested fix: Update the assembly contract so digest ADR discovery starts from matched concern/project-override ADR refs, then optionally unions in spec-id-related ADRs as secondary context. Add the missing project-level storage for concern-selection ADR refs if needed.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<governing>FEAT-011 — DDx preserve outcomes from bounded execution attempts (for example failed required executions or ratchet regressions) are not physics-level conflicts. They terminate the current DDx-managed attempt and hand control back to HELIX for escalation, follow-on beads, or user input.</governing>
</context-digest>

Sparse intake request: "design a CRM".

Interpretation at autonomy=medium: the user wants HELIX to seed governed planning work for a new CRM product design effort.

Conflict detected during artifact traversal: this repository's higher-authority frame artifacts define HELIX itself as the product (`docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md`, `workflows/README.md`). Creating CRM vision/PRD/design artifacts directly in this repo would therefore contradict the current product scope unless the request is intended as one of the following:
- a separate CRM project/repository that should use HELIX
- a HELIX example/demo artifact pack about CRM design
- a reinterpretation into HELIX product work rather than a CRM product

Deterministic next step: obtain the target-project decision before creating downstream CRM frame or design beads.
