acceptance: skills/helix/SKILL.md §Evolve names: ddx.id + ddx.depends_on read; forward and reverse graph walk; filesystem-only fallback when frontmatter absent; authority-ordered update sequence; explicit conflict surfacing. grep -nE '(ddx |helix [a-z])' skills/helix/SKILL.md still returns zero hits in the normative body (DDx execute-bead history footnote excepted).
description:
The evolve route currently says 'thread a requirement through the artifact stack' but doesn't describe how to walk the graph. PRD R-2 + R-4 require that HELIX artifacts carry ddx:depends_on relationships (the instance graph lives in instance-doc depends_on lists per the schema bead helix-f063d395), and that the skill body work without any specific runtime present.

Tighten the Evolve contract in skills/helix/SKILL.md to specify:

1. Read the instance frontmatter of the entry artifact; collect ddx.id and ddx.depends_on.
2. Walk the depends_on graph forward (artifacts this one depends on — authority above) and reverse (artifacts that depend on this one — downstream impact) by scanning all governing artifacts for matching ddx.id references.
3. When ddx: frontmatter is absent, fall back to filesystem traversal: phase-numbered directories provide authority order; artifact-type directories provide the type relationships.
4. Order updates by authority (vision > PRD > FEAT > stories > ADR/SD > TD > TP > impl plans > code).
5. Surface conflicts explicitly when a downstream artifact contradicts an updated upstream.

Acceptance ties: the contract must read as runtime-neutral; no 'ddx ' or 'helix ' command appears in the normative body. Both pathways (graph present, graph absent) are named.

title: Add ddx:depends_on graph-threading contract to evolve mode (degrades without DDx)

acceptance:
1. TestResponsiveAnnotations: each wireframe directory under docs/design/wireframes/ has a corresponding *-responsive.png or annotation indicating narrow-viewport stack behavior.
2. TestKanbanMobile: docs/design/wireframes/views/pipeline-kanban-mobile.png shows the list fallback for kanban on mobile.
3. TestA11yChecklist: docs/design/accessibility-checklist.md exists and documents color contrast, ARIA labels, and keyboard navigation.
4. TestA11yFlagged: docs/design/accessibility-checklist.md explicitly marks each item as MVP-flagged or implemented.
5. Gates: `go test ./docs/design/...` artifact-presence tests pass; `lefthook run pre-commit` succeeds.
description:
PROBLEM: Mobile responsiveness and accessibility requirements are unspecified. Without explicit responsive behavior and an a11y checklist, implementations will fail on narrow viewports and for assistive tech.

ROOT CAUSE: docs/crm.prd does not enumerate breakpoint behavior or a11y requirements at the wireframe level; no responsive/a11y artifacts exist under docs/design/.

PROPOSED FIX: Annotate wireframes from 006a-006d with breakpoint behavior (stack layout for narrow viewports; kanban switches to list on mobile). Produce docs/design/accessibility-checklist.md covering color contrast, ARIA labels, and keyboard navigation. Flag a11y items as MVP-flagged vs. implemented (per parent AC 7: not necessarily implemented in MVP, but flagged).

NON-SCOPE: Implementation of a11y in code (per parent AC 7, MVP only flags items); full design system tokens; native mobile apps; backend changes for responsive behavior.

Governing artifacts: depends on CRM-DESIGN-006a, 006b, 006c, 006d (annotations apply to their outputs); crm.prd and API contract per parent.
title: CRM-DESIGN-006e: Responsive Layouts & Accessibility Checklist

acceptance: helix run parses execute-loop --once --json output, applies post-cycle bookkeeping to results[].bead_id, does not use HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE
description:
Refactor helix run to parse execute-loop --json instead of selecting beads.

Design decisions captured in Phase 0 (helix-9a573737):
- CONTRACT-001 updated with execute-loop JSON schema (bead_id, status, result_rev, retry_after)
- HELIX owns queue curation; DDx owns selection and execution
- Epic focus: use execution-eligible=false on non-child beads
- Drift: pre-execution concern caught at helix check time
- Review/alignment: injected as regular beads, not post-cycle hooks
- Retry/backoff: deleted from HELIX, DDx handles via execute-loop-retry-after
- Performance metadata: every execution produces {new_state, perf_metadata} for optimization

Current code: scripts/helix run_loop() at ~line 3290 calls helix_select_ready_issue() 
→ sets HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE → runs execute-loop → applies post-cycle to .

New code: call execute-loop --once --json → parse results[].bead_id → apply post-cycle to parsed bead.

See: workflows/EXECUTION.md updated architecture section.
See: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-ddx-helix-boundary.md Queue Curation Policy.
notes: Cancelled 2026-05-21: pre-collapse stale work item (parent helix-e26584d7 was cancelled earlier; refers to scripts/helix, execute-loop, helix run, HELIX_SELECTED_ISSUE, or tests/helix-cli.sh — all removed in commit 823aa1ac).
title: Refactor helix run to parse execute-loop --json instead of selecting beads

acceptance: Run helix run --agent claude --summary --max-cycles 3 against ddx repo with 3 ready beads. After completion: all 3 beads remain closed. No bead reverts to open. Blocker report shows 0 intractable issues.
description: helix run closes beads via the agent, but between cycles beads revert from closed to open. The ddx bead JSONL store locking is sound (chaos tests prove it). The bug is in scripts/helix run loop: after the agent closes a bead and commits, the next cycle startup reclaims or reopens it. Likely cause: the run loop reads beads.jsonl, caches bead state, dispatches agent, then writes back the stale cached state — clobbering the agent's close. Fix: re-read beads.jsonl fresh after each agent call returns. Never cache bead state across cycle boundaries.
title: Fix helix run bead-state-drift: closed beads revert to open between cycles

In scope: docs and generated website content required for this architecture. Out of scope: changing runtime behavior or adding new public helix-* skills.

acceptance: Every top-level page and generated artifact/reference page has been reviewed in browser screenshots. Homepage explains the double-helix workflow and catalog correctly. Platforms page distinguishes DDx, Claude, Codex, Databricks, and manual/mixed use. Artifact and skill pages are discoverable from navigation or clear landing-page paths. Driving research and worked artifacts are linked from the narrative. Visual/card systems are consistent across the site. Information architecture supports clear paths for learning the methodology, browsing artifacts, choosing a platform, and applying HELIX. Pages meet high visual-quality standards for typography, spacing, hierarchy, contrast, interaction states, responsive layout, and avoidance of generic/card-heavy clutter. Follow-up beads exist for substantial gaps not completed in this pass.
description:
Goal: make the entire HELIX microsite reviewable and coherent end-to-end, not just the homepage. Audit every public page for content accuracy, visual presentation, navigation, and methodological coverage. Ensure the core HELIX methodology, governing artifacts, artifact-type catalog, installed/public skills, platform integrations, driving research, and HELIX self-governing artifacts are all represented with clear relationships and no DDx-overdependency. High visual quality and information architecture are explicit goals: evaluate each page against modern homepage/documentation best practices, accessibility expectations, responsive behavior, scannable content hierarchy, clear user journeys, and a coherent visual system.

Genie has no chat API, so the test scenario combines headless install verification with a manual browser procedure. Files:
- `tests/install/genie/install.py` — thin wrapper calling `scripts/install-genie.py`.
- `tests/install/genie/verify.py` — thin wrapper calling `scripts/verify-genie.py`.
- `tests/install/genie/test-procedure.md` — step-by-step manual procedure for the browser verification, including the three prompts from docs/install/databricks-genie.md §5.
- `tests/install/genie/screencast-template.md` — what the captured screencast must show and in what order.
- `tests/install/genie/recordings/` — output directory (gitignored except for a `.gitkeep`).
title: Author tests/install/genie/ scenario: install.py, verify.py, manual procedure, recordings/

<context-digest>

<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema match via areas:all; e2e-playwright does not match this workflow/testing slice because its concern areas are ui,site</concerns>
<practices>Tracker-only measure/report commits must preserve the executed implementation SHA on the closed bead so later review and archaeology can resolve the correct diff without guessing from git history. Deterministic wrapper tests should cover both helix commit and automated run-loop close paths.</practices>
<governing>.ddx/beads.jsonl; workflows/actions/fresh-eyes-review.md; scripts/helix; tests/helix-cli.sh</governing>
</context-digest>
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:22
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Tracker-only commit d5f4cfa ("helix-1356be0a: record measure and close bead") closed helix-1356be0a and preserved its <measure-results>, but ddx bead show helix-1356be0a --json still returns an empty closing_commit_sha even though the implementation commit is a2143e77f9fd0b0200e7351a36c9800b92373af2. The automated fresh-eyes-review action explicitly prefers an executed bead's closing_commit_sha when tracker bookkeeping lands after the code commit; without it, the run loop has to fall back to git archaeology and can target the wrong diff. Existing helix commit code and deterministic tests already treat this field as required, so the current measure/report close path has regressed relative to the workflow contract.
Suggested fix: Update the measure/report tracker-close path so tracker-only close commits preserve the executed implementation SHA on the bead, repair helix-1356be0a, and add deterministic coverage for the exact record-measure-and-close path that produced d5f4cfa.

description:
<context-digest>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | product-microsite-ia | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Define the primary reader modes before defining the navigation tree. · Reader modes: evaluators, new users, active HELIX users, and contributors · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits. · ADR-004 Artifact Dependencies Are Encoded in meta.yml.relationships Only (No Separate dependencies.yaml) — Type-level validation (required_sections, quality_checks, Why: The catalog must have a single source of truth for artifact-type dependencies. The rubric must stop asking for a file the catalog does not produce. Any consumer that currently mentions dependencies.yaml must be repointed at meta.yml so the rubric and the workflow agree. · ADR-005 Concern practices.md is HELIX-activity-keyed — Every concern's practices.md organizes its per-activity content under HELIX activity headings: Discover, Frame, Design, Test, Build, Deploy, Iterate. Topic-keyed or quality-gates-keyed organization is no longer acceptable for practices.md. Why: Per-activity context-digest assembly must be a deterministic lookup, not a heuristic. The concerns library must use one consistent organizing principle so that any consumer — context assembler, alignment review, methodology docs — extracts the practices for a given activity by reading the corresponding heading.</adrs>
<governing>PLAN-2026-06-11-FLOW-ARCHITECTURE</governing>
</context-digest>

Update docs/helix/02-design/design-2026-06-04-helix-family-marker-and-linkages.md with a surgical supersession note. Preserve useful marker/schema/link material: flows:, instance:, cross_flow_edges:, external_edges, and helix_version strict-link semantics. Retract or mark historical only the sibling public-skill/flow interpretation, HELIX-public env-var routing, HELIX-public CLI interpretations, nonexistent validator/schema references, and current-looking sibling architecture claims. Clarify that runtime-owned --flow/--methodology flags, if present, are not HELIX public product surface. Out of scope: rewriting the whole historical design, changing validators, changing SKILL.md, or turning this dated design into a status tracker.
title: Supersede stale family marker public-surface claims

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>scripts/validate_deploy_contract_docs.py — def build_surface_specs(args: argparse.Namespace) -> list[SurfaceSpec]: return [ SurfaceSpec( label="deploy README", path=args.deploy_readme, required_fragment=( "Deploy artifacts are project-specific, but current HELIX still " "treats four deploy surfaces as first-class in the live contract: " "deployment-checklist, monitoring-setup, runbook, and " "release-notes." ), ), SurfaceSpec( label="deploy enforcer", path=args.deploy_enforcer, required_fragment=( "Deploy artifacts are project-specific, but HELIX treats four " "deploy outputs as the live contract under " "docs/helix/05-deploy/:" ), ), SurfaceSpec( label="artifacts glossary", path=args.glossary, required_fragment=( "Deploy artifacts cover four first-class surfaces in the current " "HELIX contract: deployment-checklist, monitoring-setup, " "runbook, and release-notes." ), ), ]</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/validate_deploy_contract_docs.py:69
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: validate_surface() only checks that each surface still contains the canonical four-artifact fragment and that all four artifact names appear somewhere in the file. If stale three-artifact wording is reintroduced elsewhere in the same document while the canonical paragraph remains intact, the validator still exits 0. Reproduction from this review: copy workflows/phases/05-deploy/README.md, append "Deploy artifacts are project-specific, but current HELIX still treats three deploy surfaces as first-class in the live contract: `deployment-checklist`, `monitoring-setup`, and `runbook`." to the copy, then run python3 scripts/validate_deploy_contract_docs.py --deploy-readme <copy> --deploy-enforcer <enforcer> --glossary <glossary>; it prints "validated deploy contract docs: 3 surfaces" and exits successfully. That violates helix-39fc1526 acceptance, which requires the deterministic lane to fail when any reviewed surface reintroduces stale three-artifact wording.
Suggested fix: Extend the docs validator and its regression lane to detect stale three-artifact wording even when the canonical paragraph is still present elsewhere, so contradictory deploy-contract text cannot slip through by coexistence.

acceptance:
All 5 children closed. Each runtime has:
- tests/workflows/<runtime>/run-scenarios.sh exits 0 with runtime credentials present (creds mounted, not a specific API-key env var).
- An L2 activation assertion backed by a negative control: running the same scenario with the skill uninstalled exits NONZERO. No child gates solely on a free-text keyword grep.
- Chat-capable runtimes: tests/workflows/<runtime>/evals/routing.jsonl present and exercised.
- tests/workflows/<runtime>/recordings/ with a committed recording (gif/cast/webm).
- CI: PR CI runs L1 (verify-skill-layout) only; tag-push CI runs the functional harness with creds.
- docs/install/<runtime>.md links the recording as a screencast.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Validate Your Work · Runtime Neutrality · Design for Simplicity</principles>
<concerns>installer-coverage | screencast-evidence | runtime-parity | activation-signal-rigor</concerns>
<practices>L1 packaging static check + L2 structured-activation signal + L3 routing evals; negative control mandatory (skill-uninstalled run must FAIL); evidence recording (vhs gif / Playwright webm) is not the gate; never bare keyword grep as the sole assertion</practices>
<governing>docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-014-helix-workflow-coverage.md · docs/install/<runtime>.md · child beads INT-CC/INT-CX/INT-CP/INT-GN/INT-DD</governing>
</context-digest>

Operationalize "HELIX installs easily, works as a skill, and the basic workflow works" as five per-runtime integration tests, each producing a screencast for the install docs and release notes.

**Test contract (v2 — supersedes prose-grep).** A test must assert that the HELIX skill *actually activated*, using the strongest signal the runtime exposes — never a free-text keyword grep of the model's prose. Rationale: a model will mention "frame"/"align"/"review" without the skill ever loading. Verified 2026-05-22 that `claude -p "/helix frame ..."` answered by improvising with subagents while the helix Skill tool never fired — a prose grep would have falsely PASSED.

Layers every child implements:
- L1 Packaging (static, PR CI): tests/install/shared/verify-skill-layout.sh (already exists).
- L2 Activation (functional, creds-gated): prove the skill engaged via a structured/behavioral signal — claude-code/codex parse the structured event stream (`--output-format stream-json` / codex equivalent) for a tool-use event naming the `helix` skill; ddx uses `ddx work --once --json` routing decision + execution-evidence artifacts; copilot/genie use a structured exit/DOM signal where the surface exposes one. Every L2 assertion MUST be backed by a negative control: the same scenario with the skill uninstalled must FAIL. A test that still passes without the skill installed is invalid — that is the prose-grep failure mode. No bare keyword grep as the sole gate.
- L3 Routing evals (deterministic): chat-capable runtimes ship tests/workflows/<runtime>/evals/routing.jsonl (phrase -> expected mode), mirroring .claude/skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl.
- Evidence (not a gate): a committed recording (vhs gif / Playwright webm). TUI/PTY realism belongs here, not in the assertion. Do NOT drive the runtime via tmux/PTY screen-scraping for the pass/fail gate.

**Auth.** Tests use the operator's real runtime credentials mounted into the container (claude/codex OAuth token files, GITHUB_TOKEN, Genie DBAUTH cookie) — NOT a hard dependency on a specific API-key env var. PR CI runs L1 only; tag-push functional CI runs L2+L3 with creds present.

**Scope per child** (narrow for v1; full TP-014 coverage is FEAT-014's separate scope):
1. Install verification — runtime CLI starts, skill is discoverable (L1)
2. Skill activation — proven via L2 structured signal + negative control
3. One end-to-end workflow scenario — TP-014 SCN-01 (sparse intent -> vision + PRD)
4. Recording captured and committed

**Out of scope** (defer to FEAT-014): other TP-014 scenarios; cross-runtime parity benchmarking; new skill modes.

**Why these 5 children exist** (vs reusing the 25 phantom-closed FEAT-014 children): those 25 closed under prior worker runs whose commits were in a local divergence no longer on origin/main; the artifacts they claimed don't exist on disk. Re-doing the work cleanly here is simpler than reopening 25 stale beads.
title: Epic: per-runtime integration tests (install + skill + one workflow) with screencast capture

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>84e05d418c8004d736606d54c3f93d800c79f09b</governing>
</context-digest>

description:
condense_codex_output() in scripts/helix (lines ~525-620) is a bash reimplementation of output filtering that belongs in DDx's agent service (ddx-205af2f4). Once DDx implements the output condensing filter in ddx exec run, HELIX should:

1. Remove condense_codex_output() from scripts/helix entirely
2. Rely on ddx exec run's condensed output instead of filtering in bash
3. Update run_agent_prompt() codex branch to consume DDx-filtered output
4. Update tests that depend on condense_codex_output behavior

The current bash filter keeps: helix: progress lines, $ tool calls, errors, issue IDs, markdown headings/tables, phase markers. Drops: raw diffs, codex boilerplate, bulk verbose output. This logic should move to DDx.

Depends on: ddx-205af2f4 (DDx agent output condensing filter)
title: Port condense_codex_output to DDx agent output filter and remove from helix

acceptance:
1. Published install smoke scenarios use shell/interpreter assumptions that match their scripts; no /bin/sh [[ incompatibility remains.
2. DDx published-install scenario installs or locates ddx before invoking it, or is explicitly skipped with a documented, non-failing reason when DDx cannot be installed in CI.
3. Claude Code scenario distinguishes credential-required functional checks from static package/layout checks and does not fail solely because repository secrets are absent unless TEST_FUNCTIONAL/required-auth mode is enabled.
4. Codex CLI scenario validates the actual current plugin/skill install layout and passes for the published HELIX package.
5. The Install Smoke (real published install) workflow passes on a tag or documents intentional skips in the job summary with zero failed scenarios.
description: The tag-triggered Install Smoke (real published install) workflow failed on v0.7.0 (run 27485679165) and has also failed on v0.6.1, v0.6.0, and v0.5.2, so this is a pre-existing release-smoke gap rather than a v0.7.0 packaging regression. Current v0.7.0 failures: ddx scenario reports /usr/local/bin/install.sh: line 9: ddx: command not found plus /bin/sh: [[: not found; claude-code scenario refuses to claim PASS without auth; codex-cli scenario reports /root/.codex/skills/helix/ missing. Copilot passes and Genie skips without credentials. Tighten the workflow/scenario contracts so published install checks are meaningful, deterministic, and correctly skipped when credentials are intentionally absent.
title: Fix published install smoke workflow failures

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tests/validate-skills.sh:94
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new validate_helix_triage_intro regex only matches blanket priming when the quantifier is followed immediately by a bare noun like issue/issues/bead/beads/task/tasks/work item(s). In a disposable repo copy, I inserted the sentence 'All implementation issues should enter the tracker ready to execute when possible.' ahead of the current helix-triage intro and ran HELIX_VALIDATE_SKILLS_REPO_ROOT=<temp> HELIX_VALIDATE_SKILLS_SKIP_REGRESSION=1 bash tests/validate-skills.sh; the validator still exited 0. That leaves the same semantic regression possible as long as the blanket priming adds a qualifier such as implementation between 'all' and 'issues'.
Suggested fix: Broaden the intro validation so it rejects blanket execution-ready priming even when adjectives or qualifiers appear between the quantifier and noun, or switch the regression fixture to compare against a more explicit allowlist/blocklist over the normalized intro text.

<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix-1a4f3ae1</governing>
</context-digest>

2. Sloptimizer guidance or adapter text is updated so public website copy review includes brand voice/reader-context checks, not only AI-writing mechanics.
3. Homepage, Use HELIX, Skills, Platforms, Multiple Flows, Getting Started, and generated artifact banners no longer use unexplained internal phrases such as 'adopter doctrine', 'dogfood corpus', 'generated area', 'source traceability', or bare 'reusable catalog'.
4. Static validation fails on forbidden public-copy phrases and includes positive checks for novice-readable artifact catalog and example-doc wording.
5. Run sloptimizer strict audit on changed hand-authored public pages; run redundancy audit on changed hand-authored public pages; run Claude review of changed copy against the finalized voice guide and record/fold in findings; run bash tests/validate-microsite-doctrine.sh, bash tests/validate-website-generated.sh, just test, cd website && npm run test:e2e, and git diff --check.
description: Improve HELIX public website copy for intelligent novice readers. Current problem: public pages use precise internal language such as 'adopter doctrine', 'dogfood corpus', 'routing skill', 'runtime-neutral', 'artifact discipline', and 'the reusable catalog' before defining what those terms mean. This makes the site read like internal architecture notes instead of a product website. In scope: docs/website/CLAUDE.md or a linked public voice guide, sloptimizer guidance needed to respect site brand/reader context, hand-authored public pages under docs/website/content (_index.md, why/, use/, skills/, platforms/, install/, reference/ as needed), generated artifact banner wording via scripts/publish-artifacts.py, website tests/ratchets for voice terms, and regenerated generated content when the generator changes. Out of scope: changing HELIX methodology specs, changing the core helix skill contract, redesigning site layout/CSS except tests that must follow copy changes, or rewriting generated artifact source docs under docs/helix/. Requirement: pages/modules/cards must stand mostly on their own for a smart reader with no HELIX context; introduce or link terms before shorthand; make the argument and next action clear.
title: Define and apply HELIX website voice

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>FEAT-005 — implementation_prompt() { local selector="${1:-}" local context_file="${target_root}/.helix/context.md" cat <<EOF $(if [[ -f "$context_file" ]]; then cat "$context_file"; printf '\n---\n\n'; fi) Execute the following HELIX implementation action. $(inline_action "${helix_docs_root}/actions/implementation.md") Use supporting references at ${helix_docs_root}/README.md and ${helix_docs_root}/phases/04-build/enforcer.md as needed. Execute in the current repository rooted at ${target_root}. Issue tracker: built-in JSONL at ${tracker_file} (legacy: .ddx/issues.jsonl). Use \ddx bead ready --json\, \ddx bead show <id>\, \ddx bead update <id> --claim\, \ddx bead close <id>\. Concurrency rule: re-read the selected issue immediately before claim and immediately before close. If tracker metadata drifted materially, do not claim or close from stale assumptions; re-check instead. ${selector:+Implementation target: ${selector}.} CRITICAL: If tests fail after your changes, fix them. 99% of test failures are caused by your changes, not by pre-existing breakage. Do not blame external causes, do not skip tests, do not commit with failing tests. Fix the code or fix the test. Retry up to 3 times before giving up. Never commit broken work. Follow the action exactly. Do the work — do not just describe what you would do. Session log: ${helix_log} EOF }</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:2631
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new rollback path snapshots the entire repo-local tracker file before calling `tracker_update`, then restores that full snapshot with `cp -f "$tracker_snapshot" "$tracker_file"` when the bookkeeping commit fails. In a repository that explicitly supports concurrent local supervision, that restore can silently erase unrelated tracker writes that land between snapshot creation and the failed commit attempt. A minimal file-level reproduction shows the problem: snapshot JSONL with beads A=open/B=open, apply the closing_commit_sha update to A, then apply an unrelated concurrent update that closes B, and finally restore the snapshot; B reverts back to open. That violates ADR-002's requirement to detect or prevent silent lost updates and makes the new failure-handling path unsafe under overlapping local tracker mutations.
Suggested fix: Do not roll back by copying the whole tracker snapshot back over the live file. Instead, use a conflict-aware rollback that only reverts the specific bead field mutation when the tracker still matches the expected pre-commit state, or fail closed and surface the conflict when intervening tracker writes are detected. Add a deterministic regression that injects an intervening tracker mutation during the sync-failure path and proves HELIX does not clobber it.

description:
PROBLEM: After B6 adds data-prd (01-frame), data-architecture (02-design), and data-quality-expectations (03-test) as first-class artifact types, the Genie bundle must be rebuilt so these new types are packaged into the deliverable artifacts before any workspace install or live e2e can validate them.

ROOT CAUSE: scripts/build-genie-bundle.sh:1 packages the activity directory tree at build time into the bundle; without a rebuild post-B6 the existing bundle still reflects pre-B6 state. scripts/verify-genie.py:1 enumerates types from the bundled artifacts and SKILL.md §Catalog Resolution table; running it offline confirms presence without needing a workspace round-trip.

PROPOSED FIX: Run bash scripts/build-genie-bundle.sh to regenerate the bundle from the current tree; then run python scripts/verify-genie.py and assert it enumerates all three new types in their respective activity catalogs. Capture verifier stdout for the sidecar in B7c.

NON-SCOPE: workspace upload (B7b); live Playwright drive against Databricks (B7c); editing template content or SKILL.md §Catalog Resolution inline table (Phase G upstream); retry/rollback logic; non-Databricks platform substitution.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading)</practices>

Treat closure of the story's deploy issue as the deterministic ITERATE threshold. Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/state-rules.yml:42
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new story-level iterate artifact pattern is the shared directory `docs/helix/06-iterate/`. Under the same file, phase detection still uses `highest-artifact`, so once the iterate directory exists the rule can satisfy the iterate phase for every story regardless of whether that story has any iterate evidence. This collapses per-story state detection and can make partially completed stories appear to have reached ITERATE simply because the project has shared iterate docs.
Suggested fix: Remove the static iterate directory pattern from story-level state detection and make iterate determination query-only, or introduce a story-keyed evidence pattern that cannot match unrelated stories.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: tests/validate-skills.sh:299
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new regression guard only checks for one exact sentence ("Every issue should enter the tracker ready to execute.") plus a few required substrings. In a disposable repo copy, I inserted the equivalent blanket priming sentence "Every issue should enter the tracker ready for execution when possible." ahead of the current intro in skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md, and bash tests/validate-skills.sh still exited 0. That means the validator does not actually satisfy helix-1356be0a's acceptance criterion that it fails when blanket execution-ready wording returns; semantically equivalent contradictory priming can ship undetected as long as the required new phrases remain somewhere in the file.
Suggested fix: Strengthen the validator so it checks the triage intro block semantically enough to reject equivalent blanket execution-ready claims, not only the exact old sentence. Keep the positive assertion that the intro scopes readiness to implementation beads and routes vague work to planning/polish or explicit not-execution-ready paths.
notes:
<measure-results>
  <timestamp>2026-04-11T10:27:35Z</timestamp>
  <status>PASS</status>
  <acceptance>
    <criterion name="blanket-execution-ready-priming-regression" status="pass" evidence="tests/validate-skills.sh now validates the helix-triage intro block semantically and self-tests a disposable copy with blanket priming."/>
    <criterion name="in-repo-triage-intro-still-passes" status="pass" evidence="bash tests/validate-skills.sh exited 0 with the in-repo skills/helix-triage/SKILL.md unchanged."/>
  </acceptance>
  <gates>
    <gate concern="testing" command="bash tests/validate-skills.sh" status="pass"/>
    <gate concern="repo" command="git diff --check" status="pass"/>
  </gates>
  <ratchets></ratchets>
</measure-results>
title: correctness: validate-skills misses equivalent blanket triage priming

acceptance: workflows/artifact-schema.md (or equivalent) exists. It defines meta.yml fields, instance ddx: frontmatter fields, ID and directory conventions, and a consumer table. The doc treats DDx as one (reference) consumer, not as the schema authority. A second consumer (manual operation, a Databricks substrate experiment, etc.) could in principle be built from the spec alone.
description:
HELIX defines a catalog of artifact types (Vision, PRD, FEAT, US, ADR, SD, TD, TP, deploy and iterate outputs) with type-level metadata (meta.yml) and instance-level frontmatter (the ddx: block). Today this schema is implicit — it lives in the union of meta.yml files, template frontmatters, and what DDx's artifacttypes loader happens to consume. There is no canonical document a non-DDx substrate could read to implement HELIX compliantly.

Scope: write a substrate-neutral schema spec (location: workflows/artifact-schema.md or similar) that defines:
1. The artifact-type metadata shape (meta.yml) — every field, what it means, which are required.
2. The instance frontmatter shape (the ddx: block in markdown artifacts) — fields, semantics, the depends_on graph model.
3. The conventions HELIX assumes (ID naming, directory layout, phase progression).
4. Which fields each known consumer reads — DDx today as the reference implementation; future substrates as they appear.

Ratify the ddx: namespace as the open frontmatter schema HELIX adopts (not as a DDx-coupling but as the shared schema DDx ships and HELIX consumes). Future substrates are encouraged to read the same shape.

This is a documentation bead, not a code change. Its output is the spec other consumers and contributors read.
notes: Promoted: this is the substrate-neutral artifact schema spec non-DDx consumers need.
title: Document HELIX's artifact-type schema as an open, substrate-neutral spec

acceptance:
1. TestOpenAPISpecParses — loads docs/api/openapi.yaml with kin-openapi and asserts openapi == "3.0.x".
2. TestContactsCRUDPaths — asserts POST /contacts, GET /contacts, GET /contacts/{id}, PUT /contacts/{id}, DELETE /contacts/{id} all present with declared responses.
3. TestAccountsCRUDPaths — same five operations on /accounts.
4. TestLeadsCRUDPaths — same five operations on /leads.
5. TestOpportunitiesCRUDPaths — same five operations on /opportunities.
6. TestActivitiesCRUDPaths — same five operations on /activities.
7. TestEntitySchemasDefined — components.schemas contains Contact, Account, Lead, Opportunity, Activity with required fields matching CRM-DESIGN-002.
8. Gate: `go test ./api/spec/...` passes.
9. Gate: `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
description:
PROBLEM: No machine-readable API contract exists for the five core CRM entities (contacts, accounts, leads, opportunities, activities); downstream implementation beads cannot be scoped without it.

ROOT CAUSE: docs/api/openapi.yaml:1 — file does not exist; no CRUD path definitions or entity schemas are authored anywhere in the repo (depends on CRM-DESIGN-002 data design and ADR-001 framework choice).

PROPOSED FIX: Create docs/api/openapi.yaml as an OpenAPI 3.0 document. Under paths add POST /contacts, GET /contacts, GET /contacts/{id}, PUT /contacts/{id}, DELETE /contacts/{id}; repeat for /accounts, /leads, /opportunities, /activities. Under components.schemas add Contact, Account, Lead, Opportunity, Activity referencing the CRM-DESIGN-002 data model. Each operation MUST declare requestBody (where applicable) and responses.

NON-SCOPE: /pipeline, /search, /import, /export endpoints (sibling CRM-DESIGN-003b). /auth/signup, /auth/login, /auth/logout (sibling CRM-DESIGN-003c). ErrorResponse schema body, pagination parameter contract, per-endpoint example payloads (sibling CRM-DESIGN-003d). Implementation of handlers. Authentication middleware design.
title: CRM-DESIGN-003a: OpenAPI spec — core entity CRUD endpoints

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix.workflow — HELIX enforces Test-Driven Development (TDD) through a structured phase approach where tests are written BEFORE implementation. This ensures quality is built-in from the start and specifications are executable. Human creativity and AI capabilities collaborate throughout, with tests serving as the contract between design and implementation.</governing>
</context-digest>

Review repo-scope workflow bundle docs, phase enforcers, and artifact-facing documentation against the authoritative planning stack. Focus on deploy-contract wording, artifact-directory scope handling, and deterministic validation coverage for workflow docs.

title: Alignment review: repo - workflow bundle and artifact docs

acceptance: The story-level iterate contract handles multiple phase:deploy issues deterministically. Either HELIX documents one canonical deploy bead per story, or the state rule/query explicitly requires all matching deploy issues closed and zero open deploy issues before a story enters ITERATE.
description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading)</practices>
<governing>workflows/state-rules.yml — iterate: type: iterate-evidence phase: iterate query_only: true query_command: "Inspect completed tracker issues labeled helix, phase:deploy, story:US-{id}. Treat closure of the story's deploy issue as the deterministic ITERATE threshold. Linked tracker follow-on work is supporting evidence when present; canonical iterate outputs remain shared iteration context and are not queried by story ID."</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: workflows/state-rules.yml:46
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new iterate query says to inspect completed tracker issues labeled `helix, phase:deploy, story:US-{id}` and then treats closure of the story's deploy issue as the deterministic ITERATE threshold. Elsewhere in the same contract, HELIX still allows deploy issue(s) per story (for example `transitions.story.build_to_deploy`, `transitions.story.deploy_to_iterate`, `workflows/state-machine.yaml`, and `workflows/artifact-hierarchy.md` all use plural `issue(s)`). Without an explicit rule that no open `phase:deploy` issues may remain, the story can be classified as ITERATE as soon as one deploy bead closes even if another deploy bead for the same story is still open.
Suggested fix: Define whether a story has exactly one canonical deploy bead or, if multiple deploy beads are allowed, update the iterate query/rule to require all matching `phase:deploy` issues to be closed and no open deploy issue to remain before the story is in ITERATE.

<measure-results>
  <timestamp>2026-04-10T17:49:27Z</timestamp>
  <status>PASS</status>
  <acceptance>
    <criterion name='iterate-query-requires-all-deploy-issues-closed' status='pass' evidence='workflows/state-rules.yml now treats ITERATE as reachable only when all matching phase:deploy issues are complete and no open deploy issue remains.'/>
    <criterion name='story-level-contract-is-consistent-across-docs' status='pass' evidence='workflows/state-machine.yaml, workflows/artifact-hierarchy.md, and workflows/phases/06-iterate/README.md now use the same multi-deploy threshold wording.'/>
    <criterion name='validator-catches-regression' status='pass' evidence='tests/validate-state-rules.sh now asserts the iterate query inspects deploy issues by story label and requires all matching deploy issues complete with no open deploy issue remaining.'/>
  </acceptance>
  <gates>
    <gate concern='repo' command='bash tests/validate-state-rules.sh' status='pass'/>
    <gate concern='repo' command='git diff --check' status='pass'/>
  </gates>
  <ratchets />
</measure-results>
title: correctness: make deploy-to-iterate detection unambiguous when multiple deploy beads exist

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts:1
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The FEAT-007 verification lane is green, but the Playwright suite still does not exercise the user-facing search workflow at all. `rg` over `website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts` shows page-load, navigation, and screenshot coverage only; there are no search input interactions or assertions on result routing. That violates the active `e2e-playwright` concern practice that every user-facing workflow have at least one end-to-end test, and it is why the broken `/glossary` search route in the rendered index was able to ship undetected in commit e61a4f6.
Suggested fix: Add a Playwright scenario that opens site search, queries at least glossary and CLI content, asserts the result URLs are valid, and verifies navigation succeeds when a result is selected.

acceptance: Every workflows/phases/*/artifacts/*/template.md opens with a ddx: frontmatter block. The block contains at minimum an id field with either a placeholder pattern (XXX) or a fixed slug for singletons. No helix-specific blocks are inlined. A walking script confirms uniform shape.
description:
template.md files across HELIX artifact directories vary in their opening frontmatter. Some carry just an id placeholder, some carry nothing, and a recent product-vision experiment briefly inlined large helix-specific blocks. The result: authors who copy a template do not get a consistent starting frontmatter, and instance documents created from these templates have inconsistent frontmatter shapes.

Scope: every workflows/phases/*/artifacts/*/template.md opens with a minimal ddx: frontmatter block carrying the id pattern an instance should fill in. For example FEAT templates open with 'id: FEAT-XXX' (a placeholder the author replaces on instantiation); root-level singletons like product-vision open with their fixed id ('id: helix.product-vision'). Body content unchanged.

HELIX uses the ddx: namespace as the open schema for instance frontmatter — DDx ships it, HELIX adopts it, other substrates are free to consume the same shape. This bead is about consistency of that frontmatter shape across templates. Type-level metadata (description, output location, prompts, validation rules) belongs in meta.yml or prompt.md, not template.md frontmatter — see helix-ada6e67e for that work.

notes: Folded into runtime-neutral collapse critical path: standardize instance frontmatter as an open HELIX artifact convention.
title: Standardize template.md instance frontmatter shape across HELIX artifact templates

description:
CONTRACT-001 'Post-Cycle HELIX Behavior' table asserts DDx owns 'Build gate (pre-merge check)'. Verified on 2026-04-17 that no implementation exists: grep of ddx/cli/internal/agent/*.go for lefthook|cargo check|build-health|BuildGate returns nothing. HELIX today runs cargo check / lefthook run pre-commit post-agent-commit and git reverts on failure (scripts/helix:3629-3654).

Two possible resolutions, chosen by the contract audit (helix-00e2486f):

Option A — DDx implementation: file a new bead in DDx's own tracker for a pre-land build-gate hook (lefthook / cargo check / repo-declared gate script) that runs before fast-forward merge and aborts the attempt with a preserved ref on failure. HELIX keeps its current gate as a belt-and-suspenders guard until DDx ships.

Option B — CONTRACT-001 amendment: amend the Post-Cycle HELIX Behavior table to move 'Build gate (pre-merge check)' from DDx-owned to HELIX-owned. Keep HELIX's existing implementation; document it in CONTRACT-001 as HELIX's responsibility.

This bead is absorbed if helix-00e2486f's audit resolves it. Otherwise, whichever option is chosen must produce a code change that makes the contract claim true.

description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization) · Identify all user-facing pages and workflows that need testing · Test location: website/e2e/microsite.spec.ts</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix.workflow — HELIX enforces Test-Driven Development (TDD) through a structured phase approach where tests are written BEFORE implementation. This ensures quality is built-in from the start and specifications are executable. Human creativity and AI capabilities collaborate throughout, with tests serving as the contract between design and implementation.</governing>
</context-digest>

acceptance:
1. helix input "design a CRM" --autonomy low dispatches a prompt containing Autonomy level: low.
2. helix input --autonomy high "design a CRM" still dispatches Autonomy level: high.
3. Deterministic coverage in tests/helix-cli.sh exercises both flag positions and invalid autonomy.
4. bash tests/helix-cli.sh and git diff --check pass.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: scripts/helix:3395
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: The input subcommand parser only consumes --autonomy while it appears before the request. The published contract documents the request-first form (helix input "..." --autonomy high), but the current parser breaks on the first non-flag token and silently falls back to medium. Reproduction against the current branch shows bash scripts/helix input "design a CRM" --autonomy low dispatches a prompt containing Autonomy level: medium, which violates FEAT-011 / TD-011 semantics and can skip the confirmations required by low autonomy. The new deterministic coverage in tests/helix-cli.sh only exercises the unsupported flag-first form, so this documented CLI path remains broken and untested.
Suggested fix: Parse --autonomy in both positions accepted by the documented CLI surface, reject ambiguous argument layouts, and add deterministic tests that cover both helix input --autonomy high "..." and helix input "..." --autonomy low.
notes:

    <criterion name='flag-first autonomy syntax' status='pass' evidence='bash tests/helix-cli.sh covers helix input --autonomy high "design a CRM" -> Autonomy level: high'/>
    <criterion name='invalid and ambiguous input rejection' status='pass' evidence='bash tests/helix-cli.sh covers invalid autonomy, duplicate --autonomy, and ambiguous extra args'/>
    <criterion name='diff hygiene' status='pass' evidence='git diff --check'/>

1. TestJustWorkflowTestStaticExitsZero: `just workflow-test` exits 0 on a clean checkout with no secrets configured.
2. TestJustTestIncludesWorkflowStatic: `just test` invokes the workflow-test recipe as part of its dependency graph.
3. TestWorkflowTestSkipsFunctionalScenarios: scenarios tagged functional are skipped (not executed) when TEST_FUNCTIONAL is unset.
4. TestWorkflowTestNoNetworkOrSecretsRequired: recipe completes without requiring auth secrets or outbound network calls beyond Docker-image base layers already cached for static checks.

description:
PROBLEM: No just recipe exposes the static portion of the workflow harness, so PR-gating CI cannot run workflow static checks on every PR.

ROOT CAUSE: justfile — no `workflow-test` recipe and no inclusion of it in the existing `test` aggregate target.

PROPOSED FIX: Add `workflow-test` recipe to justfile that invokes tests/workflows/run-all.sh with TEST_FUNCTIONAL unset (static mode). Add `workflow-test` as a dependency of the existing `test` aggregate so `just test` exercises workflow static checks. Static checks must remain free of network/auth dependencies.

NON-SCOPE: Functional/secret-gated recipe (ENT-03); harness script implementation (ENT-01); CI configuration changes outside justfile.

GOVERNING: FEAT-014 ENT-02 · justfile · tests/workflows/run-all.sh
PARENT: helix-88f790e4
DEPENDS-ON: ENT-01 harness contract
title: ENT-02: justfile workflow-test static recipe + test-target inclusion

<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>none matched for tracker-state review</concerns>

<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity, so tracker state must preserve the true implementation context across later loop steps · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — tracker metadata mutation must surface durable state explicitly rather than leaving later actions to guess</adrs>
<governing>.ddx/beads.jsonl — helix-39fc1526 is closed after tracker-only commit 97f12a77f99a7fe447cbc25314e0b398e95e8159 but still lacks closing_commit_sha for implementation commit cf609f5aa376d1f6574471e904ef6822f0a0db2c</governing>
</context-digest>

Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:77
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The automated review had to recover implementation commit cf609f5aa376d1f6574471e904ef6822f0a0db2c from git history because HEAD is the tracker-only close commit 97f12a77f99a7fe447cbc25314e0b398e95e8159 and the closed execution bead helix-39fc1526 still has no closing_commit_sha field in .ddx/beads.jsonl. The fresh-eyes-review action explicitly prefers closing_commit_sha in this situation so later review and report passes can resolve the true implementation commit without git archaeology. Leaving helix-39fc1526 closed without that pointer makes the tracker row incomplete and breaks the durable hand-off the automated close path is supposed to provide.
Suggested fix: Update the automated close bookkeeping so closing helix-39fc1526 persists closing_commit_sha=cf609f5aa376d1f6574471e904ef6822f0a0db2c, and ensure tracker-only close commits do not leave closed execution beads without their implementation-commit pointer.

<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Define the site's target audience and what content they need · Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Identify which workflows need demo reels — prioritize the "first 5 minutes" experience · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading), helix-experiment (metric-driven optimization)</practices>
<adrs>ADR-001 HELIX Supervisory Control Model — Supervision must remain live to concurrent local operator activity. A running helix-run session must treat tracker and governing-artifact changes as new control input at safe boundaries. It may not assume that the selected issue is still valid at claim time or close time just because it was valid earlier in the loop. Why: HELIX must preserve bounded execution, authority order, tracker-first work management, and direct interactive operation. It must also reduce orchestration burden by autonomously selecting the least-powerful sufficient next action when authority is available. · ADR-002 HELIX Tracker Write Safety Model — The tracker will not pretend to provide arbitrary multi-writer transactional semantics. Instead, it will define the supported local execution model, require explicit detection or prevention of silent lost updates, and make malformed tracker state a surfaced failure rather than something the rest of HELIX must guess around. Why: HELIX needs a conservative local tracker that is safe enough for agent-driven issue refinement and concurrent local supervision. The tracker must surface malformed state explicitly, define what concurrency/conflict guarantees are supported, and make metadata mutation available through first-class commands instead of direct file edits.</adrs>
<governing>helix.prd — Alignment, review, measure, and report flows must capture every actionable</governing>
</context-digest>

description: Fresh-eyes review of commit f7460c6c79509bb184cd766b5fcb0ba51c4f4d18 found that docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-03-28-repo.md labels its 'First Recommended Execution Set' as an execution set while including non-execution-safe design issue hx-e7324e4f alongside execution-safe issues hx-c2dd330a and hx-650ab26b. description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema | e2e-playwright</concerns>
<practices>Keep governing docs and microsite content in sync in the same pass · Demo scripts are executable documentation and use ddx agent run as the harness · Playwright coverage should exercise every navigable site page and workflow with video enabled</practices>
<governing>FEAT-006 — beads created or refreshed through HELIX carry current context digests or an explicit rationale for omission</governing>
</context-digest>

Follow-on from alignment review issue hx-8e7f9b24.

Review finding from reconcile-alignment.
Scope: repo open queue
Category: regression
Severity: medium
Description: FEAT-006 requires triage and evolve to prepend a <context-digest> at bead creation, and EXECUTION says triage/evolve assemble a digest into every bead they create while polish refreshes stale digests. The earlier propagation fix closed under helix-7b3c6980, but the current live queue again contains recent open beads created after that closure with no digest at all, including helix-e393791b, helix-c3ff5fdf, helix-9648ca8d, helix-ce4b9180, helix-9e38e71e, helix-a938e147, and the CRM planning beads. Concern-resolution treats a missing digest as stale propagation evidence, so the queue has regressed from the FEAT-006 contract even after the earlier refresh pass.
Suggested fix: Restore digest assembly on the creation paths that are still bypassing it, backfill or supersede the recent open beads missing digests, and add deterministic coverage that fails when HELIX-created beads in the covered paths omit the digest without an explicit rationale.

description: Repackage HELIX as a portable Agent Skills library layered under the HELIX workflow and CLI without weakening the method. Preserve the core HELIX contract: command mirroring, authority-ordered planning, bounded actions, tracker-first execution, and workflow docs as the normative source of behavior. Scope includes directory/name alignment, canonical .agents/skills packaging, standards-oriented metadata and validation, and a docs rewrite that presents skills as the portable surface and the HELIX workflow/CLI as the stricter operating layer.
design: Deliver in four ordered slices. First, rename skill directories so published names, on-disk layout, and installer references match exactly. Second, adopt project-level and user-level .agents/skills packaging as the canonical layout, with .claude/skills retained only as a temporary compatibility mirror. Third, add the minimum metadata and deterministic validation needed to keep the package internally consistent. Fourth, rewrite the docs so the portable skill surface is clear while deeper HELIX semantics remain in the workflow contract.
title: Package HELIX as standards-compliant Agent Skills library

acceptance: Repeated alignment refreshes do not append duplicate or near-duplicate review evidence notes to the same closed review issue when the underlying verification state is unchanged. Tracker review history remains concise while preserving the latest materially new proof-lane evidence for each review container.
description: Fresh-eyes review of commit dab94a86e1a676ad1e57c794214c433d8dbc414f found that the repo-alignment evidence refresh appended duplicate verification text to closed review issue records. In .helix/issues.jsonl, task hx-66a57ea0 now contains the same 'current repo alignment execution reran bash tests/helix-cli.sh and revalidated scripts/helix against FEAT-001, FEAT-002, SD-001, and TD-002' note twice verbatim, and adjacent review records gained additional near-duplicate evidence paragraphs in the same refresh. This makes the tracker's canonical review history noisier without adding new state, which cuts against the reconcile-alignment requirement to keep durable review output and follow-up coverage non-duplicate.
design: Keep alignment rerun evidence durable, but coalesce or suppress repeated review-note appends when the proof lane, scope, and resulting coverage state have not changed materially. Prefer one note per distinct review-state transition, and make repeated report-wording refreshes update the durable report without duplicating tracker evidence paragraphs.

acceptance: Tracker writes detect or prevent lost updates and malformed JSONL corruption under the supported local execution model. Corruption and conflict handling are explicit and test-covered. Deterministic tests exercise concurrent update, interrupted write, malformed file, and recovery/reporting behavior. HELIX docs and skill guidance describe the tracker safety contract accurately.
description: Strengthen the built-in HELIX tracker so concurrent or interrupted updates do not silently corrupt .helix/issues.jsonl, and so failure modes are explicitly detected, surfaced, and tested. Scope includes write-path hardening, conflict detection, corruption handling, and deterministic race/corruption verification.
design: Follow the tracker write-safety ADR and tracker mutation contract first, then implement guarded mutations and deterministic tests before expanding metadata mutation surfaces. Prefer additive safeguards over ad hoc recovery logic.
notes: 2026-03-27 verification: reviewed ADR-002, API-001, TD-002, workflows/TRACKER.md, scripts/tracker.sh, and tests/helix-cli.sh coverage for lock serialization, atomic rewrites, malformed JSONL failure, and queue-drift-safe execution. Canonical proof lane: bash tests/helix-cli.sh exited 0 with PASS: 68 helix wrapper tests. git diff --check exited 0. Remaining tracker metadata work is tracked separately in hx-72f06ccb and is outside this epic's acceptance scope.
title: Harden HELIX tracker against races and JSONL corruption

acceptance: The queue-health section of docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-03-27-repo.md accounts for all tracker statuses or clearly scopes the counts it reports. The report's execution guidance no longer conflicts with its execution-safe ready-queue evidence and does not imply that helix run should execute hx-e7324e4f before hx-c2dd330a.
description: Fresh-eyes review of commit a06fd30 found two correctness issues in docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-03-27-repo.md. First, the queue-health summary records tracker state as '84 total / 8 open / 0 in-progress / 75 closed' without noting the remaining deferred issue, so the published totals are not self-consistent. Second, the report's Execution Order and closing recommendation say the strongest next move is hx-e7324e4f first, even though the same report states hx-c2dd330a is the only execution-safe ready issue. In an automated helix run context that distinction matters: strategic design priority and execution-safe next work are not the same thing. Update the durable report so its queue accounting is mechanically accurate and its next-step guidance distinguishes strategic ordering from the actual execution-safe queue.
design: Keep the alignment report durable and operationally safe. Either make the queue summary exhaustive, or explicitly state which statuses are omitted. Separate strategic follow-up ordering from execution-safe next work so the report does not imply that helix run should select a non-execution design issue ahead of the only execution-safe ready item.
title: Correct repo alignment report queue summary and execution guidance

description: Fresh-eyes review of commit db1932b15c7ad3009e5a20b8cfb7df6121190af4 found that docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-03-28-repo.md now claims the rerun 'did not append more tracker evidence notes to the closed review epic or its area tasks' and later says it completed 'without appending duplicate same-state tracker evidence notes.' But this same commit appends a new paragraph to the closed review epic hx-1297bf62 in .helix/issues.jsonl, so the durable report overstates what changed. Refresh the report so it distinguishes between leaving the area-task notes unchanged and appending one new epic-level summary note, or suppress the epic-note append if the intent is to make the report claim literally true.
design: Keep the durable report strictly evidence-based. Either narrow the prose to say only the closed area-task notes were left unchanged while one epic summary note was appended, or change the tracker mutation so no epic note is appended on same-state reruns. The report and tracker snapshot should make the same claim about what was and was not appended in this refresh.
title: Correct repo alignment report claim about suppressed epic note appends

acceptance: Running 'helix tracker create --help' or 'helix tracker create -h' prints create usage and exits without appending a tracker record. Deterministic tests cover the no-mutation help path as well as the existing missing-title failure path.
description: Fresh-eyes review of commit 97e4817717c7bb64fef2358a0f77ce1e63a5762f found that invoking 'helix tracker create --help' creates a new tracker issue instead of showing command usage. A direct reproduction in the current repository returned issue ID hx-3841301a, and the canonical tracker history already contains multiple closed accidental '--help' artifacts (for example hx-cf1064a9, hx-0a4cfd7e, hx-bc6a850e, hx-f5fe9125, and hx-f6aaf586). Treat help flags as help for the create subcommand before title parsing so the command does not mutate tracker state when an operator requests usage.
design: Update tracker-create argument handling so '--help', '-h', and 'help' invoke usage output without writing to .helix/issues.jsonl. Add deterministic coverage for the help path and preserve the existing 'missing title' failure when no title/help flag is provided.
title: Handle --help correctly for helix tracker create

description: When the run loop declares an issue or epic truly blocked, mark it with a machine-readable blocked flag and a structured blocker description so the operator can quickly find and resolve blockers. Should be queryable via helix tracker (e.g. helix tracker blocked --detail). Blocked means: conflicting requirements that cannot be resolved by project vision/principles, or intractable technical problem after exponential backoff.
title: Tracker: add blocked flag with structured blocker description for human triage

acceptance: Running the quickstart demo setup in a clean workspace no longer fails when writing Claude settings. The script consistently creates any required parent directories before redirecting files. The demo recording remains aligned with the actual script behavior.
description: Fresh-eyes review of last commit 6291a3e found a regression in docs/demos/helix-quickstart/demo.sh after the .agents packaging update. The script now creates .agents and copies skills there, but it still redirects to .claude/settings.json without first creating .claude. On a clean workspace this fails immediately with: zsh: no such file or directory: .claude/settings.json. Evidence: docs/demos/helix-quickstart/demo.sh:144-146 and a clean tmpdir reproduction.
design: Keep the canonical .agents skill install path, but preserve the demo's Claude settings write by creating .claude before redirecting to .claude/settings.json, or by updating the demo flow so the settings file path is created safely.
title: Restore .claude directory creation in quickstart demo
